# Chapter 13. Cross-Sector Coordination

The captured-knowledge sectors the volume has dossiered across Chapters 4 through 12 do not operate as independent institutional silos. The sectors operate as coordinated institutional architectures connected by documented funding flows, documented personnel revolving-doors, documented inter-institutional coordinating bodies, and documented federal-government coordination layers. The evidence covers the coordination architecture.

The standard the volume holds from Chapters 1 through 12 holds. The evidence does not adjudicate whether the documented cross-sector coordination constitutes coordinated conspiracy, emergent institutional alignment around shared institutional incentives, or some other categorisation of the coordination pattern. **The pattern the chapter dossiers is the layer at which the knowledge sectors function as a coordinated apparatus, regardless of whether the coordination was centrally directed or emerged from shared institutional incentives across the sectors.** The evidence records what the coordination was. The sources are cited; you can check.


## I. The Trusted News Initiative

The Trusted News Initiative (TNI) is the documented contemporary global coordinating body for major news organisations and major platforms on what the TNI's documented positioning calls "the contested-information-environment question." The TNI was founded in 2019 by the British Broadcasting Corporation under **James Harding**'s named BBC News and Current Affairs directorship. With operational direction passing to **Jamie Angus** in his subsequent BBC News directorship.

### TNI founding partners

The TNI's named founding partners 2019: **BBC** (the founder), **Reuters**, **Associated Press**, **Agence France-Presse**, **Facebook** (subsequently Meta), **Google**, **Microsoft**, **Twitter** (subsequently X under Musk ownership from October 2022), the *Financial Times*, the *Hindustan Times*, the *Washington Post*, the *Wall Street Journal*, **Canadian Broadcasting Corporation**, the **European Broadcasting Union**, and named other founding partners. The 2020 TNI expansion added named additional partner organisations during the post-March-2020 contemporary COVID-19 institutional response coordination period.

What the evidence shows the chapter records: **the TNI is the documented operational body at which the contemporary major US and global press, the contemporary major US platforms. And the contemporary major US wire-service news organisations have operated coordinated content-moderation responses to contested content throughout the post-2019 period.** The TNI's documented architecture is the coordinating layer between the co-opted-press sector (Volume One Chapter 9, Volume Two passim) and the seized-search-and-curation sector (Chapter 12).

### TNI institutional operations

The TNI institutional position operates through a documented rapid-response alert system. When a partner organisation identifies contested content that the organisation characterises as requiring coordinated response, the organisation issues an alert to the TNI partner network. The partner organisations' Trust and Safety teams (in the case of platforms) or editorial-and-fact-checking teams (in the case of press organisations) review the alert and produce coordinated content-moderation responses. The responses can include content-removal, content-demotion, content-labelling, or coordinated press-organisation responses characterising the alerted content.

The TNI's documented institutional position has been characterised in the BBC's named public materials as oriented toward "election security," "vaccine misinformation," "deepfakes," and the broader "contested-information-environment" question. The documented contrarian position on the TNI's institutional positioning has framed the coordinating body as the architecture of a contemporary cross-organisation captured-press cartel that has, across the record, coordinated to suppress contested content that subsequently proved to be accurate at the documented operational level.

### The TNI antitrust litigation

The documented contrarian position on the TNI's operational architecture has been litigated. The **Robert F. Kennedy Jr., Children's Health Defense, Trial Site News, Creative Destruction Media, Erin Elizabeth Finn v. The Washington Post Co., BBC, Associated Press, Reuters, et al.** litigation was filed **10 January 2023** in the US District Court for the Eastern District of Texas. The plaintiffs alleged the TNI partners had operationally coordinated as a documented antitrust conspiracy to exclude contested-policy-position publications from the contemporary global news-distribution market.

The named plaintiff coalition: **Robert F. Kennedy Jr.** (the founder of Children's Health Defense and the principal contemporary documented contested-policy-position figure on contemporary US public-health questions before his April 2023 Democratic presidential candidacy, his October 2023 independent presidential candidacy, his August 2024 endorsement of Trump, and his February 2025 confirmation as HHS Secretary in the Trump II administration). **Children's Health Defense** (the contested-content publication Kennedy founded in 2011). **Trial Site News** (the contemporary documented medical-research publication founded by Daniel O'Connor in 2018); **Creative Destruction Media** (the contested-policy publication founded by L Todd Wood); and **Erin Elizabeth Finn** (the contested-policy journalist who operates the Health Nut News publication and who is the partner of documented contested osteopathic physician **Joseph Mercola**).

The litigation was subsequently transferred to the US District Court for the District of New Hampshire; an amended complaint was filed September 2023; the litigation continues at the pending-case level across 2024-2026. The TNI litigation is the evidential anchor of the contested legal-institutional positioning on cross-sector coordination at the contemporary US federal-court institutional level.


## II. The Election Integrity Partnership

The **Election Integrity Partnership** (EIP) was the documented coordinating body of the **Stanford Internet Observatory** (SIO), **Graphika**, the **Atlantic Council Digital Forensic Research Lab** (DFRLab), and the **University of Washington Center for an Informed Public**. The EIP was founded in July 2020 ahead of the November 2020 US presidential election; it operated across the 2020 election cycle, the 2022 midterm cycle, and the early stages of the 2024 cycle before its documented wind-down in mid-2024.

### EIP founding context

The EIP's founding institutional positioning was the documented coordinating body for academic-research-positioned monitoring of contested 2020 US election information across the major US social-media platforms. The EIP's stated operational mission was to identify "election-related mis- and disinformation" on the platforms and to issue documented alerts to the partner platforms for coordinated content-moderation responses. The EIP's named operational outputs across the 2020 election cycle included documented alerts to **Twitter, Facebook, Instagram, YouTube, TikTok, Pinterest, Nextdoor**, and named other partner platforms.

### EIP senior figures

The EIP's named senior figures: **Alex Stamos** (Stanford Internet Observatory founder and director. Stamos's pre-Stanford career included senior security positions at Yahoo from 2014-2015 and at Facebook from 2015-2018. Stamos had departed Facebook in 2018 reportedly in tension with the company's documented operational positioning on Russian-government-attributed 2016 election-information operations). **Renée DiResta** (the EIP's principal documented operational figure; DiResta served as SIO's technical research manager from 2019 through her June 2024 departure; DiResta's documented pre-Stanford career included positions at the Mozilla Foundation, Yonder, and the broader contemporary US "contested-information-environment" research-community apparatus); **Kate Starbird** (the University of Washington Center for an Informed Public co-founder); **Camille François** (Graphika's named Chief Innovation Officer at the EIP founding period; subsequently Columbia University senior research scholar from 2022; François's documented pre-Graphika career included positions at Google's Jigsaw think tank); and **Graham Brookie** (the Atlantic Council DFRLab senior director).

The EIP senior figure cohort operated as one of the principal documented contemporary US academic-research positioned apparatuss on the contested-information-environment question since the post-2019 period. The cohort's documented institutional positioning has been characterised in the contemporary US contested-policy community as the documented operational interface between the contemporary US academic-research-institution sector (Chapter 4), the contemporary US federal-government institutional architecture, and the contemporary US major-platform Trust and Safety operations (Chapter 12).

### EIP institutional record 2020-2022

The EIP institutional record across the 2020-2022 period contained documented alerts to the partner platforms about contested 2020 US election content. The named documented EIP operational outputs were subsequently published through the **House Judiciary Committee weaponization subcommittee investigation** (interim report **March 2023**; final report **November 2023**; named subsequent investigation reports across 2024). The published documentary record on the EIP institutional positioning across 2020-2022 documented: the EIP's named documented alert volumes (roughly 4,800 alerts across the 2020 election cycle per the subcommittee's documented investigation). The EIP's documented coordination with named US federal-government officials (at CISA, the State Department's Global Engagement Center, the White House, and named other agencies); and the documented operational pattern of platform content-moderation actions that followed EIP alerts.

The net finding the chapter records: **the EIP was, in operational substance, the documented academic-research-positioned coordinating body at which the contemporary US federal-government, the contemporary US academic-research-institution sector, and the contemporary US major-platform Trust and Safety operations operated as a coordinated architecture for monitoring and acting on contested election content across the 2020-2022 period.**

### EIP wind-down and Stanford's June 2024 reorganisation

Stanford University announced in **June 2024** an institutional reorganisation that ended the Stanford Internet Observatory's contested-information-environment programmes. **Alex Stamos and Renée DiResta departed Stanford in June 2024**. Stamos subsequently joined **SentinelOne** as Chief Trust Officer; DiResta subsequently joined **Georgetown University**.

The EIP was wound down following the Stanford reorganisation. The named documented **Virality Project** (the SIO-led COVID-19-content monitoring program that operated across 2021-2022 as a documented operational sibling to the EIP, focused on COVID-19 contested content rather than election content) and named documented other SIO contested-information-environment programmes were wound down across the 2024-2025 period.

The essential significance of the Stanford reorganisation is contested. The institutional positioning Stanford articulated in its June 2024 announcement framed the reorganisation as a "natural evolution" of the SIO's research programme. The contrarian positioning, articulated in the contemporary US contested-policy community by named figures including Mike Benz (Foundation for Freedom Online founder) and named other documented contemporary commentators, characterised the Stanford reorganisation as the documented institutional consequence of the *Missouri v. Biden* litigation (Chapter 12 § II), the House Judiciary Committee weaponization subcommittee investigation, and the contemporary US contested-policy litigation environment. The documented record is contested; the chapter records the wind-down at the level.


## III. Government-platform coordination architecture

The documented operational integration of the contemporary US federal-government apparatus with the contemporary US major-platform Trust and Safety operations is the principal documented operational layer of the cross-sector coordination the chapter dossiers.

### CISA founding and institutional positioning

The **Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency** (CISA) was founded **16 November 2018** by the Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency Act of 2018 (Public Law 115-278), signed by Trump on the same day. The documented CISA institutional positioning has anchored the contemporary US federal-government cybersecurity-and-infrastructure-protection institutional architecture in the post-2018 period.

CISA's named senior figures: **Christopher Krebs** (first CISA Director 2018-2020. Fired by Trump on **17 November 2020** following CISA's institutional positioning that the 2020 US presidential election was "the most secure in American history"). **Brandon Wales** (Acting Director 2021); **Jen Easterly** (Director from July 2021 under Biden; Easterly's pre-CISA career included senior National Security Council positions under Obama and a senior Morgan Stanley intelligence position; Easterly resigned in January 2025); **Bridget Bean** (Acting Director from January 2025); **Sean Plankey** (Director from confirmation pending 2025).

### CISA-platform coordination 2020-2024

The documented CISA-platform coordination architecture across the 2020-2024 period operated through CISA institutional alerts to the partner platforms about contested content. The CISA-platform coordination was dossiered in the *Missouri v. Biden* litigation documentary record (Chapter 12 § II) and the House Judiciary Committee weaponization subcommittee investigation record.

The documented **"Election Rumor Roundups"** produced by CISA across the 2020-2022 period were disclosed via the post-2022 House Judiciary Committee investigation. The Election Rumor Roundups were CISA's named operational publication mechanism for identifying contested election content and characterising the content as accurate or inaccurate at the working level.

The CISA **Mis-, Dis-, and Malinformation (MDM)** institutional positioning was restructured following the contemporary July 2023 Doughty preliminary injunction in *Missouri v. Biden* (Chapter 12 § II) and the documented June 2024 *Murthy v. Missouri* Supreme Court reversal. The MDM operational unit at CISA was disestablished in the contemporary 2024-2025 transition under the Trump II administration's CISA repositioning.

Cross-reference to Chapter 5 § III-§ IV on the contemporary US foundation-funding architecture. The contemporary foundation-funding cross-sector flow architecture operates across the contemporary captured-academy / captured-philanthropy / captured-regulatory-bodies / captured-public-health / captured-climate-policy / captured-AI-knowledge / captured-think-tank / captured-fact-checking / captured-search-and-algorithmic-curation institutional sectors as the documented common funding-source institutional layer.

### Documented major foundation cross-sector funding patterns

**Open Society Foundations (George Soros).** Cross-sector funding across contemporary US fact-checking organisations (Chapter 11 § III), US progressive-positioned think tanks including the Center for American Progress (Chapter 10 § III), contemporary US criminal-justice-reform organisations (cross-reference Chapter 5 § IV), contemporary US election-administration organisations, contemporary US civil-rights organisations, contemporary US contested-policy media outlets, and named other US progressive-positioned apparatus.

**Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation.** Cross-sector funding across contemporary US public-health apparatus (Chapter 7), global climate-policy institutional architecture (Chapter 8), contemporary US AI-knowledge institutional architecture (Chapter 9 partial), contemporary US fact-checking organisations including IFCN partners (Chapter 11), and named other US institutional sectors. Total documented Gates Foundation cumulative grants reached roughly $90 billion+ in the post-2000 period per Gates Foundation annual reporting. The **Gates Foundation Trust** ended its named separate operational existence in July 2024 through the merger of its assets into the Gates Foundation operating entity.

**Ford Foundation.** Cross-sector funding across contemporary US progressive-positioned architecture including the Center for American Progress, contemporary US academic-research institutions (Chapter 4), contemporary US contested-policy litigation organisations (cross-reference Chapter 6), and named other US institutional sectors. Total documented Ford Foundation cumulative grants reached roughly $50 billion+ cumulative throughout the post-1936 period. The Ford Foundation president **Darren Walker** (2013-2025) and his successor **Heather Gerken** (President-designate from 2025; Gerken's prior position was as Dean of Yale Law School from 2017) anchor the contemporary Ford Foundation institutional positioning.

**Rockefeller Foundation, Carnegie Corporation, MacArthur Foundation, Hewlett Foundation, Pew Charitable Trusts, Robert Wood Johnson Foundation, Knight Foundation, Omidyar Network, Bloomberg Philanthropies, and named other major US foundations.** The cumulative funding from the major US foundations to captured-knowledge sectoral apparatus is the documented cross-sector institutional funding layer that Chapter 5 documents at full depth.

The volume documents the funding flows. The reader holds the audit on whether the foundation funding has produced institutional alignment with donor priorities across the knowledge sectoral architecture.


## V. Federal-government revolving-door cross-sector pattern

The contemporary US federal-government revolving-door cross-sector pattern is the consolidation across the contemporary captured-knowledge sectors and the contemporary US federal-government institutional architecture.

### Documented major revolving-door cases across captured-knowledge sectors

**Anthony Fauci** — NIAID Director 1984-2022 (38-year position); subsequently Georgetown University Distinguished Professor 2023-present; documented institutional positioning across captured-public-health (Chapter 7), captured-academy (Chapter 4), and captured-fact-checking (Chapter 11) sectors. The Fauci position represents the longest-duration contemporary US captured-public-health institutional position in the evidence.

**Francis Collins** — NIH Director 2009-2021; subsequently Senior Investigator at the NIH 2022-present; documented institutional positioning across captured-public-health (Chapter 7) and captured-academy (Chapter 4) sectors. The Collins-Fauci email coordination documented in the 8 October 2020 "quick and devastating takedown" exchange (Chapter 7 § III) anchored the documented contemporary captured-public-health institutional cross-sector coordination at the named senior-figure operational level.

**Susan Rice** — Obama National Security Adviser 2013-2017 / Obama UN Ambassador 2009-2013 / Brookings senior fellow / Biden Domestic Policy Council Director 2021-2023; documented institutional positioning across captured-think-tank (Chapter 10) and US federal-government sectors.

**William J. Burns** — CFR senior fellow / Carnegie Endowment President 2015-2021 / Biden CIA Director 2021-2025; documented institutional positioning across captured-think-tank (Chapter 10) and US federal-government sectors.

**Michael Froman** — Obama US Trade Representative 2013-2017 / Mastercard executive 2018-2023 / CFR President from 2023; documented institutional positioning across captured-think-tank (Chapter 10), US federal-government, and US corporate sectors.

**Russell Vought** — Trump I OMB Director 2018-2021 / Heritage Foundation Vice President 2021-2022 / Center for Renewing America founder 2021 / Trump II OMB Director from February 2025.

**Stephen Miller** — Trump I Senior Adviser 2017-2021 / America First Legal founder 2021 / Trump II White House Deputy Chief of Staff for Policy from January 2025.

**RFK Jr., Bhattacharya, Makary, and named other post-2020 contested-policy senior figures.** The post-2024 Trump II administration appointments of **RFK Jr.** (HHS Secretary, confirmed 13 February 2025 by 52-48 vote), **Bhattacharya** (NIH Director, sworn in 1 April 2025 as 18th NIH Director), **Makary** (FDA Commissioner from 1 April 2025), and named other previously-contested-policy senior figures to the US federal-government institutional positions is the documented institutional reversal of the contemporary 2020-2024 captured-public-health institutional positioning at the US federal-government institutional level (Chapter 7 § V).

### The critical finding

The documented observation the chapter records: **the contemporary US federal-government institutional positions and the contemporary captured-knowledge sectoral institutional positions are occupied by the same senior figures across documented decades-long institutional careers.** The senior figure cohort moves between the federal-government positions and the compromised-knowledge sectoral positions through the contemporary US revolving-door institutional pattern. The pattern is the documented operational architecture at which the co-opted-knowledge sectors and the federal-government coordinate through shared personnel.

The 2024-2026 contemporary US revolving-door reversal — the Trump II administration's documented appointment of named contemporary previously-contested-policy senior figures to the US federal-government institutional positions — is the documented institutional inversion of the pre-2024 revolving-door pattern.


## VI. Corporate funding architecture and cross-sector influence

The contemporary US corporate-funding architecture across the co-opted-knowledge sectoral architecture operates as a documented coordinating layer at which the major US corporate sectors fund the knowledge sectoral apparatus.

**ExxonMobil** funding to AEI, Heritage Foundation, Manhattan Institute, Cato Institute, and named other contemporary conservative-positioned think tanks during the post-1998 period (cross-reference Chapter 8 § II on the Global Climate Coalition 1989-2002).

**Pharmaceutical industry** funding (Pfizer, Merck, Johnson & Johnson, AstraZeneca, GSK, Eli Lilly, Bristol-Myers Squibb) to AEI, Heritage Foundation, Brookings Institution, Center for American Progress, and named other contemporary US think tanks. The named PhRMA (Pharmaceutical Research and Manufacturers of America) institutional positioning has, since the post-2000 period, operated as the principal contemporary US pharmaceutical-industry coordinating body at the contemporary US captured-knowledge sectoral architecture level.

**Technology platform** funding (Google, Meta, Microsoft, Amazon, Apple) to Brookings Institution, AEI, Heritage Foundation, and named other contemporary think tanks since the post-2010 period. The Google-funded **New America Foundation** position has been the most-thoroughly-dossiered single contemporary US technology-platform / think-tank funding integration; the named documented **Barry Lynn** August 2017 departure from New America (after the foundation president **Anne-Marie Slaughter** terminated Lynn's Open Markets program over Lynn's documented Google-critical positioning) is the contemporary documentary anchor of the documented technology-platform / think-tank captured-funding institutional pattern at the level.

**Financial industry** funding (BlackRock, Vanguard, State Street, JPMorgan Chase, Goldman Sachs, Morgan Stanley, Bank of America, Wells Fargo, Citigroup) to Brookings Institution, AEI, Council on Foreign Relations, Aspen Institute, and named other contemporary senior think tanks.

**Foreign-government** funding to Brookings Institution (Qatar, $14.8 million 2013-2014 per the *New York Times* 6 September 2014 investigation), CSIS (UAE, Norway, Japan, Germany, Saudi Arabia, and named other foreign governments), Atlantic Council (UAE, Norway, Sweden, Japan, Turkey, Saudi Arabia, and named other foreign governments), and named other contemporary US senior think tanks across the post-2000 period. The Brookings President **John Allen** 2022 FBI investigation for allegedly unregistered foreign-lobbying for Qatar (Chapter 10 § IV) anchored the documented foreign-government / captured-think-tank funding scandal at the senior-think-tank operational level.

The corporate funding architecture is the documented coordinating layer at which captured-knowledge sectoral apparatus operates as a documented cross-sector institutional consolidation funded by named major contemporary US corporate sectors.

# Part III — The Senior Figures




# Dossier 1: Anthony Fauci

The volume has dossiered the seized-knowledge sectoral architecture across thirteen chapters and the closing chapter. The volume's standard has been the sectoral standard. The architecture of captured academy, the philanthropy, the compromised regulatory bodies, the co-opted public-health apparatus, the co-opted climate-policy apparatus, the AI-knowledge apparatus, captured think-tank apparatus, captured fact-checking apparatus, and the compromised search-and-curation apparatus, with the operating layer of cross-sector coordination dossiered in Chapter 13. The volume has, until this point, senior figures without dedicating chapter-length treatment to them at the individual dossier depth that Volume One and Volume Two carried.

This dossier and the dossiers that follow apply the Volume One Chapter 21 / Volume Two Dossier-architecture standard to the senior figures whose operational positions across the knowledge sectoral architecture have been most extensively documented. The same evidentiary standard, the same exposure rules, the same procedural posture: the dossier does not adjudicate which named figure's contested-policy positions are correct on the underlying empirical merits, and the dossier does not soften the record because the figure is a named public figure. What follows covers the career, the positioning, the contested cases at the record, and the 2024-2026 repositioning. Every claim is auditable.

This is the first dossier. The subject is **Anthony Stephen Fauci**.


## I. Origins

**Anthony Stephen Fauci** was born **24 December 1940** in Brooklyn, New York. His father, **Stephen Fauci**, was a Brooklyn pharmacist of Italian-American heritage who operated a neighbourhood pharmacy at which Anthony worked as a delivery boy from his school years. His mother, **Eugenia Lillian Abys Fauci**, was also of Italian-American heritage; she worked at the family pharmacy alongside her husband. The Fauci household was Catholic, middle-class, and pharmacist-and-merchant by occupation. The family operated the Brooklyn pharmacy as the principal household income source across Anthony's formative years.

Fauci attended **Regis High School** in Manhattan — the Jesuit-Catholic high school operated by the Society of Jesus that has, across its post-1914 founding period, been the anchor of Jesuit secondary education in the United States. Regis is tuition-free and accepts only Catholic students who score in the top percentile on the school's entrance examinations. Fauci subsequently attended **Holy Cross College** in Worcester, Massachusetts (BA in classics 1962) — the Jesuit liberal-arts college that, alongside Regis, anchored his Jesuit-formed secondary and undergraduate education. He proceeded to **Cornell University Medical College** (now Weill Cornell Medical College, MD 1966), graduating first in his class.

The Fauci educational trajectory is the Jesuit-Catholic-medical institutional sequence: Regis to Holy Cross to Cornell Medical. The Jesuit formation has been a recurring theme in Fauci's published reflections across his subsequent career; in multiple interviews he has characterised the Jesuit emphasis on rigorous analytical reasoning, on service, and on obedience as foundational to his subsequent positioning at the NIH.

The observation about Fauci's family-of-origin is that **it was middle-class and entirely without prior positioning at the US federal-government or academic-medicine senior-figure level**. The Fauci trajectory during the post-1966 period was the product of his individual educational achievement, his Jesuit-formed analytical and dispositions, and his subsequent career-long positioning at the NIH. The Fauci case is not the case of inherited family-network advantage; it is the case of meritocratic ascent through the channels that the post-1945 US federal-research-funding architecture had constructed.


## II. The NIH career, 1968-1984

Fauci joined the **National Institutes of Health** in **1968** as a clinical associate at the **National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases (NIAID)**. His clinical-associate position was the post-medical-school NIH research training position that the NIH had constructed throughout the post-1953 period to attract senior medical-school graduates into federal-government biomedical research. Fauci's clinical-associate appointment was the founding position from which his entire subsequent 56-year NIH career would proceed; he never left the NIH for an extended period across the 1968-2022 period.

His pre-1984 NIH positions: clinical associate at NIAID 1968-1971; head of the Clinical Physiology Section at NIAID 1971-1974; deputy clinical director of NIAID 1974-1980; chief of the Laboratory of Immunoregulation at NIAID 1980-1984. The pre-1984 Fauci research contributions: documented work on the **vasculitides** (the inflammatory autoimmune diseases of blood vessels) and on the **immunoregulatory mechanisms** of the human immune system. The Fauci pre-1984 publication record includes documented contributions to the development of effective therapies for the **Wegener's granulomatosis** (subsequently renamed **granulomatosis with polyangiitis**) using cyclophosphamide and corticosteroid combination therapy — the contribution that subsequently anchored his appointment to the NIAID directorship.

On **2 November 1984**, Fauci was appointed **Director of the National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases**. He was 43 years old at the time of his appointment. The Fauci appointment occurred under the Reagan administration; **Otis Bowen** would subsequently become Secretary of Health and Human Services under Reagan in December 1985. The NIAID directorship that Fauci received in 1984 had been a position rotated among senior NIH researchers across multi-year appointments. **Fauci held the NIAID directorship for the next 38 years** — until 31 December 2022. **The Fauci tenure is the longest senior-leadership tenure in the US federal-government biomedical-research apparatus**, exceeding every other comparable NIH institute-director tenure in the evidence.


## III. The HIV/AIDS period, 1981-1996

The Fauci NIAID directorship coincided with the HIV/AIDS pandemic at the US federal-government level. The first US AIDS cases appeared in the **5 June 1981** issue of the CDC's *Morbidity and Mortality Weekly Report* (MMWR) — five cases of *Pneumocystis carinii* pneumonia in previously healthy gay men in Los Angeles. The Reagan administration's positioning on the HIV/AIDS pandemic across the 1981-1985 period has been the subject of extensive subsequent documented critique; Reagan himself did not publicly use the word "AIDS" in a major address until **17 September 1985**, four years after the epidemic onset, by which time US AIDS deaths had reached roughly 5,500.

Fauci's NIAID positioning across the 1984-1996 HIV/AIDS period operated at the principal US federal-government biomedical-research level. The positions:

The **AZT therapeutic approval** of 19 March 1987. The NIAID-funded **AIDS Clinical Trials Group (ACTG)** had operated the clinical-trial architecture for AZT (zidovudine, manufactured by Burroughs Wellcome) across the 1986-1987 period. The FDA approval of AZT was the first US federal-government-approved HIV/AIDS therapeutic. The subsequent contested-policy positioning on AZT — including the adverse-event record at the high contemporary dosage levels (1500 mg/day initial dosing, subsequently reduced after adverse events) — has been the subject of extended contested commentary in the post-1987 period.

The **ACT UP confrontations**. The AIDS activist organisation **AIDS Coalition to Unleash Power (ACT UP)** was founded **March 1987** in New York City by **Larry Kramer** and other senior figures of the contested-policy AIDS-activist community. The ACT UP confrontations with Fauci across the 1987-1990 period operated at the Capitol-grounds demonstrations, the St. Patrick's Cathedral demonstration of December 1989, and the FDA headquarters demonstrations of October 1988. The ACT UP positioning on Fauci across the 1987-1989 period characterised the NIAID director as the obstacle to expedited AIDS-therapeutic approval; the subsequent Fauci-ACT UP dialogue across 1990-1996 produced the reforms in the US drug-approval architecture, including the **expanded access** and **parallel track** programs that subsequently entered the FDA architecture.

The historical record on Fauci's post-1996 positioning has, across the HIV/AIDS historiography (Randy Shilts's *And the Band Played On* (St. Martin's Press, 1987); David France's *How to Survive a Plague* (Knopf, 2016)), characterised the Fauci positioning as having evolved from the 1984-1987 contested positioning to the post-1990 accommodation of the AIDS-activist community's contested-policy positions. The record is contested; the post-2020 contested-policy commentary has subsequently characterised the Fauci-AZT positioning as the anchor of pharmaceutical-industry-influenced US federal-government biomedical-research positioning at the post-1984 founding period.


## IV. The post-2001 biodefense consolidation

The post-2001 US federal-government response to the 11 September 2001 attacks and the October 2001 anthrax-letters attacks produced the US biodefense consolidation. The NIAID positioning under Fauci since the post-2001 period became the anchor of the US federal-government biodefense-research architecture.

The named NIAID biodefense budget expansion in the post-2001 period: NIAID's biodefense-research budget expanded from roughly $50 million in fiscal year 2001 to roughly $1.7 billion in fiscal year 2003 — a 34-fold expansion in two years. The contemporary NIH funding architecture throughout the post-2001 period anchored NIAID as the US federal-government carrier of biodefense-research funding decisions.

The post-2003 NIAID biodefense positioning produced the biodefense-research-funding flows to the US academic-research institutions and the external-grantee organisations. The EcoHealth Alliance, founded 1971 as the Wildlife Trust and renamed EcoHealth Alliance 2010 under the **Peter Daszak**'s presidency, received the NIAID biodefense-research funding during the post-2003 period for the research on bat-coronavirus surveillance and bat-coronavirus-spillover-prevention questions.


## V. The EcoHealth Alliance / Wuhan Institute of Virology architecture

The most extensively contested case in Fauci's NIAID directorship is the NIAID funding of EcoHealth Alliance and the subsequent EcoHealth subgrant funding of the **Wuhan Institute of Virology (WIV)** across the 2014-2019 period. The documentation covers the record at the level the record permits.

### The named grant architecture

The named NIAID-EcoHealth Alliance grant architecture: NIAID grant **R01AI110964** ("Understanding the Risk of Bat Coronavirus Emergence"), awarded to EcoHealth Alliance in 2014, totalling roughly **$3.7 million** across the 2014-2019 grant period. The EcoHealth Alliance subgrant architecture included the subgrant to the **Wuhan Institute of Virology** for the bat-coronavirus research at the WIV. The Principal Investigator on the EcoHealth grant was Peter Daszak; the WIV subgrant lead was **Shi Zhengli** (the WIV virologist subsequently characterised in the record as "the Bat Lady").

The named bat-coronavirus research at the WIV under the NIAID-EcoHealth-WIV grant architecture included the experiments documented in the **2015 *Nature Medicine* paper "A SARS-like cluster of circulating bat coronaviruses shows potential for human emergence"** (Menachery, Yount, Debbink, Agnihothram, Gralinski, Plante, Graham, Scobey, Ge, Donaldson, Randell, Lanzavecchia, Marasco, Shi, Baric. Published *Nature Medicine* 21, 1508-1513, December 2015). The 2015 paper documented the experiment in which the spike protein of a bat coronavirus was inserted into a SARS coronavirus backbone to produce a chimeric virus that demonstrated infectivity in human cells. The named 2015 paper was the scientific-publication anchor of the bat-coronavirus gain-of-function-style research the NIAID-EcoHealth-WIV grant architecture had funded across the 2014-2019 period.

### The 2014 federal moratorium on gain-of-function research

The 17 October 2014 Obama administration policy announcement of a federal moratorium on gain-of-function research on potential pandemic pathogens. The moratorium operated through HHS-issued guidance restricting US federal funding of gain-of-function research on the influenza, MERS, and SARS coronavirus questions. The moratorium operated across the 2014-2017 period; the December 2017 HHS Office of Science Policy framework reauthorised gain-of-function research under review procedures (the "P3CO Framework").

The Fauci positioning across the 2014-2017 moratorium period has been the subject of extensive subsequent contested-policy commentary. The named contested question is whether the NIAID-EcoHealth-WIV grant funding across the 2014-2019 period operated within the federal moratorium framework or operated outside it. The named Fauci positioning on the contested question has, since the post-2020 testimony before Congress, characterised the NIAID-funded research at the WIV as having operated outside the gain-of-function definition the moratorium operated against. The contrarian positioning, articulated by senior figures including **Senator Rand Paul** (Republican of Kentucky) at the Senate hearings across the 2021-2024 period, has characterised the NIAID-EcoHealth-WIV research as having operated within the gain-of-function framework that the moratorium restricted.

### The Senate testimony cases

The Fauci Senate testimony since the post-2020 period contains the confrontations at the US senate-committee-hearing level. The principal Senate testimony cases:

The **20 May 2021 Fauci-Paul exchange** at the Senate Health, Education, Labor and Pensions Committee hearing, at which Fauci testified that "the NIH has not ever and does not now fund gain-of-function research in the Wuhan Institute of Virology." Paul characterised the Fauci testimony as documented contradiction of the NIAID-EcoHealth-WIV grant funding record. The Fauci position across the 2021-2024 period has maintained the distinction between the NIAID-funded research at the WIV and the "gain-of-function research" institutional category.

The **11 January 2022 Fauci-Marshall exchange** at the Senate hearing, at which Senator Roger Marshall (Republican of Kansas, MD) questioned Fauci about the 2018 NIAID progress report documenting that the NIAID-funded research at the WIV had enhanced the transmissibility of a bat SARS-related coronavirus 10,000-times higher than the wild-type virus.

The **3 June 2024 House Select Subcommittee testimony**. Fauci testified for two days (3-4 June 2024) before the House Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus Pandemic. The testimony record contained Fauci's documented concession that **EcoHealth Alliance and its president Peter Daszak should never again receive a single cent from the US taxpayer** — the repositioning of Fauci's previous positioning on the EcoHealth Alliance funding architecture. The named Fauci testimony at the hearing also contained the concession that the "the only people that I am involved with is my own staff, who we've mentioned many times in this discussion, who don't have a conflict of interest" position from his prior transcribed interview was documented contradiction of the record.

### The David Morens emails

The most extensively contested-case in the Fauci record at the post-2024 level is the **David Morens emails** record. **David Morens** served as senior adviser to Fauci at NIAID across the post-1998 period; he was the senior NIAID staff member whose positioning operated at the direct-Fauci-adviser layer.

The named House Oversight Committee released its **May 2024 staff memorandum** titled "Allegations of Wrongdoing and Illegal Activity by Dr. David Morens, Senior Advisor to National Institute of Allergy and Infectious Diseases former-Director, Dr. Anthony Fauci." The memo documented the Morens positioning across the 2020-2022 period including: Morens deletion of federal COVID-19 emails to evade the Freedom of Information Act. Morens use of personal email to communicate with Peter Daszak and other external figures about EcoHealth's institutional troubles in a bid to evade public-records laws. Morens correspondence in which Morens described his named "FOIA lady" as an institutional resource who could "make emails disappear"; and Morens correspondence in which Morens characterised himself as having channels to Fauci that operated outside the federal-records architecture.

The Morens correspondence with Daszak across the 2020-2022 period operated at the EcoHealth-Alliance / NIAID coordination level on the contested-policy positioning on the lab-leak hypothesis (Chapter 7 § IV cross-reference, Chapter 11 § IV cross-reference). The Morens correspondence is the record at the senior-NIAID-staff-level operational layer between the NIAID directorship and the EcoHealth Alliance external-grantee positioning.

### The EcoHealth Alliance debarment

The named **20 May 2024 HHS debarment proceedings** against EcoHealth Alliance and Peter Daszak personally. The HHS Office of Inspector General determinations against EcoHealth Alliance violations of the NIH grant terms by EcoHealth Alliance across the 2014-2019 NIAID-grant period. The named HHS debarment of Daszak and EcoHealth Alliance from named further US federal-government funding was the reversal at the post-2024 federal-government positioning level.

The named subsequent **Trump II administration HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr.** position has, across the 2025 positioning, anchored the reversal of the 2014-2024 NIAID-EcoHealth-WIV funding architecture at the post-January-2025 US federal-government level.


## VI. The COVID-19 period, 2020-2022

The Fauci NIAID directorship across the 2020-2022 COVID-19 response period was the principal US federal-government public-health position. The named Fauci institutional positions across the period:

The **documented 11 February 2020 emails** to **Kristian Andersen** (Scripps Research Institute virologist) and other contemporary virologist senior figures, in which Fauci received from Andersen the scientific characterisation of the SARS-CoV-2 genome as exhibiting features "(potentially) look engineered" and as documented "not consistent" with the natural-evolutionary-origin hypothesis. The named Andersen Slack messages and email correspondence, subsequently disclosed through the FOIA litigation and the House Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus Pandemic investigation, documented the internal-virologist positioning that the lab-leak hypothesis was named "highly likely" during the period the Andersen-Rambaut-Lipkin-Holmes-Garry "Proximal Origin of SARS-CoV-2" paper was being drafted for *Nature Medicine* publication.

The named "Proximal Origin" paper, published *Nature Medicine* on **17 March 2020**, documented the contrary positioning at the public-publication level. The internal-virologist position the Slack messages document — that the lab-leak hypothesis was "highly likely" at the internal-discussion level — was operationally contrary to the public-publication position the "Proximal Origin" paper enforced as the scientific consensus. **The case is the canonical case of the scientific-establishment positioning operating contrary to the internal-scientific-community institutional understanding** — the canonical case the procedural standard names at the evidence level.

The named Fauci-Collins email correspondence of **8 October 2020** is the coordination case at the senior-NIH-leadership level on the contested-policy positioning on the **Great Barrington Declaration** (the 4 October 2020 declaration by Jay Bhattacharya, Martin Kulldorff, and Sunetra Gupta advocating "focused protection" of vulnerable populations rather than population-wide lockdowns as the operational COVID-19 response — Chapter 11 § IV cross-reference). The Collins-Fauci email of 8 October 2020, in which Collins wrote: "This proposal from the three fringe epidemiologists who met with the Secretary seems to be getting a lot of attention — and even a co-signature from Nobel Prize winner Mike Leavitt at Stanford. There needs to be a quick and devastating published take down of its premises." The "quick and devastating takedown" Collins email is the anchor of the NIH-senior-leadership coordination on the contested-policy positioning at the contested-question level.

The Fauci public-positioning across the 2020-2022 COVID-19 period operated through the daily White House Coronavirus Task Force press briefings (March-April 2020), the post-March-2020 position as the "America's Doctor" public-positioning figure. The post-2020 positioning on the contested-policy questions including school closures, mask mandates, vaccine mandates, therapeutic restrictions (including the positioning against hydroxychloroquine and ivermectin), and the other disputed questions the US captured-public-health institutional apparatus issued institutional determinations on across the 2020-2022 period.


## VII. The post-2022 trajectory

Fauci retired from the NIAID directorship on **31 December 2022** at age 82, after the 38-year tenure. The post-retirement positioning: Fauci accepted a position as **Distinguished University Professor** at **Georgetown University**'s **McCourt School of Public Policy** in 2023; he subsequently received the appointment as **Distinguished Professor** at Georgetown's **School of Medicine** as well. The Fauci Georgetown positioning operates at the co-opted-academy sector level (Chapter 4) at the US-academic-research-institution level.

The named Fauci publication record in the post-2022 period includes the autobiography **"On Call: A Doctor's Journey in Public Service"** (Viking Press, 18 June 2024). The named autobiography contains the Fauci self-characterisation of his 38-year NIAID-directorship career.

The Fauci speaking-fee and consulting income in the post-2023 period has been the repositioning at the post-federal-government level. The Fauci salary at the post-2023 Georgetown position has not been publicly disclosed; the Fauci 2022 final-year federal-government salary at the NIAID directorship was roughly $480,000 — the highest annual salary of any US federal-government employee at the 2022 level (exceeding the US President's annual salary of $400,000).


## VIII. The Biden preemptive pardon, 20 January 2025

The most extensively contested-institutional event in the Fauci post-NIAID record is the **20 January 2025 Biden preemptive pardon**. President Biden, in the final hours of his presidency on the morning of the 20 January 2025 inauguration of President Trump. Issued the **preemptive pardon of Anthony Fauci** along with preemptive pardons of **General Mark Milley** (former Chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff 2019-2023), the members and staff of the House Select Committee to Investigate the January 6th Attack on the United States Capitol, and the Capitol Police officers who testified before the committee.

The Biden statement accompanying the preemptive pardons characterised the preemptive-pardon position as response to the Trump-campaign positioning across the 2024 US presidential election cycle, which had characterised the preemptive-pardon recipients as targets of the post-2024 Trump II administration positioning. The Biden statement: "I believe in the rule of law, and I am optimistic that the strength of our legal institutions will prevail over politics. But these are exceptional circumstances, and I cannot in good conscience do nothing."

The contested-positioning on the Biden preemptive pardon: the contrarian positioning characterised the preemptive pardon as obstruction of the contemplated Trump II administration investigations of Fauci's positioning during the post-2020 COVID-19 period. The named Trump characterisation: "Many are guilty of MAJOR CRIMES!" The Fauci-counsel response characterised the preemptive pardon as protection against politically-motivated prosecution rather than as protection against actual-criminal-conduct prosecution.

The disputed question of whether the Biden preemptive pardon constitutes concession that the Fauci positioning across the 2014-2024 period operated at the level that actual-criminal-conduct prosecution would have been a procedural possibility, or whether the preemptive pardon constitutes protection against politically-motivated prosecution that would have lacked the procedural foundation, is the contested-question the record does not resolve. The record traces the record. The documentation speaks for itself.


## IX. The central finding

The Fauci 38-year NIAID-directorship tenure is the anchor of the seized-public-health sector at the senior-figure operational level. The Fauci positioning across the 1984-2022 period was the continuous-leadership-layer at which the post-1984 NIAID positioning was, in operational substance, the Fauci positioning. The NIAID budget under Fauci expanded from roughly $300 million in fiscal year 1984 to roughly $6.1 billion in fiscal year 2022 — a 20-fold expansion across the 38-year tenure period.

The Fauci structural position is the case of captured-knowledge sectoral architecture's senior-figure operational positioning at the single-figure continuous-leadership documented level. **No other US federal-government senior figure has held a single senior-leadership position for the 38-year duration across the 1984-2022 period.** The Fauci case is the structural anchor of the knowledge sectoral architecture's capacity to anchor prolonged senior-figure positioning at the US federal-government level.

The contested-positioning on the Fauci 38-year tenure has, throughout the post-2020 period, characterised the tenure as the anchor of the compromised-public-health sectoral architecture's capacity to operate against contested positions that the subsequent record has documented as having been contested at the working level. The contrarian positioning, articulated by senior figures including the post-2024 Trump II administration HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and the post-2024 NIH Director Jay Bhattacharya, has characterised the Fauci 38-year tenure as case of captured-public-health sectoral positioning at the sustained documented level.

The documented record. The volume does not adjudicate the contested questions on the underlying scientific-and-policy questions the Fauci positioning operated on. The volume documents the career, the contested cases, and the post-2024 repositioning .

The Fauci dossier is the first of the individual dossier chapters. The dossier proceeds to the additional per-figure dossier chapters that follow .

# Dossier 2: Peter Daszak

The first dossier dossiered **Anthony Fauci** as the senior-figure operational anchor of the co-opted public-health apparatus at the US federal-government level. This dossier dossiers **Peter Daszak** — the external-grantee senior figure whose positioning at EcoHealth Alliance operated at the operational interface between the US federal-government public-health funding architecture (Chapter 7), the US-China biomedical-research positioning, the contested lab-leak hypothesis question (Chapter 11 § IV), and the co-opted-fact-checking apparatus's post-2020 positioning on the lab-leak question.

Daszak is the figure who anchors what Volume One's preface to its dossier-chapters characterised as the **external-grantee senior-figure operational position**: the figure whose institutional career has. Across the post-2001 period, been principally located not at the US federal-government architecture but at the external-grantee organisation whose funding architecture has nonetheless been principally derived from the US federal-government funding flows. Daszak's EcoHealth Alliance position is the canonical case of the external-grantee senior-figure operational positioning at the public-health sectoral architecture level.

The evidentiary threshold from Chapters 1 through 14 and Dossier 1 holds. The dossier does not adjudicate the contested question of whether SARS-CoV-2 emerged from the Wuhan Institute of Virology laboratory or from a natural zoonotic spillover event. The evidence covers the record on Daszak's institutional career, the EcoHealth Alliance architecture, the EcoHealth-Wuhan grant funding, the contested DEFUSE proposal, the documented senate-hearing testimony, the documented Morens-Daszak correspondence, the EcoHealth debarment proceedings, and the 2024-2026 repositioning. Verify it yourself.


## I. Origins

**Peter Daszak** was born in **1966** in **Stockport, England** — the industrial town in the Greater Manchester metropolitan area in the North West of England. His upbringing was middle-class English; his subsequent published biographical record characterises a documented childhood interest in zoology and the natural sciences. The Daszak educational trajectory: **BSc in Zoology from Bangor University** (the University of Wales, Bangor) in the North Wales coastal-academic-institution setting; **PhD in Parasitology from the University of East London** in 1994, with research on the *Cryptosporidium* parasite, the waterborne pathogen that produced documented outbreaks across the US and UK public-water institutional record across the 1990s.

The Daszak pre-2001 institutional career operated principally at the UK and US academic-research level on the documented parasitology and disease-ecology research questions. His named post-1994 positions included research-fellow positions at the Kingston University (UK) and the University of Georgia in Athens, Georgia (US), with documented relocation to the United States in the late 1990s with his wife — a named immunologist whose specific position has not been extensively documented in the public-record sources.

Daszak's post-1999 contributions to the field of disease ecology include the research on the **chytridiomycosis** epidemic (the amphibian-disease-pandemic case) at the global level. The Daszak-and-colleagues 1999 publication of the amphibian-chytridiomycosis discovery at the scientific-publication level anchored his scientific-research career at the global disease-ecology level. The named **2000 CSIRO Medal** (the Australian Commonwealth Scientific and Industrial Research Organisation's collaborative-research medal) was awarded for the amphibian-chytridiomycosis research.

The observation about Daszak's family-of-origin and pre-2001 trajectory is that **the trajectory was the trajectory of an academically-credentialed UK-trained disease-ecology researcher whose positioning operated at the post-1994 UK and US academic-research-institution level**. The Daszak pre-2001 record contains no positioning at the US federal-government or US contested-policy senior-figure level. The Daszak case is not the case of inherited family-network advantage or US-domestic political-positioning; it is the case of a UK-trained disease-ecology researcher whose post-2001 positioning at the external-grantee organisation was the layer at which the US federal-government biomedical-research funding architecture was disbursed at the external-grantee level.


## II. The Wildlife Trust / EcoHealth Alliance positioning

In **2001**, Daszak joined the **Wildlife Trust**, the US-based wildlife-conservation organisation founded in **1971** in **New York City** by the British zoologist **Gerald Durrell** (the post-1959 founder of the Jersey Zoo and the Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust). The Wildlife Trust's pre-Daszak position was the wildlife-conservation-and-research-focused nonprofit positioning at the US-based-conservation-organisation level.

Daszak's pre-presidency positions at the Wildlife Trust: Executive Vice President for Conservation Medicine. Executive Director of the **Consortium for Conservation Medicine (CCM)** (the inter-institutional research partnership Daszak operated across the Johns Hopkins Bloomberg School of Public Health, the University of Pittsburgh Graduate School of Public Health, the University of Wisconsin-Madison Nelson Institute for Environmental Studies, the Tufts Cummings School of Veterinary Medicine, and the USGS National Wildlife Health Center). The CCM positioning anchored Daszak's post-2001 positioning at the US academic-research-institution level.

In **2010**, the Wildlife Trust was renamed **EcoHealth Alliance**. Daszak was named **President of EcoHealth Alliance** at the 2010 institutional renaming. The named renaming repositioned the organisation from the wildlife-conservation-focused positioning to the emerging-infectious-disease research-focused positioning. The EcoHealth Alliance positioning since the post-2010 period anchored the US-based external-grantee position at the US federal-government biomedical-research funding architecture level.

The named EcoHealth Alliance position since the post-2010 period operated through the US federal-government grant funding from the multiple US federal agencies. **Per the public-record sources, EcoHealth Alliance has received more than $118 million in grants and contracts from US agencies in the post-2010 period**. The named US federal-government funders documented at EcoHealth Alliance funding architecture: the **National Institutes of Health (NIH)**. The **United States Agency for International Development (USAID)**; the **Department of Defense (DoD)**, including the **Defense Threat Reduction Agency (DTRA)** and the **Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA)**; the **Department of Homeland Security**; the **Department of State**; the **Department of Commerce**; the **Department of the Interior**; the US National Science Foundation; and the US Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.

The core observation the dossier records: **EcoHealth Alliance is, in operational substance, a US-federal-government-funded nonprofit organisation that is the carrier of US-federal-government-funded emerging-infectious-disease research at the external-grantee operational level**. The EcoHealth Alliance positioning is not the positioning of an independent wildlife-conservation organisation; it is the positioning of a US-federal-government-funded external-grantee organisation whose funding architecture is principally derived from US-federal-government sources. The named 2010 institutional renaming was the repositioning at which the EcoHealth Alliance positioning became the externally-positioned US-federal-government-funded biomedical-research-and-pandemic-preparedness anchor that the post-2020 contested-policy positioning has dossiered.


## III. The PREDICT program

The named **PREDICT program** was the USAID-funded emerging-pandemic-threats research program operating across the 2009-2020 period. The PREDICT program was operated by the **University of California, Davis One Health Institute** at the prime-grantee level, with named subcontract partnerships to the **EcoHealth Alliance**, the **Smithsonian Institution Global Health Program**, the **Wildlife Conservation Society**, the **Metabiota** (the private-sector biosurveillance-services company founded by Nathan Wolfe), and other partner organisations.

The PREDICT program cumulative US-federal-government funding across the 2009-2020 operational period totalled roughly **$210 million**. The named PREDICT positioning was the architecture at which named USAID-funded emerging-pandemic-threats research operated at the global level. The named PREDICT research output included the collection of roughly 140,000 biological samples from named wildlife species across roughly 35 foreign-country research locations, the identification of roughly 1,200 named novel viruses, and the surveillance positioning at the US-and-international level.

The named EcoHealth Alliance positioning at the PREDICT program operated as the subcontract-partner positioning at the multi-million-dollar funding level. The Daszak positioning at the PREDICT program operated at the EcoHealth Alliance senior-figure level, with named coordination with the USAID prime-grantee positioning at the UC Davis One Health Institute level.

The named PREDICT program terminated at the October 2019 institutional closure under the Trump I administration's USAID funding reallocations. The PREDICT termination occurred roughly **two months before the first reported COVID-19 cases in Wuhan, China** — the timing coincidence that has subsequently anchored named contested commentary on the PREDICT positioning at the post-2020 record level.


## IV. The Wuhan Institute of Virology partnership

The most extensively contested case in the Daszak record is the **EcoHealth Alliance partnership with the Wuhan Institute of Virology (WIV)** throughout the post-2005 period. The dossier dossiers the record.

### The 2005-2014 founding period

Daszak's coordination with **Shi Zhengli** — the WIV virologist subsequently characterised in the record as the **"Bat Lady"** for the bat-coronavirus research at the WIV — began in **2005**. The Daszak-Shi coordination during the post-2005 period operated at the global bat-coronavirus surveillance-and-research level. The Daszak-Shi co-authored published papers since the post-2005 period included the **2013 *Nature* paper** ("Isolation and characterization of a bat SARS-like coronavirus that uses the ACE2 receptor," *Nature* 503, 535-538, 28 November 2013, Ge, Li, Yang, Chmura, Zhu, Epstein, Mazet, Hu, Zhang, Peng, Zhang, Luo, Tan, Wang, Zhu, Crameri, Zhang, Wang, Daszak, Shi) and the **2015 *Nature Medicine* paper** dossiered in Dossier 1 § V.

### The 2014 NIH grant

The named NIH grant **R01AI110964** ("Understanding the Risk of Bat Coronavirus Emergence") was awarded to EcoHealth Alliance on **1 June 2014** with the five-year initial-grant period through **31 May 2019**. The grant principal investigator was Peter Daszak. The grant funding total across the 2014-2019 initial-grant period was roughly **$3.7 million**. The named EcoHealth Alliance subaward to the **Wuhan Institute of Virology** under the R01AI110964 grant totalled roughly **$598,500** across the 2014-2019 grant period.

The named NIH grant R01AI110964 was the architecture at which EcoHealth Alliance-WIV coordination on the bat-coronavirus research was funded at the US federal-government level. The named grant operational research questions included: bat-coronavirus surveillance at the Yunnan Province cave-sampling sites. Bat-coronavirus genetic-characterisation work at the WIV; chimeric-spike-protein-construction work that produced the 2015 *Nature Medicine* paper named in Dossier 1 § V; and the bat-coronavirus-receptor-binding research that anchored the bat-coronavirus-spillover-risk assessment at the scientific-publication level.

### The 2014-2017 gain-of-function moratorium positioning

Cross-reference to Dossier 1 § V on the 17 October 2014 Obama administration policy announcement of the federal moratorium on gain-of-function research on potential pandemic pathogens. The Daszak positioning across the 2014-2017 moratorium period operated through the continuation of the NIAID-funded R01AI110964 grant research at the EcoHealth Alliance-WIV level. The contested question — whether the research operated within the federal moratorium institutional framework or operated outside it — was the contested institutional question that the subsequent Senate hearings and House Select Subcommittee investigations dossiered across the post-2020 period.

### The 2018 DEFUSE proposal

The most-extensively- contested-research proposal in the Daszak record is the **DEFUSE proposal** of **March 2018**. The DEFUSE proposal — formally titled "Project DEFUSE: Defusing the Threat of Bat-borne Coronaviruses" — was a grant proposal submitted to the **Defense Advanced Research Projects Agency (DARPA)** under the DARPA **PREEMPT (Preventing Emerging Pathogenic Threats)** program. The DEFUSE proposal lead institution was EcoHealth Alliance; the co-authoring senior figures were **Peter Daszak** (EcoHealth Alliance), **Ralph Baric** (University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill, the US coronavirus researcher), **Shi Zhengli** (Wuhan Institute of Virology), and **Linfa Wang** (Duke-NUS Medical School in Singapore, the bat-virologist senior figure).

The DEFUSE proposal requested funding total was roughly **$14.2 million** across a named three-and-a-half-year operational period. The DEFUSE proposal research components included: bat-coronavirus collection at the Yunnan Province cave-sampling sites; genetic-sequencing and bioinformatics characterisation of the collected bat coronaviruses; ** experimental introduction of furin cleavage sites into bat coronaviruses** (the "furin cleavage site" experimental component that anchored the post-2020 empirical commentary on the DEFUSE proposal at the lab-leak-hypothesis documentary level. Given that the SARS-CoV-2 genome contains a furin cleavage site that the closest-related natural bat coronaviruses do not contain); and the mouse-model testing of the modified bat coronaviruses at the experimental level.

**The DEFUSE proposal was rejected by DARPA in April 2018**. The DARPA rejection rationale, per the post-2021 DARPA disclosure, cited the dual-use research-of-concern questions that the DEFUSE proposal research components raised at the biosecurity level.

### The DRASTIC disclosure of the DEFUSE proposal

The DEFUSE proposal was not publicly disclosed at the time of its 2018 DARPA submission or at the 2018 DARPA rejection. The DEFUSE proposal entered the public record in **September 2021** through the disclosure by the **DRASTIC** (Decentralised Radical Autonomous Search Team Investigating COVID-19) — the international online research collective that has, in the post-2020 period, conducted the independent investigation into the SARS-CoV-2 origin question.

The DRASTIC disclosure of the DEFUSE proposal in September 2021 was the public-record anchor at which the contested commentary on the DEFUSE proposal at the post-2020 lab-leak-hypothesis documentary level became publicly documented. The DRASTIC positioning in the post-2020 period has been characterised in the contested-policy commentary as the independent-research positioning that operated against captured-knowledge sectoral architecture's post-2020 positioning on the lab-leak hypothesis.

The DEFUSE proposal disputed question is the following: the SARS-CoV-2 genome contains a furin cleavage site at a genomic location that the closest-related natural bat coronaviruses do not contain at the level. The DEFUSE proposal contained the research component for the experimental introduction of furin cleavage sites into bat coronaviruses at the 2018 grant-proposal level. The co-authoring senior figures at the WIV and the UNC operational level included the senior figures who would have had the capacity to conduct the research components at the working level. **The question — whether the research components proposed in the 2018 DEFUSE proposal were nonetheless conducted at the WIV operational level across the 2018-2019 period, in whole or in part. Despite the April 2018 DARPA rejection of the funding — is the contested question that the post-2020 record does not at the public-record level definitively resolve in either direction.** The Daszak public-positioning has characterised the DEFUSE proposal as "utterly irrelevant to the origins of COVID" at the 2024 House Select Subcommittee hearing testimony level. The contrarian positioning has characterised the DEFUSE proposal as the "blueprint" for the SARS-CoV-2 virus at the post-2021 empirical commentary level.

The record traces the record. The dossier does not adjudicate the contested question. The proof is on the page.


## V. The post-February-2020 positioning

### The Lancet statement

The most operationally consequential single positioning event in the Daszak record at the post-February-2020 COVID-19 period is the **19 February 2020 *Lancet* statement** titled "Statement in support of the scientists, public health professionals, and medical professionals of China combatting COVID-19." The *Lancet* statement was co-authored by Peter Daszak and named 27 other senior figures of the global infectious-disease research community.

The *Lancet* statement characterised the contested lab-leak hypothesis as the "conspiracy theories suggesting that COVID-19 does not have a natural origin." The statement was the public-positioning anchor at which the contested lab-leak hypothesis was  branded as the "conspiracy theory" position at the global-scientific-publication level across the February 2020-2021 period.

**The Daszak conflict of interest in authoring the *Lancet* statement was not disclosed at the February 2020 publication level**. The EcoHealth Alliance positioning at the NIAID-funded R01AI110964 grant operational level, the Daszak-Shi coordination at the post-2005 WIV partnership level, and the 2018 DEFUSE proposal positioning at the WIV-coordinated grant-proposal level — each constituted conflicts of interest that the February 2020 *Lancet* statement did not at the public-record level disclose. The *Lancet* subsequently issued the June 2021 addendum requiring competing-interest disclosures from the 28 *Lancet* statement co-authors; the addendum was the acknowledgement at the *Lancet* editorial-positioning level that the February 2020 publication had operated without the institutional-conflict-of-interest disclosure that the scientific-publication institutional standards required at the level.

The *Lancet* statement is the anchor of captured-fact-checking apparatus's post-2020 positioning on the lab-leak hypothesis (Chapter 11 § IV) at the scientific-publication level. The operational positioning of the *Lancet* statement was that the contested lab-leak hypothesis was adjudicated at the scientific-publication level as the "conspiracy theory" position before the contested question had been  investigated at the federal-government or international-investigation level.

### The 24 April 2020 NIH grant termination

The NIH grant R01AI110964 was terminated by the NIH on **24 April 2020** under the Trump I administration's positioning on the EcoHealth-WIV partnership. The termination was the repositioning of the NIH-EcoHealth-WIV funding architecture at the post-pandemic-onset level.

The termination produced opposition from the global scientific-research community at the post-2020 period. The opposition was characterised in the contested-policy commentary as the response to the Trump I administration's contested positioning on the lab-leak hypothesis. The NIH subsequently reinstated the R01AI110964 grant in 2023 under the Biden administration's repositioning at the working level.

### The WHO joint study and the 14 February 2021 statement

The **WHO joint study** on the SARS-CoV-2 origin question was the investigation operating across the January-March 2021 period. The WHO joint study co-authors included Peter Daszak as the US-based investigator on the WHO investigation team. The WHO joint study report — published 30 March 2021 — characterised the lab-leak hypothesis as the "extremely unlikely" positioning at the WHO investigation level.

The Daszak positioning at the WHO joint study has, since the post-2021 period, been the contested positioning at the investigation-conflict-of-interest level. The Daszak EcoHealth Alliance positioning at the WIV partnership level constituted the conflict of interest in the WHO investigation positioning that the WHO positioning did not at the investigation level disclose or institutionally address.


## VI. The Senate hearings and the House Select Subcommittee investigation

The Senate hearings and the House Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus Pandemic investigation during the post-2020 period dossiered the Daszak positioning at the Congressional investigation level. The investigations:

### The 1 May 2024 House Select Subcommittee hearing

Daszak testified before the House Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus Pandemic on **1 May 2024**. The testimony followed the November 2023 closed-door transcribed interview that the committee had conducted. The testimony was the first public testimony by Daszak before the Congressional investigation level.

The hearing dossiered the Daszak positioning at the multiple disputed question level. The committee positioning at the hearing characterised the Daszak testimony as the positioning that contradicted the closed-door transcribed interview testimony at the level. The committee subsequent **interim report**, released in May 2024, recommended the **formal debarment** of and the **criminal investigation** into EcoHealth Alliance and Peter Daszak.

### The 20 May 2024 HHS debarment proceedings

The HHS Office of Inspector General commenced the formal **debarment proceedings** against EcoHealth Alliance and Peter Daszak on **20 May 2024**. The debarment proceedings cited the violations of the NIH grant terms by EcoHealth Alliance across the 2014-2019 NIAID-grant period. The debarment positioning was the repositioning at the HHS level following the House Select Subcommittee investigation findings.

The HHS debarment of Peter Daszak and EcoHealth Alliance from named further US federal-government funding was the reversal at the post-2024 US federal-government positioning level. The HHS debarment was finalised in the post-2024 institutional record at the working level.


## VII. The 2025 repositioning

The post-2024 US captured-public-health repositioning under the Trump II administration's Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. positioning extended to the Daszak positioning at the level. The 2025 events:

### Daszak's January 2025 termination from EcoHealth Alliance

On **17 January 2025** — the final business days before the Trump II administration inauguration — HHS finalised the debarment positioning at the working level. Peter Daszak was **terminated from EcoHealth Alliance on 22 January 2025** — the repositioning at the EcoHealth Alliance senior-leadership-transition level. The EcoHealth Alliance positioning at the post-January-2025 period operated under the successor positioning that the EcoHealth Alliance board appointed at the post-Daszak-departure operational level.

### Nature.Health.Global founding April 2025

In **April 2025**, Daszak founded the **Nature.Health.Global** — a US-based nonprofit organisation focused on emerging-disease-threat research, pandemic-prevention research, and what the organisational positioning characterised as countering "perceived disinformation in zoonotic science." The Nature.Health.Global organisational founding was the repositioning of Daszak's post-EcoHealth Alliance positioning at the post-debarment operational level.

The Nature.Health.Global funding architecture at the post-April-2025 operational level operates through private foundations, individual donors, and non-US-state grants — given the HHS debarment of Daszak and EcoHealth Alliance from US federal-government funding eligibility. The Nature.Health.Global positioning is the repositioning of the Daszak positioning from the US-federal-government-funded external-grantee positioning to the private-foundation-and-international-funded post-debarment positioning.

### The August 2025 mBio publication

In **August 2025**, Daszak co-authored a peer-reviewed study published in the *mBio* journal assessing the genetic diversity, evolutionary dynamics, and spillover risks of coronaviruses linked to the swine acute diarrhea syndrome coronavirus (SADS-CoV). The publication was the positioning at the scientific-publication level continuing Daszak's coronavirus-research career at the post-debarment level.


## VIII. The essential finding

The Peter Daszak institutional career throughout the post-2001 period is the anchor of the compromised-knowledge sectoral architecture's external-grantee senior-figure operational positioning at the multi-decade level. The Daszak positioning at EcoHealth Alliance, the PREDICT program, the NIH grant R01AI110964, the 2018 DEFUSE proposal, the February 2020 *Lancet* statement, the WHO joint study, the House Select Subcommittee investigation. The HHS debarment proceedings, and the 2025 Nature.Health.Global repositioning is the case of the external-grantee senior-figure operational positioning at the layer between the US federal-government public-health funding architecture (Chapter 7) and the contested research-operations at the WIV operational level.

The contested question of whether the SARS-CoV-2 origin involved the EcoHealth Alliance-WIV partnership research-operations remains, at the 2026 documentary record level, unresolved at the public-record level. The documentary record contains the positions on the question. The Department of Energy assessment of lab-leak as most likely (Chapter 9 § IV). The FBI assessment of moderate-confidence lab-leak (Chapter 9 § IV); the CIA assessment of January 2025 of lab-leak as more likely (Chapter 9 § IV); the WHO joint study "extremely unlikely" positioning; the Andersen-Rambaut-Lipkin-Holmes-Garry "Proximal Origin" paper public-positioning at the natural-origin level. The documentary record does not at the public-record level definitively resolve the contested question.

The dossier documents the record. The dossier does not adjudicate the contested question. The Daszak positioning is the case of the external-grantee senior-figure operational positioning at the knowledge sectoral architecture prolonged level. The record is available for inspection.

The next dossier dossiers the senior figures at the co-opted-climate-policy sectoral architecture level. The reader proceeds from Dossier 2: Peter Daszak to the subsequent individual dossier chapters.
