# Dossier 3: Francis Collins

The first two dossiers dossiered **Anthony Fauci** and **Peter Daszak** as the senior-figure operational anchors of the seized public-health apparatus at the US federal-government and external-grantee institutional layers. This dossier dossiers **Francis Sellers Collins** — the senior figure whose 12-year directorship of the National Institutes of Health from 2009 to 2021 was the layer immediately above Fauci's NIAID directorship at the same NIH architecture. And whose senior-figure positioning since the post-2020 COVID-19 period was the documented coordinating layer with Fauci on captured public-health sector's response to the Great Barrington Declaration and the broader contested record on the COVID-19 positioning.

Collins is the figure who anchors a specific operational position the prior two dossiers did not name at depth: the **scientist-administrator-as-public-faith-figure operational position**. In which the senior figure's positioning at the largest US federal-government biomedical-research apparatus operates in parallel with the same figure's public-positioning as a senior figure of the US science-and-religion reconciliation movement. Collins's religious-positioning since the post-2006 period anchors a layer of the co-opted public-health sector's operational sociology that the volume has not previously dossiered at the senior-figure-and-religion intersection level.

The verification standard from Chapters 1 through 14 and Dossiers 1 and 2 holds. The dossier does not adjudicate the contested questions on the underlying scientific-and-policy questions Collins's positioning operated on. The record covers the career, the research contributions, the senior-figure positioning at the Human Genome Project, the 12-year NIH directorship, the contested cases at the COVID-19 level, the religious-positioning at BioLogos and the Templeton Prize, and the post-2021 repositioning. The reader judges.


## I. Origins

**Francis Sellers Collins** was born **14 April 1950** in **Staunton, Virginia**, in the Shenandoah Valley region of western Virginia. His parents — Fletcher Collins Jr. (a Yale-educated drama professor at Mary Baldwin College who had earlier worked with the Federal Theatre Project under Hallie Flanagan during the New Deal period) and Margaret James Collins (a playwright and educator) — operated a small farm in the Shenandoah Valley region. The Collins household was secular, intellectually engaged, and not religious; Collins has, in his subsequently published autobiographical writings, characterised his childhood household as one in which the parents' position on religious questions was characterised as the position that religion was not a primary household concern at the childhood level.

Collins was **homeschooled by his mother through the sixth grade** before entering the local public-school system. The Collins educational trajectory: **BS in chemistry from the University of Virginia in 1970** at the state-flagship-university level (Collins was a Phi Beta Kappa graduate). **PhD in physical chemistry from Yale University in 1974**, with research on the quantum-mechanical analysis of the DNA-and-RNA structural questions; **MD with honours from the University of North Carolina at Chapel Hill in 1977**. The educational sequence — UVa to Yale to UNC — was the mid-twentieth-century US elite-credentialed academic-medicine sequence at the canonical research-university-trained physician-scientist level.

Collins's religious conversion occurred during his medical-school period at UNC. The published autobiographical record characterises the conversion as having occurred at age 27 (1977) following encounters with medical patients facing terminal illness, who Collins documented as having anchored their dying experience in religious faith of a kind Collins had not at that level encountered. The post-conversion Collins published positioning has characterised his religious turn as having moved from atheism (Collins's pre-1977 published self-characterisation: "I had equated belief in God with intellectual suicide") to evangelical-Protestant Christianity at the post-1977 level. The published religious-conversion narrative anchored his subsequent **2006 publication of *The Language of God: A Scientist Presents Evidence for Belief*** (Free Press, 2006) — the best-selling book that anchored Collins's subsequent US-public-figure-as-religious-scientist positioning.

The observation about Collins's family-of-origin and pre-1984 trajectory: the trajectory was the trajectory of an academically-credentialed US-state-flagship-and-Ivy-League-and-research-university-trained physician-scientist whose religious-positioning was acquired at the post-1977 individual-conversion level rather than at the inherited-family-religion level. The Collins case is the canonical case of the US scientist-administrator whose documented professional-and-religious-positioning operates at the intersection of senior-figure science administration and senior-figure US evangelical-Protestant Christianity at the level.


## II. The University of Michigan period, 1984-1993

Collins joined the faculty of the **University of Michigan at Ann Arbor** in **1984** as an assistant professor in the Department of Internal Medicine and the Department of Human Genetics. His parallel position as a **Howard Hughes Medical Institute (HHMI) investigator** anchored the Michigan-period funding architecture at the HHMI senior-investigator level. The HHMI investigator position is the US biomedical-research-position that operates at the HHMI long-term-investigator-funding level — a level of biomedical-research funding the US individual-investigator architecture characterises as among the most-prestigious and most-stable funding positions available at the US biomedical-research level.

The Collins Michigan-period research contributions across the documented 1984-1993 period anchored his subsequent senior-figure positioning at the NIH Human Genome Research Institute directorship. The research contributions:

The named **1989 identification of the cystic fibrosis gene** (the CFTR gene, identified via the positional-cloning research at the Michigan-Toronto research collaboration with **Lap-Chee Tsui** at the Hospital for Sick Children in Toronto). The CFTR gene identification anchored the breakthrough at the genetic-disease-identification level — the case of the positional-cloning research methodology that subsequently anchored multiple additional documented genetic-disease-identification cases in the post-1989 research-genetics period.

The named **1990 identification of the neurofibromatosis-1 gene** (the NF1 gene, identified via documented positional-cloning research at the Michigan level).

The named **1993 identification of the Huntington's disease gene** (the HTT gene, identified via the international collaborative research with the Huntington's Disease Collaborative Research Group). The Huntington's disease gene identification anchored the fatal-neurodegenerative-disease-gene identification at the research-genetics-publication level.

The cumulative Collins Michigan-period research output anchored the US "gene hunter" position that the documented post-1993 US senior-genetics-research community subsequently positioned Collins as the senior figure at. The Collins research-genetics positioning anchored his subsequent April 1993 NIH appointment at the position layer.


## III. The Human Genome Project, 1993-2008

In **April 1993**, Collins was appointed **Director of the National Center for Human Genome Research** (subsequently renamed the **National Human Genome Research Institute** in 1997). The appointment was the US federal-government senior-research-administration position at the US federal-government biomedical-research-level. Collins succeeded the **James D. Watson** — the 1953 co-discoverer of the chemical structure of DNA, who had served as the founding director of the NIH Human Genome Project from 1989 to 1992 before his named 1992 resignation following the contested-policy questions on the gene-patenting positioning at the NIH-NIH-extramural-research-grants level.

The Collins NHGRI directorship across the 1993-2008 period operated as the US federal-government senior-research-administration position at which the US publicly-funded Human Genome Project was operationally directed at the federal-government level. The named Human Genome Project architecture operated at the multi-institutional international level — the public-funded consortium including the US NIH and US Department of Energy operational positioning, the UK Wellcome Trust Sanger Institute positioning, and the additional international-partner positioning.

The named parallel privately-funded Human Genome Project operated at the **Celera Genomics** position under **J. Craig Venter** (Venter founded Celera in May 1998 with the $300 million private-investor funding architecture). The Collins-Venter contested-policy positioning across the 1998-2001 period anchored the US-and-international Human Genome Project competition between the public-funded and the private-funded sequencing-and-mapping efforts at the scientific-research-level.

The Human Genome Project completion was announced at the **26 June 2000 White House ceremony** at which President Bill Clinton, Prime Minister Tony Blair (via satellite from London), Collins, and Venter jointly announced the completion of the draft sequencing of the documented human genome. The final Human Genome Project completion was announced in **April 2003** at the publication of the completed reference-quality sequencing of the 3 billion base pairs of the human DNA genome.

The Collins post-2003 NHGRI directorship across the 2003-2008 period operated at the post-Human Genome Project US federal-government senior-research-administration position. The post-2003 Collins positioning anchored named subsequent US federal-government biomedical-research initiatives including the **ENCODE Project** (the Encyclopedia of DNA Elements project, the international consortium operating across the 2003-onwards period on the functional-genomic-elements-identification research questions) and the subsequent **HapMap Project** (the international consortium on the human-genome haplotype-mapping research questions).


## IV. The NIH directorship, 2009-2021

On **17 August 2009**, Collins was sworn in as the **16th Director of the National Institutes of Health**. The appointment was the Obama administration's senior US federal-government biomedical-research-administration appointment at the post-2009 positioning level. The Collins NIH directorship operated across **three US presidential administrations** — Obama (2009-2017), Trump I (2017-2021), and Biden (2021) — at the unique-multi-administration-tenure US federal-government senior-leadership position. **Collins is the only Presidentially appointed NIH Director to serve more than one administration in the post-WWII US federal-government institutional history**.

The NIH budget under Collins expanded from roughly $30 billion in fiscal year 2010 to roughly $45 billion in fiscal year 2021 — the 50% expansion across the 12-year tenure period. The Collins NIH directorship anchored named major US federal-government biomedical-research initiatives including: the **BRAIN Initiative** (the Brain Research through Advancing Innovative Neurotechnologies initiative, launched April 2013 under the Obama administration). The **Precision Medicine Initiative** (launched January 2015 under the Obama administration); the **All of Us research program** (the million-person US biomedical-research cohort program); the **Cancer Moonshot Initiative** (launched January 2016 under the Obama administration); and the post-2020 **Operation Warp Speed** US federal-government COVID-19 vaccine-development positioning at the Trump I administration level.

### The Collins-Fauci coordination

The Collins NIH directorship operated at the position immediately above the Fauci NIAID directorship at the NIH institutional-architecture organisational chart. NIAID is one of the 27 NIH institutes and centers; the Fauci NIAID directorship reported to the Collins NIH directorship across the 2009-2021 period operational layer.

The Collins-Fauci coordination across the 2009-2021 period operated at the senior-NIH-leadership coordination layer on multiple contested-policy questions including the EcoHealth-WIV grant funding (Chapter 7 § III, Dossiers 1 and 2). The post-2014 gain-of-function-research moratorium positioning (Dossier 1 § V), the post-2020 COVID-19 response (the section the dossier addresses immediately below), and the additional contested-policy questions across the 2009-2021 positioning period.

### The COVID-19 period positioning

The Collins post-2020 COVID-19 positioning operated at the NIH-directorship-senior-figure level on the multiple contested-and-policy questions. The dossier dossiers the cases:

**The "Proximal Origin" coordination of 1-17 March 2020**. The Collins-Fauci email correspondence of 1 February 2020 — subsequently disclosed via the FOIA litigation and the House Select Subcommittee investigation — anchored the Collins positioning at the "Proximal Origin" paper drafting period. The Collins position on the 17 March 2020 *Nature Medicine* publication of the "Proximal Origin" paper was the institutional-coordinator senior-figure operational positioning at the NIH-NIAID-Andersen-Rambaut-Lipkin-Holmes-Garry coordination level on the contested lab-leak-hypothesis question.

The Collins positioning anchored the NIH positioning at the natural-origin-hypothesis-as-scientific-consensus public-positioning level throughout the post-March-2020 period. The Andersen Slack messages disclosed via the post-2020 FOIA litigation revealed the internal-virologist position that the lab-leak hypothesis was "highly likely" at the internal-discussion level during the period the "Proximal Origin" paper was being drafted for the *Nature Medicine* publication — Dossier 1 § VI cross-reference.

### The Great Barrington Declaration "quick and devastating takedown" email of 8 October 2020

The most extensively- contested-institutional event in the Collins COVID-19 record is the **Great Barrington Declaration "quick and devastating takedown" email of 8 October 2020**. The email — disclosed via the Freedom of Information Act litigation conducted by the **American Institute for Economic Research (AIER)** at the post-2021 disclosure level — anchored the Collins NIH-directorship positioning on the Great Barrington Declaration contested-policy positioning.

The Great Barrington Declaration was published on **4 October 2020** by the three senior figures: **Martin Kulldorff** (the Harvard University epidemiologist); **Sunetra Gupta** (the Oxford University epidemiologist); and **Jay Bhattacharya** (the Stanford University professor of medicine and public-policy figure subsequently appointed as NIH Director under the Trump II administration from March 2025). The Declaration's positioning was the **"focused protection"** approach — the contested-policy alternative to the population-wide-lockdowns positioning the US public-health-architecture had  adopted during the post-March-2020 COVID-19 positioning period.

The Collins email of 8 October 2020 — sent to Anthony Fauci (NIAID Director), H. Clifford Lane (NIAID Deputy Director for Clinical Research and Special Projects), and Lawrence A. Tabak (then NIH Deputy Director, subsequently Acting NIH Director from December 2021 following Collins's retirement) — contained the text:

> *"Hi Tony and Cliff, See https://gbdeclaration.org/. This proposal from the three fringe epidemiologists who met with the Secretary seems to be getting a lot of attention — and even a co-signature from Nobel Prize winner Mike Leavitt at Stanford. There needs to be a quick and devastating published take down of its premises. I don't see anything like that on line yet — is it underway? Francis"*

The email is the anchor of the contested-positioning of the compromised-public-health sectoral architecture at the senior-NIH-leadership level on the Great Barrington Declaration contested-policy position. The Fauci subsequent response to the Collins direction was the promotion of named press articles characterising the Great Barrington Declaration in the contrary direction (the Wired UK article and the *Nation* article that the Fauci promoted in the subsequent days) and the 15 October 2020 ABC News appearance at which Fauci publicly characterised the Declaration as the "things that are like apple pie and motherhood" public-positioning.

The post-2021 contested-policy commentary on the Collins-Fauci "quick and devastating takedown" email has characterised the email as the case of the co-opted-public-health sectoral architecture's post-2020 positioning at the senior-NIH-leadership level. The contrarian positioning, articulated by senior figures including Kulldorff, Gupta, and Bhattacharya at the post-2021 commentary level, has characterised the email as the anchor of the institutional-suppression positioning at the contested-question-adjudication level.

### The Collins post-October-2020 COVID-19 positioning

The Collins post-October-2020 COVID-19 positioning across the November 2020-December 2021 period operated at the senior-NIH-leadership positioning level on the multiple contested-policy questions including: the school closures, the mask mandates, the vaccine mandates (including the positioning that the COVID-19 vaccines were "safe and effective" at the public-positioning level). The Operation Warp Speed Trump I administration COVID-19 vaccine-development positioning; the post-2020 vaccine-and-transmission contested-question public-positioning (Chapter 11 § IV cross-reference); and the additional contested-policy questions across the 2020-2021 period.


## V. The religious-positioning at BioLogos and the Templeton Prize

The Collins religious-positioning operates at the intersection of the senior-figure US federal-government biomedical-research administration position and the senior-figure US evangelical-Protestant Christianity public-positioning level. The dossier dossiers the architecture.

### *The Language of God* publication, 2006

Collins's 2006 publication of ***The Language of God: A Scientist Presents Evidence for Belief*** (Free Press, 2006) was the best-selling US religious-publication that anchored the Collins post-2006 US public-figure-as-religious-scientist positioning. The book argued the compatibility of evolutionary-biological-science and Christian-religious-faith at the theistic-evolution positioning level — the position that God-as-creator and evolutionary-biological-mechanisms-as-creation-process are  compatible at the theistic-evolution-as-scientific-and-religious-position level.

The Collins post-2006 positioning at the intersection of US senior-figure science-administration and US senior-figure evangelical-Christianity-public-positioning anchored the subsequent architecture: the **2007 founding of the BioLogos Foundation** (the US-based 501(c)(3) nonprofit organisation Collins founded with his wife **Diane Baker**. The genetic counselor and founding faculty member of the University of Michigan genetic-counselling program). The **October 2009 appointment by Pope Benedict XVI to the Pontifical Academy of Sciences** (the Catholic-Church-institutional Vatican-affiliated scientific-research-positioning that Collins received at the post-2009 Vatican-positioning level); and the **2020 Templeton Prize** (the £1.1 million ($1.3 million) annual prize from the **John Templeton Foundation**. The Christian-positioned philanthropic-foundation that operates as the largest US-and-international funder of science-and-religion-reconciliation research and publishing).

### The BioLogos Foundation architecture

The BioLogos Foundation operates at the position the Wikipedia summary characterises as the "Christian advocacy group that supports the view that God created the world using evolution of different species as the mechanism" at the theistic-evolution level. The BioLogos architecture across the post-2007 period operated under multiple succession-of-president positioning: Collins (founding president 2007-2009, required-to-resign-following-NIH-appointment positioning); Darrel R. Falk (President 2009-2013); Deborah Haarsma (President 2013-2024); Kristine Torjesen (President from 2024 onwards). Collins returned to the BioLogos Foundation as a **Senior Fellow following his 2021 NIH retirement**.

The BioLogos positioning at the US Christian-and-science-reconciliation level has anchored the Collins post-2007 US public-figure-as-religious-scientist positioning at the institutional-architecture-and-funding level. The BioLogos architecture operates at the US religious-philanthropic-funding level under the Templeton Foundation major-funder positioning at the post-2007 level.

### The 2020 Templeton Prize

The **John Templeton Foundation**'s 2020 Templeton Prize awarded to Collins on **24 September 2020** was the positioning at which the Collins religious-positioning at the BioLogos level was recognised at the international religious-philanthropic-prize level. The Templeton Prize is the annual prize the Templeton Foundation has awarded since 1973 — the prize originally named the "Templeton Prize for Progress in Religion." The previous Templeton Prize recipients include: Mother Teresa (1973); Aleksandr Solzhenitsyn (1983); Billy Graham (1982); Desmond Tutu (2013); and other senior figures of the US-and-international religious-and-spiritual-thought record.

The Collins Templeton Prize receipt at the September 2020 ceremony — held virtually from Washington, D.C., due to the COVID-19 pandemic — operated at the positioning level immediately preceding the Collins-Fauci "quick and devastating takedown" email of 8 October 2020. The two-week temporal proximity of the Templeton Prize ceremony and the Great Barrington Declaration "takedown" email is the observation at the post-2021 contested-policy commentary level — the temporal coincidence of the institutional-religious-prize and the institutional-suppression-of-contested-policy-position operating at the same senior-figure position layer across the same two-week period.


## VI. The post-2021 trajectory

Collins announced his retirement as NIH Director on **5 October 2021**. He stepped down from the NIH directorship on **19 December 2021** — the effective retirement date that produced the succession to **Lawrence A. Tabak** as Acting NIH Director from December 2021 until the November 2023 confirmation of **Monica Bertagnolli** as the permanent NIH Director under the Biden administration.

### The February-October 2022 acting White House Science Advisor position

Collins was appointed as **Acting Science Advisor to the President** by President Biden on **18 February 2022** following the resignation of **Eric Lander** (the MIT professor and Broad Institute founder who served as Science Advisor and OSTP Director from January 2021 until his February 2022 resignation following the White House internal-investigation findings on workplace conduct). Collins served as Acting Science Advisor across the February-October 2022 period before the confirmation of **Arati Prabhakar** (the National Institute of Standards and Technology former-director and former DARPA director) as the permanent Science Advisor on 3 October 2022.

### The post-2022 positioning

The Collins post-2022 positioning operated at the BioLogos Senior Fellow position, the continued NIH affiliate-investigator position at the NIH Human Genome Research Institute, the post-2022 speaking-and-public-positioning activities, and the religious-and-scientific-publishing activities at the post-NIH-retirement level.

Collins's **March 2025 formal retirement announcement** from the NIH at the end of his 32-year NIH-employment institutional tenure (April 1993-March 2025) was the positioning event at which Collins's sustained US federal-government biomedical-research administration career was  concluded at the post-Trump-II-administration positioning level. The March 2025 retirement followed the Trump II administration's January 2025 repositioning of the NIH senior-leadership architecture under the Trump II HHS Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. and the Trump II NIH Director Jay Bhattacharya — the senior figure who had been the "fringe epidemiologists" recipient of the Collins-Fauci "quick and devastating takedown" email of 8 October 2020.

The Francis Collins career since the post-1984 period. University of Michigan HHMI investigator 1984-1993, NIH Human Genome Research Institute Director 1993-2008, NIH Director 2009-2021, Acting White House Science Advisor 2022, BioLogos Senior Fellow from 2021 — anchors the case of the co-opted-public-health sectoral architecture's senior-figure operational positioning at the decades-long US federal-government biomedical-research administration level.

The Collins-Fauci coordination operated at the senior-NIH-leadership position layer across the 2009-2021 period at the Fauci-reporting-to-Collins NIH-NIAID institutional-architecture organisational structure.

The Collins religious-positioning at the BioLogos Foundation, the Templeton Prize, and the Pontifical Academy of Sciences appointments anchors the additional layer the Fauci and Daszak careers did not operate at. The intersection of senior-figure US federal-government biomedical-research administration and senior-figure US evangelical-Protestant-Christianity public-positioning at the Volume Three captured-religion sectoral architecture institutional-intersection level.

**The Collins case is the case of the public-health and captured-religion sectoral architectures' operational intersection at the senior-figure level**. The Collins positioning operates at the intersection layer Volume Three did not at the senior-figure operational level dossier — the intersection of the senior-figure US federal-government biomedical-research-administration apparatus and the senior-figure US evangelical-Protestant-Christianity public-positioning apparatus, operating at the same senior-figure position layer through the US Templeton Foundation funding architecture at the US religious-philanthropic-funding level.

The Collins contested record on the post-2020 COVID-19 positioning at the Great Barrington Declaration "quick and devastating takedown" email of 8 October 2020, the "Proximal Origin" paper institutional-coordination positioning. # Dossier 4: Bill Gates (revisited)

The first three dossiers dossiered **Anthony Fauci**, **Peter Daszak**, and **Francis Collins** as the senior-figure operational anchors of captured public-health apparatus at the US federal-government, external-grantee, and senior-NIH-leadership institutional layers. This dossier dossiers **William Henry Gates III** — the figure whose positioning at the **Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation** is the senior-figure operational anchor of the compromised public-health apparatus at the **private-philanthropic-funding** layer. Gates is the figure who anchors the position the prior three dossiers did not name at depth: the **non-state senior-figure operational position** at which a single accumulated industrial-capital fortune has, in the post-2000 period, was the largest private funder of the global public-health architecture at scales the prior philanthropic-funding record had never previously sustained.

Gates was dossiered in Volume One Chapter 19 from the financial-mask angle — the angle of the Microsoft-fortune accumulation, the post-2000 positioning at the philanthropic-fortune-redistribution operational layer, the tech-industry coordination with Microsoft and the named other senior tech-industry figures in the post-1975 period, and the broader financial-architecture positioning. This dossier dossiers Gates from the public-health and captured-philanthropy angle — the angle Chapter 5 § III named at the sectoral level but did not at the per-figure dossier depth treat. The two dossiers operate as the parallel-volume cross-reference architecture the series's open-edit posture permits.

The standard of proof from Chapters 1 through 14 and Dossiers 1, 2, and 3 holds. The dossier does not adjudicate the contested questions on the underlying scientific-and-policy questions Gates's positioning has operated on. What follows covers the career, the foundation funding architecture, the Gavi and CEPI founding events, the WHO funding positioning, the COVID-19 period public-positioning, the Jeffrey Epstein relationship at the post-2011 period level, the 2021 divorce, and the 2024-2026 repositioning . Check the documentation.


## I. The Microsoft-fortune-to-foundation pivot

**William Henry "Bill" Gates III** was born **28 October 1955** in Seattle, Washington. His family-of-origin and Microsoft-fortune accumulation are dossiered in Volume One Chapter 19 § II. The condensed reference: Gates's father, **William H. Gates Sr.**, was a senior Seattle attorney at Preston Gates & Ellis; his mother, **Mary Maxwell Gates**, served on the board of First Interstate Bancorp and the United Way of America at the regional and national positioning levels respectively. The Gates household at the Seattle-establishment professional-and-civic level is dossiered in Volume One; the volume returns to it briefly because the family-of-origin positioning matters here at the foundation-founding layer.

Gates co-founded **Microsoft Corporation** on **4 April 1975** in Albuquerque, New Mexico, with **Paul Allen**. Microsoft's incorporation, the IBM-PC-and-DOS licensing positioning of 1980-1981, the Windows operating-system positioning since the post-1985 period, and the 1986 Microsoft initial public offering are dossiered in Volume One. The institutional fact relevant here: by 1996, he was the wealthiest person in the world by net worth at the Microsoft-stock-valuation level — a position he held through most of the 1996-2017 period, when **Jeff Bezos** subsequently surpassed him at the Amazon-stock-valuation positioning level (Volume One Chapter 12 § III dossiers the Bezos record).

The **William H. Gates Foundation** was founded in **1994** with the initial endowment from him personally. The **Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation** was founded in its present institutional configuration in **2000** through the merger of the William H. Gates Foundation, the Gates Library Foundation (founded 1997), and the Gates Learning Foundation. The 2000 foundation architecture is the architecture the evidence covers; the foundation as constituted in 2000 was capitalised at roughly $17 billion in initial assets and has expanded during the post-2000 period to roughly $78 billion in assets at the 2024 position level.

The structural fact the dossier records at the founding period: **the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation is the largest private philanthropic foundation in the world at the contemporary scale**. The foundation operates at a scale that exceeds every other comparable US-and-international private foundation at the level. A scale at which the foundation's programmatic positioning across the global-health, global-development, US-K-12-education, and US-agricultural-research sectoral architecture has, throughout the post-2000 period, anchored the private-philanthropic-funding position at the global level.

In **2008**, Gates stepped down from his full-time Microsoft role to focus on the foundation. He served as Microsoft chairman until 2014 and as Microsoft board director until **March 2020**, when he resigned from the Microsoft board following the Microsoft internal investigation of Gates's extramarital relationship with a Microsoft employee (the Microsoft board process subsequently disclosed by the *Wall Street Journal* in May 2021). The Microsoft board-resignation positioning of March 2020 anchored Gates's post-2020 position at the foundation-and-public-positioning operational level rather than at the Microsoft corporate-governance level.


## II. The Gavi founding, 2000

The most extensively-founding event of the Gates Foundation's positioning at the co-opted public-health sectoral architecture is the **Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance** founding of **January 2000** at the World Economic Forum in **Davos, Switzerland**. The Foundation provided the founding $750 million five-year pledge that anchored Gavi's establishment at the private-philanthropic-funding founding-funder operational positioning level.

Gavi's founding architecture: an alliance between the **World Health Organization (WHO)**, the **United Nations Children's Fund (UNICEF)**, the **World Bank**, the **Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation**, donor-country governments, implementing-country governments, vaccine manufacturers, civil-society organisations, and the named technical-and-research-institute partners. Gavi's position has, since the post-2000 period, was the largest single international vaccine-procurement-and-delivery architecture at the global-health-funding scale.

The Foundation funding trajectory to Gavi across the post-2000 period:

The **2000 founding $750 million five-year pledge** anchored the founding period. The **June 2011 $1 billion+ pledge** at the London pledging conference covered the 2011-2015 period; Gates's public-positioning at the conference characterised the pledge as positioned alongside the documented £1.3 billion UK government pledge from Prime Minister David Cameron. The **2014 $241 million additional pledge** to Gavi's polio-eradication and inactivated-polio-vaccine programme. The **2015 Berlin Pledging Conference $1.55 billion pledge** for Gavi's 2016-2025 strategic period. The **2020 Global Vaccine Summit $1.6 billion pledge** for Gavi's 2021-2025 strategic period. The **2020 additional $150 million pledge** to the Gavi COVAX AMC for equitable-access-to-COVID-19-vaccines positioning. The **24 June 2025 $1.6 billion pledge** to Gavi for the 2026-2030 strategic period.

The cumulative Gates Foundation contribution to Gavi across the 2000-2025 period totals roughly **$7.7 billion** — the largest private-donor cumulative contribution at the Gavi architecture. The Foundation has, across the same post-2000 period, committed **more than $30.6 billion to vaccine-related programming** at the cumulative contribution level — the cumulative Gates Foundation positioning at the seized-vaccines sector level.

Gavi's positioning at the post-2000 global-health architecture has been the layer at which the global-vaccine-procurement-and-delivery architecture has operated at scale. Per Gavi reporting at the 2024 level, Gavi has helped vaccinate over 1.1 billion children across 78 lower-income countries and has prevented an estimated 19 million deaths from vaccine-preventable diseases across the 2000-2024 operational period. The contested-policy commentary on the Gavi positioning at the global-health architecture has, in the post-2020 period, been the contested positioning at the private-philanthropic-funder-of-global-health-architecture level. The question of whether the private-philanthropic funder's positioning at the prolonged scale produces the accountability the public-health architecture requires at the democratic-governance level.


## III. The CEPI founding, 2017

The second major founding event of the Gates Foundation's captured public-health sectoral architecture positioning is the **Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI)** founding of **January 2017** at the World Economic Forum in **Davos, Switzerland**.

CEPI was co-founded by the **Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation**, the **Wellcome Trust** (the UK-based pharmaceutical-fortune philanthropic-foundation founded 1936 from Henry Wellcome's pharmaceutical-industry fortune), the **Government of Norway**, the **Government of India**, and the **World Economic Forum**. The founding $460 million initial investment anchored CEPI's establishment at the epidemic-preparedness vaccine-development positioning level. The Foundation contribution to CEPI's founding totalled roughly $100 million across the 2017 founding-period positioning level.

CEPI's positioning was the post-2014-Ebola response to the contested-policy question of how to accelerate vaccine development for emerging-infectious-disease pathogens at the global-health-emergency level. The CEPI architecture operated at the public-private-partnership funding model that operates between the founding-member national governments, the founding-member private-philanthropic foundations (Gates and Wellcome), the pharmaceutical-industry partners (including GlaxoSmithKline, Pfizer, Johnson & Johnson, and other major pharmaceutical companies), and the global-health-architecture multilateral organisations (including WHO, GAVI, and the other multilateral partners).

The CEPI post-2020 positioning at the COVID-19 pandemic response was the vaccine-development architecture at which the mRNA-vaccine positioning (Moderna, Pfizer-BioNTech) and the vector-vaccine positioning (Oxford-AstraZeneca, Johnson & Johnson) were funded at the public-private-partnership operational layer. The January 2022 Gates Foundation $150 million additional pledge to CEPI — alongside a Wellcome Trust $150 million additional pledge totalling $300 million combined Gates-Wellcome contribution — anchored CEPI's five-year-plan positioning across the 2022-2026 institutional period.

The contested-policy commentary on the CEPI positioning at the post-2020 COVID-19 vaccine-development architecture has characterised CEPI as the architecture at which the contested mRNA-vaccine-and-vector-vaccine technology platforms were funded at the public-private-partnership level without the contested review the contested-policy positioning has. Across the post-2021 period, characterised as institutionally necessary at the vaccine-safety-and-efficacy level (Chapter 11 § IV cross-reference on the vaccine-and-transmission disputed question).


## IV. The WHO funding positioning

The Foundation's positioning at the **World Health Organization (WHO)** funding architecture operates at the multi-billion-dollar funding-flow level. The dossier dossiers the record.

The Foundation funding to WHO across the 2000-2024 period totals roughly **$5.4 billion** at the cumulative-contribution level. The 2020-2021 Gates Foundation positioning anchored the Foundation's position as the **second-largest contributor to WHO** at the global-funder level. Exceeded only by the German government's contribution at the 2020-2021 WHO funding architecture level following the Trump I administration's April 2020 announcement of US withdrawal from WHO funding (subsequently reversed by the Biden administration in January 2021 and re-reversed by the Trump II administration in January 2025).

The Foundation funding architecture at the WHO positioning operates at the specified-funding level — the funding architecture in which the Foundation's funding is specified for the specific programmatic positions the Gates Foundation has prioritised. Per the 2024 *BMJ Global Health* analysis ("Who's leading WHO? A quantitative analysis of the Bill and Melinda Gates Foundation's grants to WHO, 2000-2024"), the BMGF funding to WHO across the 2000-2024 period was disproportionately allocated to vaccine-related programmatic positions. Roughly $2.9 billion (53.3% of all BMGF global health grants to WHO) was allocated to vaccine-related programme-and-project support across the 2000-2024 institutional period.

The substantive finding the dossier records: **the Foundation's positioning at the WHO funding architecture has, throughout the post-2000 period, operated at the scale that has produced contested-policy commentary on the question of whether the WHO positioning at the global-health architecture has been captured by the Gates Foundation's specified-funding positioning at the sustained level**. The contested-policy commentary on this question — articulated by named senior figures including former senior WHO figures, named global-health-policy scholars at the academic-research level. And named contested-policy senior figures at the post-2020 period — characterises the Foundation's positioning at the WHO funding architecture as the case of the private-philanthropic-funder-institutional-capture-of-multilateral-global-health-institution operational positioning at the decades-long scale.

The evidence presents the record. The volume does not at the level adjudicate the contested-policy question of whether the Foundation's funding architecture at the WHO positioning constitutes capture at the contested level. The volume documents the record. The reader's own judgment applies.


## V. The COVID-19 period positioning

The Foundation's positioning at the post-March-2020 COVID-19 response operated at the multi-billion-dollar funding-and-public-positioning level. The dossier dossiers the record.

### The Event 201 pandemic-preparedness exercise of October 2019

The **Event 201** pandemic-preparedness tabletop exercise was held on **18 October 2019** at the **Pierre Hotel in New York City**. Event 201 was co-hosted by the **Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security**, the **World Economic Forum**, and the **Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation**. The Event 201 positioning was the tabletop-exercise simulation of a hypothetical coronavirus pandemic — the hypothetical CAPS (Coronavirus Associated Pulmonary Syndrome) pandemic scenario the Event 201 exercise dossiered at the global-pandemic-response level.

The Event 201 positioning of October 2019 — roughly two months before the December 2019 first reported COVID-19 cases in Wuhan, China (Chapter 7 § IV cross-reference, Dossier 2 § IV cross-reference) — has, during the post-2020 contested-policy commentary period, been the observation at the temporal-coincidence level. The Event 201 architecture was not, per the Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security positioning, a predictive exercise on the specific COVID-19 pandemic that subsequently emerged; the exercise was the general-pandemic-preparedness positioning at the global-pandemic-response level. The contested-policy commentary at the post-2020 period level has characterised the Event 201 positioning as the case of captured-public-health-sectoral-architecture institutional preparation for the subsequent COVID-19 pandemic response at the global level.

### The COVID-19 vaccine-development positioning

The Foundation's post-March-2020 COVID-19 positioning operated at the vaccine-development funding-and-coordination architecture level. The Foundation positioning at the post-March-2020 COVID-19 vaccine-development architecture: a $1.75 billion commitment to the COVID-19 response across the 2020-2021 period including funding to Gavi, CEPI, the therapeutics-development positioning, and the additional positioning at the public-health-sector level.

The his public-positioning since the post-March-2020 COVID-19 period operated at the high-profile-public-figure level. Documented his published positions across the 2020-2022 period included the March 2015 TED talk ("The next outbreak? We're not ready") at the pre-pandemic-preparedness positioning level — the 36-million-view public-positioning Gates that subsequently anchored the post-2020 public-positioning of Gates as the "pandemic-preparedness senior figure" at the US-and-international public-positioning level.

The his published positions on the COVID-19 vaccine-and-transmission contested question across the 2020-2022 period operated at the public-positioning level at which the compromised-public-health-sectoral-architecture positioning was amplified at the scale. The his published positions on the mRNA-vaccine positioning and the vaccine-mandate positioning have, since the post-2021 period, been the contested-policy commentary at the public-figure-level positioning at the contested-question-adjudication level.


## VI. The Jeffrey Epstein relationship

The most extensively- contested-personal-case in the Gates record at the post-2011 level is the **Jeffrey Epstein relationship** across the 2011-2014 period (with subsequent Epstein-Gates correspondence across the 2014-2019 period).

**Jeffrey Edward Epstein** was the US financier who pleaded guilty on **30 June 2008** in Florida state court to two state-level prostitution charges including the solicitation of a minor for prostitution — the 13-month Florida sentence with work-release positioning that the subsequent contested-policy commentary has characterised as the lenient-prosecution case at the federal-prosecutorial-discretion level (the subsequent **Alexander Acosta** positioning at the 2007-2008 Southern District of Florida US Attorney level; Acosta subsequently served as the US Secretary of Labor under Trump I from April 2017 to July 2019, when he resigned following the July 2019 second federal indictment of Epstein on the federal sex-trafficking-of-minors charges in the Southern District of New York).

Epstein died in his Manhattan jail cell on **10 August 2019** while awaiting trial on the federal sex-trafficking-of-minors charges. The New York City Office of the Chief Medical Examiner determination of suicide is the official institutional determination at the determination-of-death level; the subsequent contested-policy commentary at the post-2019 period has characterised the Epstein death-in-custody institutional determination as the contested question at the record level.

### The Gates-Epstein meetings, 2011-2014

The Gates-Epstein relationship has been the subject of investigation by *The New York Times* (October 2019 investigation by James B. Stewart, Matthew Goldstein, and Jessica Silver-Greenberg), the *Wall Street Journal* (May 2023 investigation), and the *Daily Beast* (2021 reporting). The record:

He met Epstein in **2011** — the three years after Epstein's 2008 Florida conviction. The 2011 meetings included: a January 2011 party at Epstein's Manhattan townhouse; a March 2011 conference in Long Beach, California; a May 2011 meeting at Epstein's Manhattan townhouse with attendees including Boris Nikolic (the Gates "right hand" and chief science advisor) and Larry Summers (the former US Treasury Secretary). The 2011 Gates-internal email referring to Epstein's "lifestyle" — disclosed by *The New York Times* in the 2019 investigation — contained the Gates text: "His lifestyle is very different and kind of intriguing although it would not work for me."

The Gates-Epstein contact across the 2011-2014 period included at least six meetings (per the *New York Times* investigation), and per the *Daily Beast* reporting potentially as many as dozens of meetings as late as 2014. # Dossier 5: Michael Mann

The prior four dossiers dossiered **Anthony Fauci**, **Peter Daszak**, **Francis Collins**, and **Bill Gates** as the senior-figure operational anchors of the public-health and captured-philanthropy sectoral architectures. This dossier dossiers **Michael Evan Mann** — the senior figure whose positioning at the co-opted-climate-policy sectoral architecture (Chapter 8) is the canonical case of the contested climate-science senior-figure positioning at the US-and-international scale.

Mann is the figure who anchors a specific operational position the prior four dossiers did not name: the **single-scientific-paper-as-institutional-anchor operational position**, in which the contested positioning of a single named published paper has anchored the global climate-policy positioning across two-and-a-half decades. The named paper is the 1999 Mann-Bradley-Hughes "Northern hemisphere temperatures during the past millennium" publication in *Geophysical Research Letters* — the paper subsequently characterised in the scientific commentary as the **"hockey stick graph"** for the shape of the reconstructed temperature record's twentieth-century terminal rise. The hockey-stick graph is the single most-extensively-deployed scientific-visualisation in the US-and-international climate-policy architecture; the Mann personal-and-institutional career has been principally organised, since the post-1999 period, around the contested positioning of the hockey-stick graph at the IPCC, US-federal-government, and US-public-policy institutional levels.

The evidence standard from Chapters 1 through 14 and Dossiers 1, 2, 3, and 4 holds. The dossier does not adjudicate the underlying contested scientific question of whether the hockey-stick reconstruction is correct on the underlying paleoclimate-reconstruction methodological merits. The evidence covers the career, the hockey-stick publication and contested commentary record, the 2009 Climategate email-disclosure episode, the named ongoing defamation litigation, and the 2024-2026 positioning at the record level. The evidence is available.


## I. Origins

**Michael Evan Mann** was born **28 December 1965** in **Amherst, Massachusetts** — the named academic-town setting at which the Five College Consortium (Amherst College, Smith College, Mount Holyoke College, Hampshire College, and the University of Massachusetts at Amherst) operates the US senior-academic-research-institution-cluster positioning. His family-of-origin was the US academic-and-professional middle-class household; his subsequent published biographical record characterises his upbringing as one in which scientific and intellectual curiosity were the household-positioning institutional values across the 1965-1983 formative years.

Mann attended **Amherst Regional High School** in the Amherst, Massachusetts public-school positioning level. The Mann educational trajectory: **AB in physics and applied mathematics from the University of California, Berkeley** in 1989; **MS, MPhil, and PhD in geology and geophysics from Yale University** at the 1989-1998 graduate-school institutional period. His doctoral dissertation, supervised by **Barry Saltzman** at the Yale Department of Geology and Geophysics, was on the topic of climate-system dynamics and the spectral-analysis methods for the contested paleoclimate-reconstruction questions. The Mann PhD was awarded in 1998.

The observation about Mann's family-of-origin and pre-1998 trajectory: the trajectory was the trajectory of an academically-credentialed US-state-flagship-and-Ivy-League-trained physicist-turned-climate-scientist whose pre-1998 positioning operated at the US academic-research-institution-graduate-school level. The Mann case is not the case of inherited family-network advantage; it is the case of the meritocratically-credentialed climate-science researcher whose post-1998 positioning at the named published hockey-stick paper anchored his subsequent prolonged contested institutional career.


## II. The hockey-stick papers, 1998-1999

The most-extensively-event in the Mann record is the **1998 and 1999 hockey-stick publications**. The dossier dossiers the record.

### The MBH98 paper

In **April 1998**, Mann, **Raymond S. Bradley** (University of Massachusetts Amherst climatologist), and **Malcolm K. Hughes** (University of Arizona dendrochronologist) published "Global-scale temperature patterns and climate forcing over the past six centuries" in *Nature* 392, 779-787 (23 April 1998). The paper used statistical methods to reconstruct hemispherical temperature variations across the past 600 years from proxy data including tree-ring records, ice-core records, coral records, and historical-document-based records. The named 1998 publication is the paper subsequently characterised in the scientific commentary as the **MBH98 paper** (for the author surnames and publication year).

### The MBH99 paper and the "hockey stick" naming

In **March 1999**, the same Mann-Bradley-Hughes team published "Northern hemisphere temperatures during the past millennium: Inferences, uncertainties, and limitations" in *Geophysical Research Letters* 26(6), 759-762 (15 March 1999). The named 1999 publication extended the 1998 reconstruction backward to cover the past 1,000 years. The named 1999 paper is the paper subsequently characterised in the scientific commentary as the **MBH99 paper**.

The naming of the MBH99-reconstruction as the **"hockey stick"** occurred when Mann presented the reconstruction at the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration's Geophysical Fluid Dynamics Laboratory at Princeton. **Jerry D. Mahlman** — the director of the Geophysical Fluid Dynamics Laboratory at the 1999 positioning level — coined the "hockey stick" terminology for the shape of the reconstructed temperature curve: a long, relatively flat shaft representing the stable temperatures across the past millennium, with a sharp upward "blade" representing the twentieth-century industrial-period temperature rise.

### The IPCC Third Assessment Report positioning

In **2001**, Mann was named as one of **eight lead authors** of the "Observed Climate Variability and Change" chapter of the **Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC) Third Assessment Report** — the global-climate-change institutional report published 2001. The hockey-stick graph was featured **multiple times** in the Third Assessment Report including in the "Summary for Policymakers" section that has, across the 2001-2007 period, anchored the hockey-stick graph at the IPCC positioning level.

The IPCC Third Assessment Report positioning was the operational event at which the Mann hockey-stick graph entered the global-climate-policy architecture at the public-positioning level. The subsequent Al Gore-and-IPCC joint 2007 Nobel Peace Prize at the Nobel Peace Prize 2007 award ceremony. At which Mann was one of the IPCC contributing-author senior figures whose contributions to the IPCC reports anchored the IPCC Nobel Peace Prize positioning — was the event at which Mann's hockey-stick contribution was globally recognised at the Nobel Prize level.

The empirical commentary on the MBH98 and MBH99 hockey-stick reconstructions at the post-2003 level operated through multiple senior-figure contested critiques. The principal critique was the **Stephen McIntyre** (the Canadian retired-mining-executive and contested-policy senior figure who founded the *Climate Audit* website in 2002) and **Ross McKitrick** (the University of Guelph economist) joint contested critique published in **2003** (Energy and Environment 14, 751-771) and **2005** (Geophysical Research Letters 32, L03710). The McIntyre-McKitrick critique characterised the MBH98 and MBH99 reconstructions as the contested case of statistical-methodology-and-tree-ring-data-selection positioning at the contested paleoclimate-reconstruction operational level.

The subsequent 2006 National Research Council report (*Surface Temperature Reconstructions for the Last 2,000 Years*, National Academies Press, June 2006) and the 2006 Wegman Report (commissioned by the US House Committee on Energy and Commerce under Chairman Joe Barton, published July 2006) constituted the contested-institutional-investigations of the hockey-stick reconstructions at the post-2005 level. The NRC report characterised the MBH98 and MBH99 reconstructions as "plausible" with stated reservations about the statistical-methodology and tree-ring-proxy-data questions. The Wegman Report characterised the MBH98 and MBH99 reconstructions in more critical terms at the statistical-methodology level.

The volume documents the contested record. The volume does not at the contested-paleoclimate-reconstruction-methodology level adjudicate the underlying contested question of whether the hockey-stick reconstructions are correct on the statistical-methodology and proxy-data-selection underlying merits. This section documents the record. The standard is verifiability.


## III. The Climategate episode, November 2009

The most extensively contested-institutional episode in the Mann record is the **November 2009 Climategate email-disclosure episode**. The dossier dossiers the record.

On **17 November 2009** — roughly three weeks before the **Copenhagen Climate Conference (COP15)** scheduled for 7-18 December 2009 — an unknown entity disclosed roughly **1,073 emails and 3,587 other files** from the **Climatic Research Unit (CRU)** at the University of East Anglia in Norwich, United Kingdom. The disclosed emails dated from the 1996-2009 period and contained correspondence among senior figures of the international climate-science research community, including Mann, **Phil Jones** (the CRU director), **Keith Briffa** (the CRU senior dendrochronologist), and named other senior figures of the international climate-science research community.

The disclosed emails contained correspondence that the contested-policy commentary subsequently characterised as the contested-institutional-record of coordination among the international climate-science senior-figure community on the multiple contested-and-policy questions. The principal quoted-correspondence passages included: the **"hide the decline"** Jones-to-Mann email of 16 November 1999 (the Jones email containing the phrase about a "trick" of "hiding the decline" in the post-1960 dendrochronological proxy record at the Briffa reconstruction level). The **Phil Jones-Michael Mann correspondence** on the contested Medieval Warm Period positioning; and the additional correspondence at the institutional-coordination operational layer.

The Climategate episode at the November-December 2009 period coincided with the Copenhagen Climate Conference and was the contested-institutional-positioning event at which the international climate-science research community's internal correspondence was publicly disclosed at the contested-institutional-record level. The subsequent investigations:

The **Penn State University investigation** of Mann's positioning at the Climategate disclosed correspondence was conducted in two phases: a February 2010 initial inquiry by a Penn State faculty committee that cleared Mann of three of four named allegations. And a July 2010 second-phase investigation by a Penn State faculty investigatory committee that cleared Mann of the remaining research-misconduct allegation. The Penn State investigations have been the subject of contested-policy commentary at the institutional-investigation-procedure level, including the temporal-coincidence of the Penn State investigations and the separate Penn State process the Jerry Sandusky child-sexual-abuse case at the Penn State level was operating at the same 2010-2012 period.

The additional Climategate-period investigations: the **UK House of Commons Science and Technology Committee investigation** (March 2010 report). The **Muir Russell Review** (the Independent Climate Change Email Review chaired by **Sir Muir Russell**, published 7 July 2010). The **Oxburgh Inquiry** (the Scientific Assessment Panel chaired by **Lord Ron Oxburgh**, published 14 April 2010). The **US Environmental Protection Agency endangerment-finding response** (the EPA's December 2010 response to the Petitioners' Reconsideration Request that characterised the Climategate emails as not institutionally invalidating the EPA endangerment finding); and the **US National Science Foundation Office of Inspector General investigation** (August 2011 closeout report).

Each of the named investigations characterised the disclosed emails as the contested-institutional-record that did not at the investigative-finding level establish scientific-research-misconduct against Mann or against the broader international climate-science research community at the contested-research-misconduct level. The contested-policy commentary on the Climategate investigations has, during the post-2010 period, characterised the investigations as the contested-institutional-positioning case at the institutional-investigation-procedure-and-findings level.

The evidence documents the record. The volume does not at the contested-research-misconduct level adjudicate the underlying disputed question of whether the Climategate emails established scientific-research-misconduct at the institutional-investigation-finding level. The volume documents the record. You are the assessor of last resort.


## IV. The Penn State period and the move to the University of Pennsylvania

Mann's Penn State period operated across the **2005-2022** positioning level. Mann joined the Penn State Department of Meteorology as an Associate Professor in 2005, was promoted to Full Professor in 2009, and was named **Distinguished Professor of Atmospheric Science** in 2013. Mann served as the **Director of the Penn State Earth System Science Center** across the 2005-2022 institutional period.

In **July 2022**, Mann announced his move from Penn State to the **University of Pennsylvania**, where he was named **Presidential Distinguished Professor in the Department of Earth and Environmental Science** at the Penn School of Arts and Sciences and inaugural director of the **Penn Center for Science, Sustainability, and the Media (PCSSM)**. Mann was additionally named as **Penn's Vice Provost for Climate Science, Policy, and Action** at the 2022 positioning level. The Mann post-2022 positioning operates at the Ivy League-and-climate-policy positioning level at the University of Pennsylvania architecture.


## V. The Mann v. Steyn and Simberg defamation litigation

The most extensively contested-litigation case in the Mann record is the **Mann v. Steyn and Simberg defamation lawsuit** filed in 2012 in the District of Columbia Superior Court. The dossier dossiers the record.

### Origin

The defamation lawsuit was filed by Mann in **October 2012** against four defendants: **Mark Steyn** (the Canadian-British conservative columnist contributing to *National Review*); **Rand Simberg** (the former adjunct fellow at the Competitive Enterprise Institute, the Washington-based libertarian-positioned think tank, dossiered in Chapter 10 § III); ***National Review*** (the US conservative-positioned magazine founded 1955 by William F. Buckley Jr.); and the **Competitive Enterprise Institute (CEI)** (the Washington-based libertarian-positioned think tank).

The defamation claim related to two 2012 blog posts published by Simberg at the Competitive Enterprise Institute website and by Steyn at the *National Review* website. The Simberg blog post compared the Penn State investigation of Mann's Climategate-era positioning with the Penn State handling of the Jerry Sandusky case at the Penn State level. Steyn's subsequent blog post quoted the Simberg comparison and characterised the Mann research as "fraudulent."

### Pre-trial procedural history

The pre-trial procedural history extended across the 2012-2024 period at the twelve-year-litigation level. The pre-trial procedural history included anti-SLAPP motions, appellate review at the District of Columbia Court of Appeals level, certiorari petitions to the US Supreme Court (including the November 2019 cert denial in which Justice Samuel Alito dissented). And the 2021 summary judgment ruling in favour of *National Review* and the Competitive Enterprise Institute (which removed the two institutional defendants from the continuing litigation while retaining Steyn and Simberg as the individual defendants at the continuing-litigation level).

### The 2024 trial verdict

The **Mann v. Steyn and Simberg trial** was conducted in the **District of Columbia Superior Court** before Judge **Alfred S. Irving Jr.** across a four-week trial period in **January-February 2024**. The six-member jury announced its unanimous verdict on **8 February 2024**:

- Compensatory damages: **$1 against each defendant** (the nominal one-dollar compensatory damage award);
- Punitive damages: **$1,000,000 against Mark Steyn** and **$1,000 against Rand Simberg** (the punitive-damages award).

The jury verdict found that the blog posts had been published with "maliciousness, spite, ill will, vengeance or deliberate intent to harm" at the defamation-tort-standard level.

### Post-verdict reductions and sanctions

The post-verdict procedural history at the post-February-2024 level subsequently reduced and sanctioned the Mann v. Steyn trial outcome at the Superior Court level:

In **January 2025**, Judge Irving ordered Mann to pay more than **$530,000** in legal fees to *National Review* under the District of Columbia Anti-SLAPP Act for the anti-SLAPP motions the *National Review* defendants had filed at the pre-2021-summary-judgment institutional period level.

In **March 2025**, Judge Irving reduced the Steyn punitive-damages award from **$1,000,000 to $5,000** at the post-trial-review level — the Irving determination characterised the original $1,000,000 punitive-damages award as the "grossly excessive" defamation-tort-standard positioning.

On **12 March 2025**, Judge Irving sanctioned Mann and his legal team for the "bad-faith trial misconduct" — the Irving determination characterised the disparities in the case-handling as the "affront to the Court's authority" at the procedural-misconduct level. The Irving determination characterised the Mann trial-testimony grant-loss-claim positioning as the contested-institutional-record of misleading trial testimony at the Superior Court trial level.

In **November 2025**, Mann reached an agreement with *National Review* under which Mann agreed to drop his appeal of the January 2025 legal-fees order in exchange for *National Review*'s agreement to not seek the additional legal-costs assessment.

### Structural observation on the litigation

The Mann v. Steyn and Simberg litigation operated across the twelve-year-litigation institutional period at the public-figure-defamation-tort level. The February 2024 jury verdict was the positioning event at which Mann's defamation claim was vindicated at the Superior Court trial-jury level. The post-verdict reductions and sanctions across the 2025 institutional period constituted the reversal at the post-trial-judicial-review level. The litigation outcome at the 2026 documentary record level is the contested-institutional-record at the public-figure-defamation-tort level. The case at which the original $1,000,000+ jury verdict was reduced at the post-trial-review level to the $5,000 punitive damages plus $1 nominal compensatory damages against Steyn, with Mann bearing the $530,000+ legal-fees obligation to *National Review* prior to the November 2025 settlement-agreement positioning.


## VI. The post-2024 positioning

The Mann post-2024 positioning operates at the multi-institutional US-and-international level:

The **Penn Vice Provost for Climate Science, Policy, and Action** positioning at the University of Pennsylvania post-2022 level. The **PCSSM Director** positioning. The continuing IPCC positioning at the IPCC Working Group level. The **2024 election to Foreign Member of the Royal Society** positioning at the UK Royal Society international scientific-society level. The **2020 election to the US National Academy of Sciences** positioning at the US National Academy level. # Dossier 6: James Hansen

The prior five dossiers dossiered **Anthony Fauci**, **Peter Daszak**, **Francis Collins**, **Bill Gates**, and **Michael Mann** as the senior-figure operational anchors of the co-opted public-health, captured-philanthropy, and captured-climate-policy sectoral architectures at the US-and-international level. This dossier dossiers **James Edward Hansen** — the senior figure whose positioning at the **NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies (GISS)** across the 1981-2013 period anchored the US climate-policy architecture at the founding-figure pre-Mann operational positioning level.

Hansen is the figure who anchors the position the prior five dossiers did not name at depth: the **founding-senior-figure-as-government-scientist-then-activist operational position**. In which a single US federal-government scientist's positioning across a 46-year US federal-government career anchored the US climate-policy public-positioning architecture at the founding moment and continued the US climate-policy advocacy positioning at the post-retirement level. Mann (Dossier 5) is the post-1998 anchor of the co-opted-climate-policy sectoral architecture at the contested paleoclimate-reconstruction senior-figure operational level; Hansen is the pre-Mann founding-figure anchor of the US climate-policy public-positioning architecture at the 1988 Senate-testimony founding moment.

The standard applied from Chapters 1 through 14 and Dossiers 1, 2, 3, 4, and 5 holds. The dossier does not adjudicate the contested questions on the underlying climate-science questions Hansen's positioning has operated on. The record traces the career, the 1981 *Science* paper publication, the **23 June 1988 Senate testimony** founding event, the post-1988 GISS directorship, the post-2005 Bush-administration institutional conflict, the 2013 NASA retirement, the post-2013 Columbia Earth Institute positioning and the climate-activism positioning, and the 2024-2026 positioning at the evidence level. You are the final judge.


## I. Origins

**James Edward Hansen** was born **29 March 1941** in **Denison, Iowa** — the small-agricultural-town setting in Crawford County in western Iowa. His parents operated a small family business; his subsequent published biographical record characterises his Iowa upbringing as one in which scientific curiosity was the household-positioning value across the 1941-1959 formative years.

Hansen attended the **University of Iowa** at Iowa City, graduating with a BA in physics and mathematics in 1963. He continued at the University of Iowa for graduate study under the **James Van Allen** — the discoverer of the Van Allen radiation belts and one of the founding senior figures of US space science at the University of Iowa Department of Physics and Astronomy. Hansen earned his MS in astronomy in 1965 and his PhD in physics from the University of Iowa in 1967 with a dissertation on the Venus-atmosphere question; his doctoral research applied the Van Allen-trained physics-and-astronomy methodology to the question of the Venus-atmospheric-radiative-transfer properties at the planetary-science level.

His post-1967 institutional trajectory: postdoctoral fellow at the **NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies (GISS)** in New York City from 1967-1969 under the NASA National Academies of Sciences National Research Council Resident Research Associate programme. Postdoctoral fellow at the **Leiden Observatory** in the Netherlands 1969-1970; research associate at Columbia University 1970-1972; NASA-GISS space scientist and subsequently Manager of the Planetary and Climate Programs from 1972-1981. The pre-1981 Hansen research contributions operated principally at the planetary-atmospheric-radiative-transfer positioning level on Venus, Jupiter, and Earth atmospheric questions at the NASA-GISS senior-figure-research-scientist level.

The observation about Hansen's family-of-origin and pre-1981 trajectory: the trajectory was the trajectory of an academically-credentialed Iowa-trained physicist-turned-planetary-scientist whose pre-1981 positioning operated at the NASA federal-government research-scientist level. The Hansen case is the case of the meritocratically-credentialed US federal-government scientist whose post-1981 positioning at the NASA-GISS directorship anchored his subsequent sustained contested-policy institutional career as the founding-figure of US climate-policy public-positioning.


## II. The GISS directorship and the 1981 *Science* paper

Hansen was named **Director of the NASA Goddard Institute for Space Studies** in 1981. The GISS positioning was the NASA federal-government research-institute operating at the Columbia University campus in New York City — the positioning that operated as the NASA Goddard Space Flight Center New York satellite institute at the US federal-government climate-and-planetary-science architecture. Hansen held the GISS Director position across the 32-year tenure period 1981-2013 — the longest GISS directorship tenure.

Hansen and seven co-authors published "Climate Impact of Increasing Atmospheric Carbon Dioxide" in *Science* 213(4511), 957-966 (28 August 1981). ## III. The 23 June 1988 Senate testimony

The most extensively-founding-public-positioning event in the Hansen record is the **23 June 1988 Senate testimony**. The dossier dossiers the record.

### The founding event

On **23 June 1988**, Hansen testified before the **United States Senate Committee on Energy and Natural Resources** at the US Senate hearing on climate change. The 23 June 1988 Senate hearing was scheduled at the summer-period US Senate positioning. The scheduling decision his subsequent published commentary has characterised as the institutional choice resulting from a 1987 staffer-level discussion at which Hansen named the positioning concern that US climate-change Senate hearings had been  scheduled in cold-weather periods and had received the limited media attention at the US Senate level.

The 23 June 1988 Washington, D.C. weather conditions were exceptionally hot — the 98-degree Fahrenheit Washington, D.C. temperature at the record-temperature-for-that-date level (the record temperature that the Bloomberg subsequent commentary has characterised as the hottest 23 June Washington recorded temperature that remained on record through at least 2021). The named high-temperature Washington conditions produced the intensified media attention to the Senate testimony at the US national-media level.

### The Hansen testimony statements

The Hansen Senate testimony of 23 June 1988 contained the three principal institutional-positioning conclusions:

1. **"The earth is warmer in 1988 than at any time in the history of instrumental measurements."**
2. **"The global warming is now large enough that we can ascribe with a high degree of confidence a cause and effect relationship to the greenhouse effect."**
3. **"Our computer climate simulations indicate that the greenhouse effect is already large enough to begin to effect the probability of extreme events such as summer heat waves."**

Hansen additionally characterised the position at the "99% confidence" institutional-positioning level — the quoted Hansen statement that "with 99% confidence we can state that the warming during this time period is a real warming trend" at the US Senate testimony positioning level.

### The post-testimony positioning

His 23 June 1988 Senate testimony was the positioning event at which the contested anthropogenic-climate-change question entered the US public-political architecture at the founding moment. The 24 June 1988 *New York Times* front-page coverage of the Senate testimony, under the headline **"Global Warming Has Begun, Expert Tells Senate"** by Philip Shabecoff, was the US national-media positioning event at which the US public-positioning on the contested anthropogenic-climate-change question entered the US public-political-discourse level.

The Hansen 1988 Senate testimony additionally anchored the subsequent US federal-government positioning at the 1988 establishment of the **Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change (IPCC)** in November 1988 (the IPCC positioning Mann's Dossier 5 § II addresses). The 1992 United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC) positioning at the Rio Earth Summit level, the 1997 Kyoto Protocol positioning, the 2015 Paris Agreement positioning, and the subsequent decades-long US-and-international climate-policy architecture at the post-1988 contested-policy level. **The Hansen 1988 Senate testimony is the canonical US founding-public-positioning event of the US-and-international climate-policy architecture at the 1988-onwards record level.**

## I. The Alex Stamos institutional trajectory

**Alex Stamos** is the Greek-American computer scientist and cybersecurity senior figure whose institutional trajectory during the post-1997 US tech-industry-and-academia operational positioning anchored his subsequent Stanford Internet Observatory founding-director positioning. The dossier dossiers the record.

### Origins and early career

Stamos grew up in **Fair Oaks, California** and graduated from **Bella Vista High School** in 1997. He attended the **University of California, Berkeley**, graduating in 2001 with a BS in Electrical Engineering and Computer Science (EECS). His pre-2004 career operated at the named Loudcloud positioning at the post-2001 California Bay Area tech-industry level, followed by the documented @stake security-consultancy positioning level.
