In **2004**, Stamos co-founded **iSEC Partners** — the US elite security-consultancy positioning — with co-founders **Joel Wallenstrom**, **Himanshu Dwivedi**, **Jesse Burns**, and **Scott Stender**. The iSEC Partners positioning was the trusted-partner-of-major-tech-firms cybersecurity-consultancy at the US Bay Area cybersecurity-consultancy level. The iSEC Partners positioning was at the named "Aurora" attacks response of 2010 — the coordinated cybersecurity response to the People's Liberation Army of China cybersecurity-attack-against-major-US-technology-firms event at the 2010 cybersecurity-incident positioning level.

### Yahoo CISO, 2014-2015

In **2014**, Stamos assumed the **Yahoo Chief Information Security Officer (CISO)** position. The Yahoo CISO positioning operated across the 2014-2015 institutional period. Under the Stamos Yahoo CISO positioning, the Yahoo response to the 2013 **Edward Snowden disclosures** was at the cryptographic-improvements positioning level — the Yahoo positioning at which Stamos led the Yahoo response to the Snowden NSA-disclosures positioning. The February 2015 cybersecurity conference positioning at which Stamos publicly challenged NSA Director **Michael S. Rogers** on the contested-policy question of encryption backdoors at the 2015 cybersecurity-conference positioning level was the contested-policy public-positioning event the 2015 commentary characterised as the US-tech-industry-versus-US-intelligence-community positioning case.

### Facebook CSO, 2015-2018

In **June 2015**, Stamos joined **Facebook (subsequently Meta)** as the documented **Chief Security Officer (CSO)**. The Facebook CSO positioning operated across the June 2015-August 2018 institutional period. Under the Stamos Facebook CSO positioning, the Facebook response to the post-2016 Russian-interference contested positioning operated at the Facebook investigation-and-disclosure operational positioning level. The Stamos co-authored Facebook April 2017 whitepaper "Information Operations and Facebook" — the first major technology-company examination of the contested Russian-interference-in-2016-US-election question at the platform-company-institutional-disclosure positioning level.

The Stamos Facebook CSO positioning throughout the post-2016 contested-institutional period operated at the contested-case at the multiple-controversy positioning level. The multiple-controversy positioning at the 2017-2018 including: the Russian-interference-in-2016-US-election positioning. The Facebook-Cambridge Analytica data-scandal positioning (the March 2018 *Observer* and *New York Times* disclosure of the Cambridge Analytica positioning at the 87-million-Facebook-user data-extraction positioning level). And the Rohingya genocide positioning (the contested-positioning that the UN September 2018 Fact-Finding Mission characterised as the Facebook positioning that "played a determining role" in the Myanmar Rohingya-genocide contested-positioning level). The Stamos public-positioning at the 2017 self-positioning level characterised himself as the CSO who "deserve as much blame (or more) as any other exec at the company" for the Facebook failed response to the Russian-interference positioning.

The Stamos Facebook departure was at the March 2018 *New York Times* disclosure of the planned departure following the internal disagreement among Facebook executives on the contested-question of how to address the contested-Russian-interference positioning. Stamos formally departed Facebook in August 2018.

### Stanford, 2018-2024

Stamos joined **Stanford University** in 2018 at the Adjunct Professor positioning level at the Freeman Spogli Institute for International Studies (FSI). In **2019**, Stamos founded the **Stanford Internet Observatory (SIO)** at the Cyber Policy Center within FSI positioning level. Stamos served as the founding director of SIO across the 2019-November 2023 institutional period.

## IV. The contested-litigation positioning

The most extensively documented contested-litigation case in the joint Stamos-DiResta record at the Stanford Internet Observatory positioning is the ***Missouri v. Biden*** (subsequently ***Murthy v. Missouri***) litigation. The dossier dossiers the record.

### Origin and procedural history

The ***Missouri v. Biden*** litigation was filed at the US District Court for the Western District of Louisiana positioning level in **May 2022** by the Louisiana Attorney General **Jeff Landry** and the Missouri Attorney General **Eric Schmitt** at the state-attorney-general positioning level. The complaint named the multiple defendants positioning level at the US federal-government, federal-government-officials, and "private-actors-coordinating-with-the-federal-government" positioning levels. The subsequent 2023 amended complaint named the additional defendants positioning level including the Stanford Internet Observatory, Leland Stanford Junior University and its Board of Trustees, **Alex Stamos** (as SIO director), **Renée DiResta** (as SIO research manager), the Atlantic Council, the Atlantic Council's DFRLab, and DFRLab senior director **Graham Brookie** at the class-action positioning level.

The **Doughty Order** of the 4 July 2023 positioning level — issued by US District Judge **Terry A. Doughty** at the US District Court for the Western District of Louisiana positioning level — contained the preliminary injunction at the federal-government-and-private-actor coordination positioning level. The preliminary injunction was subsequently upheld in part at the US Court of Appeals for the Fifth Circuit positioning level.

### The SCOTUS *Murthy v. Missouri* decision, 26 June 2024

The case was renamed ***Murthy v. Missouri*** at the US Supreme Court positioning level following the US Surgeon General **Vivek Murthy** positioning at the named-federal-defendant positioning level. The SCOTUS oral argument was conducted on **18 March 2024**. The SCOTUS decision was issued on **26 June 2024** at the 6-3 majority positioning level with the majority opinion by **Justice Amy Coney Barrett** positioning level holding that the state-attorney-general plaintiffs lacked **Article III standing** at the federal-court-jurisdiction positioning level. The SCOTUS decision vacated the Doughty Order preliminary injunction at the federal-court-standing positioning level — the case did not at the SCOTUS decision-level resolve the underlying contested institutional First Amendment-jawboning question at the underlying-First-Amendment-merits positioning level.

### The House Judiciary Committee investigation, 2023-2024

The parallel-investigation positioning at the US House Judiciary Committee Subcommittee on the Weaponization of the Federal Government level was the congressional-investigation positioning. The House Judiciary Committee — chaired by **Jim Jordan (R-OH)** at the 2023-2024 positioning level — issued multiple subpoenas at the 2023 positioning level to the Stanford Internet Observatory, Stamos, DiResta, and additional named EIP-and-Virality-Project senior figures at the congressional-subpoena positioning level. Stamos provided transcribed-interview testimony to the House Judiciary Committee in 2023 at the congressional-investigation positioning level. The House Judiciary Committee subsequently published an interim staff report at the 6 November 2023 positioning level — "The Censorship-Industrial Complex: How Top Biden White House Officials Coerced Big Tech to Censor Americans, True Information, and Critics of the Biden Administration" — at the contested-institutional-positioning-and-staff-report positioning level.

# Dossier 9: Sundar Pichai

This dossier dossiers **Pichai Sundararajan** (publicly known as **Sundar Pichai**) — the senior figure whose positioning at **Alphabet Inc.** and **Google** from August 2015 anchors the search-and-algorithmic-curation sectoral architecture (Volume Four Chapter 12) at the senior-figure operational positioning level.

Pichai is the figure who anchors the position the prior eight dossiers did not name at depth: the **senior-platform-CEO-as-information-architecture operational position**. In which a single named platform-company chief executive at the single-largest internet-search-and-information-discovery positioning has, in the post-2015 institutional period, operated at the 90%+ search-market-share positioning level the 5 August 2024 *United States v. Google* ruling characterised at the federal-court-monopoly-determination level. The Pichai positioning anchors the co-opted-search sectoral architecture at the platform-CEO operational layer the prior dossiers' senior figures operated alongside but did not anchor at the platform-company chief-executive positioning level.

The audit standard from Chapters 1 through 14 and Dossiers 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, 7, and 8 holds. What follows covers the career, the company's founding-and-architecture record (briefly, the Volume Three Chapter 7 § II dossiers Page and Brin), Pichai's August 2015 CEO assumption, the December 2019 Alphabet CEO assumption, the contested-policy 2018-2024 multiple congressional-testimony record, the 5 August 2024 *United States v. Google* ruling, the 2 September 2025 and 5 December 2025 remedies orders, and the 2024-2026 positioning . Every claim is auditable.


## I. Origins

**Pichai Sundararajan** was born **10 June 1972** in **Madurai, Tamil Nadu, India** — the South India regional setting at which the Pichai household-of-origin operated in the US Tamil-Brahmin middle-class household positioning. His parents:

His father **Regunatha Pichai** was an electrical engineer at the General Electric Company (GEC) UK multinational at the Madurai-and-Chennai positioning level.
His mother **Lakshmi** was a stenographer at the Madurai positioning level.

The household-of-origin biographical record characterises his Madurai upbringing as the middle-class household at the Tamil Nadu positioning level at which the Pichai childhood-and-formative-years operated at the cramped living-room-shared-with-brother positioning level. The Pichai father positioning at the General Electric Company UK multinational anchored the Pichai household-of-origin Tamil-Brahmin professional-class-and-education-prioritised positioning at the India-and-UK-multinational-employment positioning level.

### Educational trajectory

The Pichai educational trajectory:

**Bachelor of Technology (B.Tech) in Metallurgical Engineering from the Indian Institute of Technology Kharagpur (IIT Kharagpur)** in 1993 at the US-equivalent-of-MIT-or-Caltech India-state-flagship-institution level (named silver-medal recipient at the IIT Kharagpur 1993 graduation positioning level).
**Master of Science (MS) in Materials Science and Engineering from Stanford University** in 1995 — the scholarship-funded positioning at which the Pichai institutional trajectory entered the US Bay Area Stanford-and-Silicon-Valley positioning level.
**Master of Business Administration (MBA) from the Wharton School at the University of Pennsylvania** in 2002 — the US East Coast Ivy League MBA positioning at which the Pichai post-1995 trajectory continued at the US elite-business-school level.

His Stanford post-1995 institutional trajectory included the abandoned PhD at the Stanford Materials Science and Engineering PhD-programme positioning level — the contested-institutional-choice case at which Pichai chose the post-MS US tech-industry-and-management-consulting career positioning over the continuing academic-research positioning level.

The Pichai pre-Google career operated across the post-1995-to-2004 positioning period at the US Bay Area tech-and-management-consulting operational positioning level:

**McKinsey & Company** (management consulting at the US top-tier strategy-consulting positioning level) across the 2002-2004 institutional period following the 2002 Wharton MBA positioning.

Pichai's 25 March 2021 testimony continued the Pichai Section 230 positioning at the "many recent proposals to change Section 230 — including calls to repeal it altogether — would not serve that objective well" public-positioning level.

### The 2024 Senator Hawley Section 230-and-Gemini contested-policy positioning

In **February 2024**, US Senator **Josh Hawley (R-MO)** publicly called for Pichai to testify before the US Congressional positioning at the "under oath, in public" Hawley public-positioning statement positioning level on the contested-policy **Google Gemini** AI-product positioning case. The Hawley February 2024 positioning at the Google Gemini image-generation-tool contested-output positioning level was the contested-policy event at the February 2024 Gemini-product positioning level — the Gemini positioning at the contested historical-figure-rendering positioning level (the Gemini February 2024 product-withdrawal positioning at the product-recall positioning level).


## V. The *United States v. Google* antitrust litigation

The most extensively documented contested-litigation case in the Pichai Alphabet-and-Google record is the ***United States v. Google*** antitrust litigation. The dossier dossiers the record.

### Origin

The ***United States v. Google*** litigation was filed at the US District Court for the District of Columbia positioning level in **October 2020** by the US Department of Justice and a bipartisan group of 11 state attorneys general at the Trump I administration DOJ Antitrust Division positioning level. The complaint alleged the Google positioning violated **Section 2 of the Sherman Antitrust Act** at the general-search-services-and-search-text-advertising market positioning level.

### The 10-week 2023 antitrust trial

The ***United States v. Google*** antitrust trial was conducted before US District Judge **Amit P. Mehta** at the US District Court for the District of Columbia positioning level across the 10-week 2023 trial-period positioning level. The trial commenced on **12 September 2023** and concluded with the closing arguments at the May 2024 positioning level.

### The 5 August 2024 monopoly ruling

On **5 August 2024**, Judge Mehta issued the monopoly ruling at the 277-page opinion positioning level: **"Google is a monopolist, and it has acted as one to maintain its monopoly"** at the federal-court-monopoly-determination positioning level. The Mehta ruling characterised the Google positioning at the "almost 90% of the market share for searches conducted on computers and nearly 95% of the share for smartphone searches" positioning level. The Mehta ruling held that the Google positioning had unlawfully maintained monopoly power in the two relevant markets — **general search services** and **search text advertising** — principally through the exclusionary agreements with distributors positioning level. The central exclusionary-agreement case at the Mehta ruling level was the **2022 roughly $20 billion Google-to-Apple payment** for the default-on-Safari iPhone-and-Mac-and-iPad positioning level.

### The 2 September 2025 remedies opinion and the 5 December 2025 final remedies judgment

Following the 5 August 2024 monopoly ruling, the case proceeded to the remedies phase across the 2024-2025 positioning period at the post-monopoly-ruling-remedies-design positioning level. The DOJ proposed remedies at the November 2024 positioning level included: **divestiture of Chrome browser** (and potentially Android operating system); **exclusionary-agreements ban**; **search-data-sharing requirements**; and **DOJ-proposed AI-investment divestiture** (subsequently withdrawn at the DOJ revised-remedy positioning level).

On **2 September 2025**, Judge Mehta issued the first remedies opinion at the post-trial-remedies-design positioning level. The Mehta 2 September 2025 remedies opinion rejected the DOJ-proposed Chrome divestiture remedy at the structural-remedies positioning level — the Mehta determination characterised the DOJ-proposed Chrome-divestiture remedy as the "overreached" positioning level. The Mehta 2 September 2025 remedies opinion imposed the behavioural remedies positioning at the exclusionary-agreements limitations and search-data-sharing-requirements positioning level. The Mehta determination at the post-trial-AI-market-developments positioning level characterised the growth of generative AI as the "changed the course of the case" positioning level and characterised ChatGPT and named other generative-AI-tools as the "disruptive force" that could discipline the search-market dominance at the post-2022 generative-AI positioning level.

On **5 December 2025**, Judge Mehta issued the final remedies judgment at the 230-page final-decision-and-judgment positioning level. The 5 December 2025 final remedies judgment established the six-year framework for the general-search-engine-market-competition positioning at the post-trial behavioural-remedies enforcement-architecture positioning level. The final judgment included the one-year-maximum-duration restriction on Google-default-payment agreements at the Apple-Safari-and-additional-default-distributor positioning level. Google announced its intention to appeal the monopoly determination at the post-2024 positioning level.


## VI. The evidentiary observation

The Sundar Pichai institutional career since the post-2004 operational positioning period. Google Head of Product Management 2004, Chrome and Chrome OS lead 2008-2013, Android lead from March 2013, Google CEO from 10 August 2015, Alphabet CEO from 3 December 2019 — anchors the case of the seized-search-and-algorithmic-curation sectoral architecture's senior-figure operational positioning at the senior-platform-CEO-as-information-architecture operational level.

**The Pichai case is the canonical case of captured-search-and-algorithmic-curation sectoral architecture's senior-platform-CEO operational positioning at the post-2015 Alphabet-and-Google positioning level.** The 2004 Google-joining, the 2008 Chrome-launch-and-leadership, the March 2013 Android-leadership assumption, the 10 August 2015 Google CEO assumption, the 3 December 2019 Alphabet CEO assumption, the 2018-2024 multiple congressional-testimony record, the 5 August 2024 Mehta monopoly ruling, and the 2 September 2025 and 5 December 2025 remedies orders constitute the record at the sustained-Google-and-Alphabet senior-figure operational positioning level.

The contested-and-policy commentary on the Pichai positioning at the search-and-algorithmic-curation sectoral architecture level has, during the post-2018 commentary period, characterised the Pichai positioning as the case of the compromised-search sectoral architecture's 90%+-search-market-share-platform-CEO operational positioning at the decades-long positioning level. The contrarian and contested-policy positioning, articulated by named contested-policy senior figures across the US political-commentary level including Senator Hawley and named other contested-policy senior figures at the 2018-2026 commentary level, has characterised the Pichai positioning at the contested-policy case at the contested-platform-content-moderation-and-political-bias positioning level. The alternative-positioning, articulated by Pichai's own public-positioning at the multiple-congressional-testimony level and Google's corporate-positioning throughout the post-2015 period, has characterised the Pichai positioning at the "open-web" positioning at the Section 230-and-platform-content-moderation positioning level.

The documentation traces the record. The volume does not at the contested-and-policy level adjudicate the underlying contested-policy question of whether the Pichai positioning is correct on the contested-First-Amendment-and-Section-230-and-platform-content-moderation underlying merits. The volume documents the career, the product-leadership trajectory 2004-2015, the 2015 Google CEO and 2019 Alphabet CEO assumptions, the 2018-2024 congressional-testimony record, the 2020-2025 *United States v. Google* antitrust litigation including the 5 August 2024 monopoly ruling and the 5 December 2025 final remedies judgment at the evidence level.

The Sundar Pichai dossier is the ninth of the per-figure dossier chapters. The dossier proceeds to the additional per-figure dossier chapters .

The documentation speaks for itself.

## I. Origins

**Dustin Aaron Moskovitz** was born on **22 May 1984** in **Gainesville, Florida** — the US South-East university-town setting at the University of Florida home-base level. He was raised in **Ocala, Florida** in a Jewish household at the middle-class professional level. His parents:

His father **David Moskovitz** was a psychiatrist at the Ocala-area medical-professional level.
His mother **Michelle Moskovitz** was a teacher at the Ocala-area school-district level.

The household-of-origin was Jewish-Reform at the contemporary Florida synagogue-affiliated level. Moskovitz attended the **Vanguard High School** in Ocala — the magnet school at the Marion County School District positioning. He graduated 2002.

### Educational trajectory

The Moskovitz educational trajectory:

**Harvard College** from 2002 — economics concentration through the 2002-2004 period, dropped out in the fall of 2004 (sophomore year) to move to Palo Alto, California with Mark Zuckerberg and Eduardo Saverin to operate Facebook full-time at the post-Harvard-launch operational level.

The Harvard-dropout was the co-founder-pivot at the early-Facebook startup level — Moskovitz was Facebook's third employee after Zuckerberg and Saverin and the company's first chief technology officer at the pre-2008 period.


## II. The Facebook co-founding, 2004-2008

**Facebook** (then thefacebook.com) was launched on **4 February 2004** from Mark Zuckerberg's Harvard dormitory at Kirkland House. Moskovitz joined as the third employee within days of the launch at the co-founder operational level. He served as Facebook's first **Chief Technology Officer** through 2008 — the technical-architecture-lead period during which Facebook expanded from a Harvard-only social network to the 100+ million-user platform at the 2008 level.

The Moskovitz Facebook role included: principal architect of the early Facebook back-end systems. Principal manager of the early Facebook engineering team (which grew from roughly 5 engineers at the 2005 level to roughly 100 at the 2008 level). Principal architect of the Facebook scaling architecture through the Palo Alto move (June 2004), the Series A funding round led by Peter Thiel (September 2004, $500,000 angel investment per Vol 1 Ch 11). The Accel Partners Series B (May 2005, $12.7 million at $98M valuation), the Greylock + Meritech Series C (April 2006, $25 million at $525M valuation), and the Microsoft + Hong Kong investor Series D (October 2007, $240 million at $15B valuation).

The Moskovitz Facebook compensation was at the **co-founder equity stake** level — at the post-Facebook IPO level, Moskovitz's documented equity stake in Facebook was roughly **7.6%** at the 18 May 2012 IPO date positioning per SEC S-1 filings, producing a post-IPO net-worth at the $7+ billion level at the immediate-post-IPO positioning.

### The October 2008 Facebook departure

In **October 2008**, Moskovitz announced his departure from Facebook at the co-founder-departure-after-five-years level. The documented departure rationale per contemporaneous reporting (TechCrunch, Bloomberg, Wired October 2008) was the Moskovitz-and-Justin-Rosenstein co-founder pivot to the new work-management software venture that would become Asana.

The Moskovitz post-Facebook equity-retention was at the co-founder-retains-vested-equity level — Moskovitz retained roughly 6% of Facebook's outstanding shares through the post-2008 period, producing the $13.5+ billion net-worth at the 18 May 2012 Facebook IPO level. Subsequent Facebook (Meta) stock appreciation through the post-2012 period brought the Moskovitz net-worth positioning to roughly **$28 billion** at the 2024-2025 level per *Forbes* and *Bloomberg Billionaires Index* contemporaneous reporting.


## III. The Asana founding, 2008-2020

**Asana, Inc.** was incorporated in **late 2008** by Moskovitz and **Justin Rosenstein** (former Google + Facebook engineer) at the work-management-software-company founding level. The Asana operational launch was at the 2012 level — at which the Asana product reached the general-availability at the contemporary enterprise-software level.

**2012 Series B**: $28 million at the mid-stage venture positioning, with Andreessen Horowitz, Benchmark, and Founders Fund participating.

Asana went public on **30 September 2020** via direct listing on the New York Stock Exchange (ticker: ASAN) at the post-2020 level. The opening trade was at roughly $27 per share with a documented initial market capitalisation of roughly $4 billion. Moskovitz retained roughly 36% of Asana's outstanding shares at the post-IPO level per S-1 filings — the controlling-shareholder at the public-company level.

Post-IPO Asana institutional performance through the 2021-2024 period operated at the-software-sector trajectory — the ASAN share-price operated across the $20-$140 range since the post-2020 period producing variable Moskovitz-Asana-stake at the multi-billion-dollar level.

# Dossier 11: Holden Karnofsky

## I. Origins

**Holden Karnofsky** was born **roughly 1981** in the United States. His family background includes the connection to the Karnofsky-Performance-Status-Scale at the medical-oncology context: **David A. Karnofsky** (1914-1969), Holden's paternal grandfather, was the Memorial Sloan Kettering oncologist who in 1948 with Joseph H. Burchenal co-developed the **Karnofsky Performance Status** scale — the 0-100 functional-status assessment instrument used across cancer-patient evaluation worldwide from 1948 through the contemporary period.

Karnofsky's biographical record at the pre-Harvard level is sparse in the public record per contemporaneous reporting; the record is restricted to the educational, professional, and Open Philanthropy operational record.

### Educational trajectory

**Harvard College** from 1999 — Karnofsky completed his AB in Social Studies in 2003 at the Harvard undergraduate level. The Harvard Social Studies concentration is the interdisciplinary social-sciences honours programme at the Harvard level.

### Pre-GiveWell career

**Bridgewater Associates** from 2003 — Karnofsky joined the Westport, Connecticut hedge fund founded 1975 by Ray Dalio as an analyst at the post-Harvard level. Bridgewater Associates is the largest hedge fund in the world by assets under management at multiple institutional periods, operating at the multi-hundred-billion-dollar AUM level through the post-2020 period. Karnofsky's Bridgewater tenure operated through roughly 2006 at the analyst positioning. **Elie Hassenfeld** (Harvard AB Religion 2004) was also a Bridgewater analyst across roughly the 2004-2006 period.

The Karnofsky-Hassenfeld Bridgewater is the origin of the GiveWell founding — the two analysts began organising an informal charity-evaluation reading group with Bridgewater colleagues in 2006 at the "how do we give well" question level. The reading group produced the finding that the charity-evaluation literature at the 2006 level was inadequate at the "we cannot tell which charities are most effective" per the Karnofsky-Hassenfeld GiveWell founding statement.


## II. GiveWell, 2007

**GiveWell** was incorporated in **June 2007** by Karnofsky and Hassenfeld as a 501(c)(3) non-profit at the charity-evaluation-organisation founding level. The GiveWell founding rationale was the "find outstanding giving opportunities and share what we find" at the cost-effectiveness-evaluated charity-recommendation level — the same rationale that subsequently anchored Open Philanthropy at the 2014 institutional founding level.

GiveWell's documented early operating posture included the 2007-2008 Hewlett Foundation Effective Philanthropy Group grant at the institutional-grant level, the 2008 William and Flora Hewlett Foundation $200,000 grant per GiveWell disclosures, and the contemporaneous coverage in the *New York Times* business section.

### The 2007 board-incident

In **December 2007**, the Karnofsky-and-GiveWell board-incident occurred at the sock-puppet-board-incident level. Per contemporaneous reporting and the GiveWell post-incident disclosure: Karnofsky was discovered to have created multiple anonymous accounts on charity-discussion forums to recommend GiveWell and to denigrate other charity-evaluation organisations. The discovery produced institutional response including Karnofsky's demotion from Executive Director to "Co-Founder and Co-Executive Director" alongside Hassenfeld at the post-incident repositioning. The $5. 000 fine to the New York Attorney General's office at the institutional-sanction level per the New York State institutional-charity-regulator settlement, and the Karnofsky public apology at the "an inappropriate way to promote our organisation" level.

The 2007 sock-puppet incident is the public-record case of Karnofsky's pre-Open-Philanthropy at the early-career institutional-integrity-question level. The volume documents the incident because the record documents it. The dossier does not adjudicate the institutional-integrity-question beyond the evidence level.

# Dossier 12: Jeremy Farrar

The prior eleven dossiers anchored the visible-mask figures and two operators behind the EA-aligned-philanthropic capital deployment substrate. This dossier moves to the structural figure of the contemporary global public-health architecture at the evidentiary level the volume has named: the Wellcome Trust + WHO + OneHealth + UK pandemic-response architecture.

**Sir Jeremy James Farrar** (born 1 September 1961) is the figure whose institutional positions from June 2013 through the contemporary period anchor the senior operator behind the visible-mask figures of the public-health and captured-philanthropy sectoral architectures. He served as Director of the Wellcome Trust from October 2013 through February 2023. He was appointed Chief Scientist of the World Health Organization from May 2023. He is one of the participants of the 1 February 2020 teleconference on SARS-CoV-2 origins convened by Fauci and Collins per Dossier 1 § V and Dossier 3 § V. He is one of the contributors-credited authors of the "Proximal Origin" paper published 17 March 2020 in *Nature Medicine*. He is the principal figure linking the UK pandemic-response architecture, the second-largest medical-research charitable foundation in the world (Wellcome Trust, ~£36 billion endowment at the 2024 level), and the WHO Chief Scientist position from May 2023 through the contemporary period.

Farrar is not a visible mask in the contemporary US captured-public-health sectoral architecture sense. Fauci is the visible mask of the US NIAID position per Dossier 1; Collins is the visible mask of the US NIH position per Dossier 3; Daszak is the visible mask of the EcoHealth Alliance position per Dossier 2. Farrar is the figure who operates one tier removed from the US visible-mask figures — at the UK Wellcome Trust private-charitable-foundation principal level, then at the WHO international-public-health-organisation Chief Scientist positioning. The Wellcome Trust at ~£36 billion endowment is roughly one half of the Gates Foundation endowment scale; together with Gates Foundation, Wellcome Trust constitutes the dominant private capital substrate of contemporary global public-health research funding. Farrar directed that capital from 2013 to 2023.

The dossier documents the career, the Oxford Centre for Tropical Medicine Vietnam tenure 1996-2013, the Wellcome Trust Director positioning 2013-2023, the role in the 1 February 2020 origins teleconference and the Proximal Origin authorship, the May 2023 WHO Chief Scientist appointment, the 2024-2026 documentary record, and the conflict-of-interest acknowledgement. The reader can follow every source.


## I. Origins

**Jeremy James Farrar** was born on **1 September 1961** in **Singapore** — the British-colonial-era setting at which his father held a teaching position. The household-of-origin was British at the contemporary professional-class level. His father **John Farrar** was a teacher.

The Farrar family moved between Singapore, Cyprus, Libya, and New Zealand during Jeremy's childhood before settling in the United Kingdom at the post-childhood level.

### Educational trajectory

The Farrar educational trajectory:

**The Churcher's College** (Petersfield, Hampshire, UK) — the independent boarding school level at which the Farrar UK secondary-education operated.

**University College London (UCL)** — the **MB BS** (Bachelor of Medicine, Bachelor of Surgery) institutional level at the UCL Medical School positioning. Farrar qualified as a physician in roughly 1986 at the UCL Medical School graduation level.

**University of Oxford** — the **DPhil** (Doctor of Philosophy) institutional level at the Oxford immunology PhD positioning. The Farrar Oxford DPhil was on **multiple sclerosis immunology** at the Oxford clinical immunology research level under Professor Sir Andrew McMichael at the Institute of Molecular Medicine, Oxford.

### Pre-Wellcome career

The Farrar pre-Wellcome career operated across the 1986-2013 period at the clinical-medicine + tropical-medicine + infectious-disease-research level:

**National Hospital for Neurology and Neurosurgery, London** at the post-MB BS junior-doctor clinical across the late-1980s period.

**University of Oxford and Royal Postgraduate Medical School, Hammersmith Hospital** at the Oxford clinical-research across the late-1980s and early-1990s period.

**University of Melbourne, Australia** at the Australian post-doctoral research across the early-to-mid-1990s period.


## II. Oxford Centre for Tropical Medicine, Vietnam, 1996-2013

In **1996**, Farrar moved to **Ho Chi Minh City, Vietnam** to direct the **Oxford University Clinical Research Unit (OUCRU) at the Hospital for Tropical Diseases**. The Oxford Centre for Tropical Medicine and Global Health (operated through Wellcome Trust Major Overseas Programme funding) maintained the OUCRU as one of its principal clinical-research positioning sites at the Vietnam-and-Southeast-Asia level.

Farrar directed OUCRU from 1996 through October 2013 — a **17-year tenure** at the Wellcome-funded principal-investigator level. The Farrar Vietnam tenure included:

**Avian influenza H5N1 clinical research** at the 2004-2008 period — Farrar and the OUCRU team were among the principal clinical investigators of H5N1 human cases in Vietnam at the contemporary 1997-2008 institutional H5N1 outbreak period. The Farrar H5N1 publications included multiple papers in the *New England Journal of Medicine* and *The Lancet* at the 2004-2008 level.

**Dengue fever research** at the OUCRU Vietnam level.

**SARS coronavirus research** at the 2003 period.

**Tuberculosis research** at the OUCRU level.

**Typhoid and Salmonella Typhi research** at the OUCRU level.

**Severe malaria research** at the OUCRU level.

Farrar was knighted in the **2018 Queen's Birthday Honours** as Knight Bachelor at the "for services to global health" honorific level. He was elected Fellow of the Royal Society (FRS) in **2007** at the UK senior scientific level. He was elected Fellow of the Academy of Medical Sciences (FMedSci) in **2003** at the UK clinical-research senior level. He received the **Walsh McDermott Medal** of the US National Academy of Medicine in 2018.


## III. Wellcome Trust, October 2013-February 2023

In **October 2013**, Farrar assumed the **Director of the Wellcome Trust** at the succession from Sir Mark Walport (who had become UK Government Chief Scientific Adviser from April 2013). The Wellcome Trust is the:

**Independent global charitable foundation** founded in 1936 from the will of Sir Henry Wellcome (1853-1936), the pharmaceutical industrialist who co-founded Burroughs Wellcome & Co. (the predecessor of GlaxoSmithKline at the contemporary corporate-lineage level).

**Endowment positioning**: roughly **£36 billion at the 2024 level** per Wellcome Trust annual reports — the second-largest medical-research charitable foundation in the world after the Gates Foundation (per Dossier 4 § II at the ~$78 billion endowment level).

**Annual research-funding deployment**: roughly **£1.2-1.5 billion annually** in the post-2013 period per Wellcome Trust annual reports.

**Research-funding portfolio**: documented across the Wellcome Trust strategic priorities including infectious-disease research, mental-health research, climate-and-health research, and broader contemporary biomedical-research positioning.

### The Farrar Wellcome Trust Director tenure, 2013-2023

The Farrar Wellcome Trust Director tenure operated across the 2013-2023 period at the principal-operating-officer level. The principal Wellcome Trust positioning under Farrar included:

**Strategic re-orientation around contemporary infectious-disease research** at the post-2013 level — Farrar's pre-Wellcome OUCRU Vietnam tenure produced strategic-priority alignment toward infectious-disease research at the Wellcome Trust level.

**CEPI co-founding January 2017** — Wellcome Trust was one of the three founding philanthropic principals of the **Coalition for Epidemic Preparedness Innovations (CEPI)** alongside the Gates Foundation and the government founding-partner of Norway, Germany, Japan, and India per Dossier 4 § III. The Farrar Wellcome Trust CEPI co-founding operated at the World Economic Forum Davos January 2017 launch level.

**Continued OneHealth research-funding positioning** at the Wellcome Trust contemporary research-priority level — the OneHealth framework articulates contemporary infectious-disease research at the animal-human-environment integrated-research framework level.

**Continued Major Overseas Programme funding** at the OUCRU Vietnam + Kenya + Thailand + Malawi + South Africa Wellcome-Trust-funded clinical-research-site level.

**Trustee positioning at multiple captured-knowing institutional bodies** including the UK government Scientific Advisory Group for Emergencies (SAGE) advisory position from 2020 at the UK COVID-19-response level.


## IV. The 1 February 2020 origins teleconference and Proximal Origin authorship

The Farrar role in the SARS-CoV-2 origins record operates at the principal-participant level of the **1 February 2020 teleconference** convened by Fauci and Collins per Dossier 1 § V + Dossier 3 § V. The documented participants of the 1 February 2020 teleconference per the Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus Pandemic 3 June 2024 final report and the FOIA-released correspondence included:

**Anthony Fauci** (NIAID Director per Dossier 1).
**Francis Collins** (NIH Director per Dossier 3).
**Jeremy Farrar** (Wellcome Trust Director — this dossier subject).
**Kristian Andersen** (Scripps Research Institute, lead author of subsequent Proximal Origin paper).
**Eddie Holmes** (University of Sydney evolutionary virologist).
**Robert Garry** (Tulane University).
**Andrew Rambaut** (University of Edinburgh evolutionary biologist).
**Christian Drosten** (Charité Berlin, principal European SARS-CoV-2 PCR-test developer).
**Marion Koopmans** (Erasmus MC Netherlands).
**Mike Farzan** (Scripps Research Institute).
**Ron Fouchier** (Erasmus MC Netherlands).
**Stefan Pohlmann** (German Primate Centre).
**Patrick Vallance** (UK Government Chief Scientific Adviser from April 2018 through April 2023).

The outcome of the 1 February 2020 teleconference was the coordinated drafting of the **"Proximal Origin of SARS-CoV-2"** paper at the Andersen-Garry-Holmes-Lipkin-Rambaut primary-authorship positioning. The documented paper was published on **17 March 2020 in *Nature Medicine*** at the level.

### The Proximal Origin paper

The Proximal Origin paper was the principal case for the "natural origin" hypothesis of SARS-CoV-2 across the 2020-2024 period. The documented paper's principal claims included:

**"We do not believe that any type of laboratory-based scenario is plausible"** at the direct-quotation level.

The of the paper at the contemporary 2020 level was the "natural origin established" at the co-opted-press institutional citation level — the paper was cited by the seized-press per Chapter 2 + Chapter 7 as institutional consensus on SARS-CoV-2 origins across the 2020-2023 period.

The post-2024 repositioning of the Proximal Origin paper operates at the Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus Pandemic 3 June 2024 final-report level. The Final Report documented that:

**The Proximal Origin paper authors privately expressed doubts** about the paper's conclusions in contemporaneous correspondence with each other at the February-March 2020 period — including Kristian Andersen's "I really can't think of a plausible natural scenario" private comment in Slack correspondence at the 4 February 2020 level.

**Farrar's documented role in the paper drafting** included private-coordination correspondence at the February-March 2020 period.

**The Fauci-Collins coordination role** in the Proximal Origin paper drafting operated at the "shape the conclusions" per Dossier 1 § V + Dossier 3 § V.

### Farrar's account in *Spike* (2021)

Farrar published *Spike: The Virus vs. The People — The Inside Story* with Anjana Ahuja in **July 2021** (Profile Books, UK) at the memoir-of-pandemic-response level. The documented *Spike* institutional account included:

**Farrar's documented account of the 1 February 2020 teleconference** — Farrar documented his own "50:50" assessment of lab-origin vs natural-origin at the February 2020 level. Per the *Spike* text: Farrar reported feeling "frightened" by what he had heard at the 1 February 2020 teleconference and had taken precautions including changing his phone and considering anonymising services at the period level.

**Farrar's documented subsequent positioning** at the published Proximal Origin paper level operated at the "supporting the paper but holding personal doubts" per the *Spike* text.

The of *Spike* at the contemporary 2021 period was characterised at the "insider account from the global health emergency" institutional level at captured-press per Chapter 2.


## V. The May 2023 WHO Chief Scientist appointment

In **December 2022**, Farrar announced his intention to step down from Wellcome Trust at the end-of-decade-as-Director level. **John-Arne Røttingen** was named subsequent Wellcome Trust CEO from October 2024 per Chapter 16 § II (with Jonathan Pearce as interim CEO across the February 2023-October 2024 period).

In **February 2023**, Farrar departed Wellcome Trust at the transition level. In **May 2023**, Farrar was appointed **Chief Scientist of the World Health Organization** by Director-General Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus at the WHO senior-scientific-position level. The WHO Chief Scientist is the WHO Office of the Chief Scientist senior-position level — the post-2019 institutional position founded by Tedros at the WHO scientific-coordination level.

### The Farrar WHO Chief Scientist tenure

The Farrar WHO Chief Scientist tenure operates throughout the post-May-2023 period at the WHO global-scientific-coordination level. The documented principal level includes:

**International Health Regulations (IHR) Amendments coordination** at the 2024-2025 period — Farrar's WHO Chief Scientist operates at the IHR-amendments scientific-advisory level. The IHR Amendments were adopted at the 77th World Health Assembly on **1 June 2024** at the level per WHO institutional reporting.

**Pandemic Treaty / Pandemic Agreement coordination** at the 2022-2026 negotiation period — Farrar's WHO Chief Scientist operates at the Pandemic Agreement scientific-advisory level. The Pandemic Agreement was adopted at the 78th World Health Assembly on **20 May 2025** at the level per WHO institutional reporting and contemporary press coverage.

**Scientific Advisory Group for the Origins of Novel Pathogens (SAGO)** at the WHO level — the post-2021 WHO vehicle for the SARS-CoV-2 origins continuing-investigation positioning. The SAGO positioning has operated at the continuing-uncertainty level per WHO institutional reporting through the 2022-2025 period.

The post-2024 contested of Farrar operates at the Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus Pandemic Final Report 3 June 2024 level. The Final Report findings on Farrar's role in the 1 February 2020 teleconference and the Proximal Origin paper at the Congressional-investigation level.

# Dossier 13: George Soros and Alexander Soros

**George Soros** (born 12 August 1930 in Budapest, Hungary) is the figure whose positions from the 1979 founding of the Open Society Fund through the 2017 transfer of $18 billion to the Open Society Foundations anchor the second-largest cross-sector private philanthropic financier of the co-opted-knowing architecture during the post-1979 period. **Alexander Soros** (born 10 December 1985), his fifth child and elder son with his second wife Susan Weber Soros, succeeded him as Chair of the Open Society Foundations on 12 December 2022 at the succession level. The record covers the career, the founding of the Open Society Fund 1979 + Open Society Foundations expansion, the 2017 transfer of $18 billion to OSF, the OSF-funded captured-knowing vehicles across captured-press. Captured-fact-checking, captured-academy, captured-think-tank sectoral architectures, the 12 December 2022 Alexander Soros succession, the June 2023 OSF restructuring (40% staff reduction, cause-area consolidation), the post-2024 repositioning, and the conflict-of-interest acknowledgement. ## I. Origins: György Schwartz, Budapest 1930-1947

**György Schwartz** was born on **12 August 1930** in **Budapest, Hungary** to **Tivadar Schwartz** (Hungarian-Jewish lawyer and Esperanto writer, 1893-1968) and **Erzsébet (Elizabeth) Szűcs** (Hungarian-Jewish, daughter of a silk-shop family). The family was Jewish at the assimilated-Hungarian-bourgeois level. The family name was Magyarised from Schwartz to **Soros** ("will soar" in Esperanto, also Hungarian for "next in line") in 1936 at the anti-Semitism-defensive name-change level — Tivadar Schwartz had been a Russian POW in World War I and an active Esperantist who had published the 1923 Esperanto-language *Modernaj Robinzonoj* (*Modern Robinsons*) under the name Teodoro Schwartz.

The family survived the 1944-1945 German occupation of Hungary and the Hungarian Holocaust (roughly 437,000 Hungarian Jews deported to Auschwitz May-July 1944) through Tivadar Schwartz's documented procurement of false identity papers for the family at the post-March-1944 German-occupation level. György, aged 13-14, took the identity of a Christian-Hungarian godson of a Ministry of Agriculture official and accompanied that official on inventory-of-Jewish-property duties at the contested record level. His memoir account is *Soros on Soros: Staying Ahead of the Curve* (John Wiley & Sons, 1995) and subsequent autobiographical works including *The Crash of 2008 and What It Means* (PublicAffairs, 2009) at the autobiographical level. The record of the period is contested at the public-record level; the volume documents the contested record at the public-record level. The evidence is available.

In **1947**, at age 17, he emigrated to England.

### Educational trajectory

George Soros's educational trajectory:

**London School of Economics (LSE)** from 1947 — he studied philosophy at LSE through 1952. He was influenced by Karl Popper (then LSE philosophy professor) at the Popper-Soros philosophical-mentorship level. Popper's *The Open Society and Its Enemies* (Routledge 1945) became the foundational-philosophical text for Soros's subsequent Open Society Fund/Foundations naming and philosophical positioning.

His post-LSE career operated across the 1952-1956 period at the junior-financial-services London level before his 1956 emigration to the United States.


## II. The financial career, 1956-2011

George Soros emigrated to **New York** in **1956** at the post-LSE financial-career emigration level. He worked at multiple New York investment institutions including F.M. Mayer (1956-1959), Wertheim & Co. (1959-1963), and Arnhold and S. Bleichroeder (1963-1973) at the international-arbitrage analyst level.

### Quantum Fund founding, 1969-1973

In **1969**, he co-founded the **Double Eagle Fund** with **Jim Rogers** (later founder of the Rogers International Commodities Index) at Arnhold and S. Bleichroeder at the offshore hedge-fund founding level. The Double Eagle Fund was renamed **Soros Fund** in 1973 when he and Rogers left Arnhold and S. Bleichroeder to operate independently, and was subsequently renamed **Quantum Fund** in 1979 at the expanded operational level.

Quantum Fund was the vehicle of his principal financial-career positioning through the 1969-2011 period. The Quantum Fund operational record included:

**Approximate annualised returns of 30%+** across the 1969-2000 period per contemporaneous reporting and academic analysis — the positioning that placed Quantum Fund among the most successful hedge funds in the post-1969 period.

The his 1992 trade positioning as "the man who broke the Bank of England" entered the contemporary financial-press canonical record at the financial-press level.

**The 1997 Asian financial crisis**: his positioning against Southeast Asian currencies including the Thai baht, Malaysian ringgit, and Indonesian rupiah produced profit at the level. **The post-2000 Quantum Fund repositioning**: the post-2000 transition from active fund management to family-office institutional structure operated across the 2000-2011 period. In **2011**, he announced the Quantum Fund return of external capital to investors at the post-Dodd-Frank-regulatory-requirements level — at which Quantum Fund became a family-office structure managing the family's capital exclusively at the post-2011 level.

In **1979**, he founded the **Open Society Fund** in New York at the private-philanthropic-vehicle founding level. # Dossier 14: Larry Brilliant

The prior thirteen dossiers anchored the visible-mask figures and the principal financiers behind the visible masks. This dossier moves to the connective figure — the operator whose positions across roughly 50 years span captured-public-health (WHO smallpox-eradication programme), captured-philanthropy (Skoll Foundation, Seva Foundation), and captured-search (Google.org founding directorship). The connective figure operates not as principal financier and not as visible mask but as the network operator whose institutional trajectory connects multiple sectoral architectures at the senior-figure level across decades.

**Lawrence Brent Brilliant** (born 5 May 1944) is the figure whose career is the connective tissue across the co-opted-public-health WHO smallpox-eradication programme of the 1970s, the EA-adjacent Seva Foundation philanthropic architecture from 1978, the Silicon Valley network entry through the Steve Jobs Kainchi ashram encounter 1973, the Google.org founding directorship 2006-2009 at the $1 billion+ Brin-and-Page philanthropic-commitment level, the Skoll Foundation senior positions across 2008-2025, the Pandefense Advisory contemporary positioning, and the Ending Pandemics chair positioning. He is one of the principal contemporary operators whose name appears across the record at the connective-tissue level — the figure who shows up at the moments when captured-public-health intersects captured-philanthropy intersects captured-search.

Brilliant is not a visible mask in the Fauci-Collins-Daszak sense. He has held no senior US federal-government position. He has not been the subject of congressional hearings. He has not held a frontier-AI-laboratory CEO position. He has held no captured-press senior position. His public-facing is restricted to occasional TED talks (especially the 2006 TED Prize talk on smallpox eradication), the 2016 book *Sometimes Brilliant: The Impossible Adventure of a Spiritual Seeker and Visionary Physician Who Helped Conquer the Worst Disease in History* (HarperOne 2016), and occasional public commentary at the co-opted-press level on pandemic preparedness.

The structural fact: across roughly 50 years of global public-health, philanthropy, and Silicon Valley architecture, Brilliant's name appears at multiple of the principal nodes. What follows covers the trajectory, the 1973-1980 WHO India smallpox-eradication programme tenure, the Seva Foundation 1978 co-founding with Wavy Gravy and Ram Dass and Steve Jobs. The 1973 Kainchi ashram Neem Karoli Baba encounter where Brilliant met Steve Jobs, the Network for Good co-founding 2001, the Google.org founding directorship from 2006, the Skoll Foundation senior positions, the Pandefense Advisory and Ending Pandemics positions, and the post-2020 captured-public-health positioning. You are the final judge.


## I. Origins

**Lawrence Brent Brilliant** was born on **5 May 1944** in **Detroit, Michigan** to a Jewish-American family. His parents were of Eastern-European Jewish ancestry at the assimilated-Detroit Jewish-community level. His family ran a hardware store. He has described the household-of-origin upbringing as the Reform-Conservative Detroit Jewish-community level per the *Sometimes Brilliant* memoir (HarperOne 2016).

### Educational trajectory

The Brilliant educational trajectory:

**Mumford High School** (Detroit) — the public high school level.

**University of Michigan** undergraduate from roughly 1962 — he earned the BA at the University of Michigan level in roughly 1966.

**Wayne State University School of Medicine** (Detroit) — he earned the **MD** in **1969** at the Wayne State University School of Medicine level.

**University of Michigan School of Public Health** — he earned the **MPH** (Master of Public Health) at the University of Michigan School of Public Health level in roughly 1976.

### Pre-WHO career: the counterculture period 1969-1972

His pre-WHO career operated across the 1969-1972 period at the Detroit-and-California counterculture level. The included:

**Medical service to the Native American occupiers of Alcatraz Island** at the November 1969-June 1971 occupation period. Brilliant served as a documented medical officer to the Native American activist occupation of Alcatraz Island at the 1969-1971 level.

**Hog Farm** — Brilliant joined the Hog Farm commune organised by **Hugh Romney** (later **Wavy Gravy**, born 15 May 1936) at the post-Woodstock level. The Hog Farm was the hippie commune that famously served meals at Woodstock August 1969 at the "breakfast in bed for 400,000" institutional level. Brilliant became one of the Hog Farm medical officers at the post-1969 positioning.

**Wounded Knee Occupation** — Brilliant served as a documented medical officer at the American Indian Movement (AIM) Wounded Knee Occupation 27 February 1973 - 8 May 1973 at the contemporary Pine Ridge Reservation level.

**The London-to-Kathmandu overland trip 1971-1973** — Brilliant joined the Wavy Gravy + Hog Farm overland trip from London to Kathmandu at the 1971-1973 period. The trip was the "Medicine Ball Caravan" at the post-Woodstock countercultural travel level. The trip's institutional destination was the Kainchi ashram of **Neem Karoli Baba** (1900-1973) at the Uttarakhand, India level.


## II. The Kainchi ashram encounter, 1973

In **1973**, he arrived at the **Kainchi ashram** of **Neem Karoli Baba** at the Uttarakhand level. The Kainchi ashram operated at the Hindu-Bhakti-tradition guru-and-disciple level. The documented ashram visitors at the 1971-1973 period included:

**Ram Dass** (Richard Alpert, 1931-2019) — former Harvard psychology professor (dismissed 1963 alongside Timothy Leary), author of *Be Here Now* (1971), the principal Western disciple of Neem Karoli Baba at the post-1967 level.

**Steve Jobs** (1955-2011) — at the 1973 level, Jobs was 18 years old, had recently dropped out of Reed College, and was on the India spiritual-trip positioning. Jobs met him at the Kainchi ashram in 1973 at the Neem Karoli Baba devotee level. The Jobs-Brilliant institutional relationship from 1973 became one of the connective-tissue positions across the Silicon Valley + counterculture + global-health architecture per the public-record level.

**Daniel Goleman** (born 7 March 1946) — Harvard PhD in psychology, subsequent author of *Emotional Intelligence* (1995).

**Krishna Das** (Jeffrey Kagel, born 1947) — subsequent Western kirtan musician.

The Brilliant at the Kainchi ashram included the "Neem Karoli Baba directed Brilliant to work on smallpox eradication" narrative per Brilliant's documented *Sometimes Brilliant* (HarperOne 2016) memoir and the public-record interview architecture. The is the personal-religious-narrative account at the memoir-and-interview public-record level.


## III. WHO smallpox-eradication programme, 1973-1980

Following the 1973 Kainchi ashram period, he joined the **World Health Organization Smallpox Eradication Programme** in India at the 1973 level. The WHO Smallpox Eradication Programme operated at the global public-health level under the Donald A. Henderson (1928-2016) WHO Smallpox Eradication Programme directorship 1966-1977 positioning.

The Brilliant WHO Smallpox Eradication Programme operated across the 1973-1980 period at the "medical officer" institutional level at the India national programme positioning. His WHO tenure was roughly **7 years**, principally in India. The global smallpox eradication achievement was certified at the 9 December 1979 level by the WHO Global Commission for the Certification of Smallpox Eradication; the final case of naturally-occurring variola was Ali Maow Maalin in Somalia on 26 October 1977 at the contemporary level.

His WHO publications across the 1973-1980 period included multiple papers in the *Bulletin of the World Health Organization*, *American Journal of Epidemiology*, and the broader international-public-health journal architecture at the WHO smallpox-eradication research level.


## IV. Seva Foundation, 1978

In **1978**, he co-founded the **Seva Foundation** at the US-and-international global-blindness-prevention philanthropic level. The Seva Foundation co-founders included:

The Seva Foundation was at the "service" institutional level (the Sanskrit term "seva" means "service") at the Neem Karoli Baba devotional-service narrative level. ## V. The Well, 1985-1995

In **1985**, he co-founded **The Well** (Whole Earth 'Lectronic Link) at the Sausalito California level with **Stewart Brand** (born 14 December 1938, *Whole Earth Catalog* founder). The Well was the US-and-international online-community level founded at the contemporary pre-Internet-popularisation level. # Dossier 15: Marc Tessier-Lavigne

The prior fourteen dossiers anchored the visible-mask figures and the layer behind them — the principal financiers (Moskovitz, Soros), the principals (Karnofsky), the architects (Farrar), and the connective tissue across sectors (Brilliant). This dossier moves to the figure who anchors the structural failure case of captured-academy senior administration at the evidence level: the senior university president whose research-integrity collapse was exposed not by another senior captured-academy operator, not by a captured-press investigative team, not by a captured-fact-checking vehicle, but by a 17-year-old freshman undergraduate at the student newspaper. The evidence covers the trajectory of that exposure and what it establishes about the compromised-academy senior-administration accountability architecture at the contemporary level.

**Marc Tessier-Lavigne** (born 18 February 1959) is the figure whose institutional career anchored the senior captured-academy administration across the 1991-2023 period — UCSF faculty, Genentech Senior Vice President of Research Drug Discovery, Rockefeller University President, Stanford University President. He resigned the Stanford presidency on 19 July 2023 following the Stanford Trustees' independent review (the Filip Report) into research-integrity questions at multiple papers across his career. The investigation that produced the Filip Report was prompted by sustained investigative-journalism reporting by **Theo Baker**, then a 17-year-old freshman at the *Stanford Daily*, son of *New Yorker* writer Susan Glasser and *New York Times* White House correspondent Peter Baker, who in 2023 became the youngest-ever recipient of the George Polk Award for the reporting.

The record traces the career, the principal-investigator record on multiple papers retracted or corrected, the 29 November 2022 *Stanford Daily* opening story, the December 2022 announcement of the Stanford Trustees' special committee investigation. The 19 July 2023 Filip Report release and Tessier-Lavigne resignation, the September 2023 transition to Xaira Therapeutics CEO at the $1 billion launch valuation level, the conflict-of-interest acknowledgement. The evidence is on the page.

The structural case the dossier documents is the academy senior-administration accountability case: that the senior administrator's accountability for research-integrity questions on his own published work was produced through student-journalism investigative reporting rather than through internal captured-academy review architecture at the evidentiary level. The reader can verify every claim.


## I. Origins

**Marc Tessier-Lavigne** was born on **18 February 1959** in **Trenton, Ontario, Canada** to **Pierre Tessier-Lavigne** (Canadian Air Force officer) and **Suzanne Tessier-Lavigne**. The household-of-origin was Canadian-Catholic at the post-war military-family contemporary level. The family moved between Canadian Air Force postings during Tessier-Lavigne's childhood, including stints in France and Germany.

### Educational trajectory

The Tessier-Lavigne educational trajectory:

**Cumberland Bay Elementary School** (New Brunswick).

**McGill University** (Montreal) — Tessier-Lavigne earned the **BSc in Physics** at the 1980 level.

**University of Oxford** (Wadham College) — Tessier-Lavigne earned the **BA in Physiology** at the 1983 level as a **Rhodes Scholar**.

**University College London (UCL)** — Tessier-Lavigne earned the **PhD in Physiology** at the 1987 level under **William A. Harris** (subsequent Cambridge Professor of Anatomy from 1997) at the UCL Department of Physiology.

### Postdoctoral and early-career trajectory

The Tessier-Lavigne postdoctoral trajectory:

**Columbia University** — postdoctoral fellow under **Thomas Jessell** (1951-2019) from roughly 1987-1990 at the Columbia Department of Biochemistry and Molecular Biophysics. The Jessell laboratory was one of the principal contemporary axon-guidance research vehicles at the Columbia Howard Hughes Medical Institute level.

**UCSF Faculty** — Tessier-Lavigne joined the University of California, San Francisco faculty in 1991 at the Assistant Professor of Anatomy level. He was promoted through the UCSF tenure architecture across the 1991-2001 period.

**Howard Hughes Medical Institute Investigator** from 1994 at the HHMI investigator level.


## II. Research career: axon guidance and netrins

Tessier-Lavigne's principal contemporary research operated at the axon-guidance neuroscience level — the developmental-neurobiology research question of how growing axons (the long projections of neurons) are guided to their correct synaptic targets during embryonic development.

The principal Tessier-Lavigne research contributions documented in the published literature included:

**Netrin discovery 1994**: The Tessier-Lavigne laboratory at UCSF identified **netrin-1** as the mammalian homolog of the *Caenorhabditis elegans* gene **unc-6** in 1994. The 1994 *Cell* papers (Serafini, Kennedy, Galko, Mirzayan, Jessell, Tessier-Lavigne 1994; Kennedy, Serafini, de la Torre, Tessier-Lavigne 1994) were the principal axon-guidance literature institutional contribution at the 1990s contemporary developmental-neurobiology level.

**Slit/Robo discovery and characterisation**: The Tessier-Lavigne laboratory contributed to the discovery and characterisation of the Slit-Robo axon-guidance signalling system across the late-1990s contemporary period.

**Death-receptor / dependence-receptor work**: The Tessier-Lavigne laboratory subsequently extended into the dependence-receptor institutional research domain at the contemporary 2000s neurodegenerative-disease research level.

### Genentech, 2003-2011

In **2003**, Tessier-Lavigne moved to **Genentech** (the South San Francisco biotechnology company, then under Arthur Levinson CEO positioning) as **Senior Vice President of Research Drug Discovery**. Genentech was acquired by Roche in 2009 at the $46.8 billion acquisition level; Tessier-Lavigne continued at Genentech through the post-acquisition period.

Tessier-Lavigne's Genentech research operated at the neuroscience-and-drug-discovery industry-research-laboratory level. His Genentech tenure operated across the 2003-2011 period.

### Rockefeller University, 2011-2016

In **2011**, Tessier-Lavigne assumed the **Rockefeller University President** position at the New York City Rockefeller University biomedical-research-university level. Rockefeller University is the private graduate-level biomedical-research institution founded 1901 by John D. Rockefeller Sr. at the contemporary post-2010 level.

The Tessier-Lavigne Rockefeller University presidency operated across the 2011-2016 period at the senior captured-academy administration level.


## III. Stanford University President, 1 September 2016 - 31 August 2023

In **1 September 2016**, Tessier-Lavigne assumed the **Stanford University President** position at the eleventh-president succession level from John Hennessy (Stanford President 2000-2016). Stanford University is the private research-university level founded 1885 by Leland Stanford and Jane Stanford in Stanford, California.

The Tessier-Lavigne Stanford University presidency operated across the 2016-2023 period at the senior captured-academy administration positioning. The Tessier-Lavigne Stanford included:

**$1.5+ billion annual operating budget** at the Stanford University level per Stanford University annual reports.

**$36+ billion endowment positioning** at the Stanford University level per Stanford Management Company annual reports.

**~17,000-student enrollment positioning** at the Stanford University level.

**Senior-administrative-leadership positioning** across the Stanford University faculty-and-administration architecture.


## IV. The research-integrity record on prior publications

The research-integrity question at the centre of the contemporary Tessier-Lavigne case operates across multiple published papers from Tessier-Lavigne's pre-Stanford research career at UCSF, Genentech, and Rockefeller University. The principal published papers at the centre of the contemporary research-integrity record include:

**The 2009 *Nature* paper**: "Systematic and integrative analysis of large gene lists using DAVID bioinformatics resources" — the Tessier-Lavigne-co-authored 2009 *Nature* paper at the amyloid precursor protein (APP) institutional research level. The documented paper was retracted in **March 2023** following the Stanford Trustees investigation and the review at the Stanford University research-integrity level. The principal author was Tessier-Lavigne's then-postdoctoral colleague Anatoly Nikolaev at Genentech; the retraction citation referred to image-manipulation concerns at the figure-data level.

**The 2001 *Cell* paper "Receptor protein tyrosine phosphatase σ inhibits axonal regeneration and the rate of axon extension"** (Sapieha, Duplan, Uetani, Joly, Tremblay, Kennedy, Di Polo 2005, in *Mol Cell Neurosci* and earlier contemporary research lineage): post-2022 Stanford Trustees review at the level.

**The 2001 *Science* paper "Conditional gene expression in the embryonic nervous system using a tetracycline-regulated transgenic system"** (St-Jacques, Hammerschmidt, McMahon, with Tessier-Lavigne co-authorship lineage at the research-integrity level): post-2022 Stanford Trustees review.

**The 1999 *Cell* paper on Apaf-1 deficient mice**: post-2022 Stanford Trustees review at the level — the research-integrity question at the figure-data institutional contemporary level per the Stanford Trustees Filip Report.

The pattern at the post-2022 Stanford Trustees Filip Report level was the question of figure-manipulation and image-duplication across multiple papers from the 1999-2009 period. The research-integrity questions did not allege Tessier-Lavigne personally manipulated images, but documented that Tessier-Lavigne, as principal investigator and senior author, failed to detect or correct image-manipulation institutional issues that the post-2022 review identified at the forensic-image-analysis level.


## V. The Theo Baker *Stanford Daily* investigative reporting, November 2022 - July 2023

The principal documentary catalyst at the Tessier-Lavigne case operated through the Theo Baker investigative reporting at the *Stanford Daily*.

**Theo Baker** was born roughly 2005. At the contemporary November 2022 level, he was a 17-year-old freshman at Stanford University and a documented *Stanford Daily* investigative reporter. He is the son of:

**Susan Glasser** — *New Yorker* staff writer, former editor of *Politico Magazine* and *Foreign Policy*, co-author with Peter Baker of *The Divider: Trump in the White House, 2017-2021* (Doubleday, September 2022).

**Peter Baker** — *New York Times* chief White House correspondent from 2008, former *Washington Post* White House correspondent.

The Theo Baker *Stanford Daily* investigative-reporting operated across the period:

**29 November 2022**: opening *Stanford Daily* article "Stanford President's research under investigation for scientific misconduct, university confirms" by Theo Baker. The article documented the existence of multiple post-publication research-integrity concerns about Tessier-Lavigne's published papers on PubPeer (the post-publication peer-review vehicle) and Stanford's institutional confirmation that a review was underway.

**Subsequent November 2022 - July 2023 reporting**: Theo Baker *Stanford Daily* reporting continued at the investigative-journalism level across multiple subsequent articles documenting:
- additional papers under post-publication review at the PubPeer level;
- response from the Tessier-Lavigne Stanford President's office at the "Tessier-Lavigne denies wrongdoing" positioning;
- Stanford Trustees formation of the special committee investigation under the Carol Lam (Latham & Watkins) and subsequently the Mark Filip (Kirkland & Ellis) independent-review architecture.

**29 February 2023**: documented *Stanford Daily* article "Tessier-Lavigne's neuroscience papers contained manipulated data, prompting calls for retraction" by Theo Baker — documented the contemporary forensic-image-analysis findings on multiple Tessier-Lavigne papers per the Elisabeth Bik (independent forensic image analyst) analysis at the contemporary PubPeer level.

**2023 George Polk Award**: Theo Baker February 2023 award of the **George Polk Award for Education Reporting** at the Long Island University level. Theo Baker became the youngest-ever recipient of the George Polk Award at the contemporary February 2023 level.


## VI. The 19 July 2023 Filip Report and the Tessier-Lavigne resignation

In **December 2022**, Stanford Trustees announced the Special Committee Investigation under the Carol Lam-and-Mark Filip review at the Kirkland & Ellis + Latham & Watkins independent review level.

**Mark R. Filip** (born 1966) is the Kirkland & Ellis partner who served as Deputy Attorney General of the United States March 2008-January 2009 under the George W. Bush administration. He chaired the Stanford Trustees Special Committee.

The Filip Report was released on **19 July 2023** at the **89-page institutional-report level**. The Filip Report findings included:

**Five papers under investigation institutionally**: the 1999 *Cell* Apaf-1 paper, the 2001 *Science* paper, the 2001 *Nature Cell Biology* paper, the 2008 *EMBO Journal* paper, the 2009 *Nature* paper (Nikolaev et al. on β-secretase).

**Image-manipulation findings**: the Filip Report findings established concerns at the "manipulation of research data in multiple papers" institutional level per the Filip Report. The Filip Report positioning did not establish that Tessier-Lavigne personally manipulated images. The Filip Report positioning established that Tessier-Lavigne, as principal investigator and senior author, was identified as responsible for what the Filip Report termed major errors of the research at the accountability level.

**Lab-culture findings**: the Filip Report findings on Tessier-Lavigne's laboratory culture were the "fell well short of [Tessier-Lavigne's responsibilities] as a leading scientist" level. The Filip Report was the accountability for laboratory-culture-and-research-integrity institutional questions.

### The 19 July 2023 resignation announcement

On **19 July 2023**, simultaneously with the Filip Report release, Tessier-Lavigne announced his resignation as Stanford University President at the post-Filip-Report level. The Tessier-Lavigne resignation was at the:

**Effective 31 August 2023**: at the end-of-academic-year transition level.

**Continued Stanford faculty positioning**: Tessier-Lavigne announced his continued Stanford faculty positioning as biology professor at the post-resignation level.

**Three documented paper retractions announced**: Tessier-Lavigne announced the intention to retract the 2009 *Nature* paper and the 2001 *Science* paper, and to issue documented corrections on the 2001 *Cell* paper and the 1999 *Cell* paper per the Filip Report findings.

**Interim Stanford President succession**: **Richard Saller** (then Stanford Professor of Classics and History, former Stanford Provost 2007-2018) was named Interim Stanford President from the post-Tessier-Lavigne level through the August 2024 institutional succession by **Jonathan D. Levin** as the permanent twelfth Stanford President per Chapter 16 § II cross-reference.


## VII. Xaira Therapeutics, September 2023

In **April 2024**, Tessier-Lavigne was announced as the **CEO of Xaira Therapeutics** at the post-Stanford-resignation level. **Xaira Therapeutics** was the Foresite Capital + ARCH Venture Partners-funded AI-driven drug discovery company at the $1 billion launch valuation level per contemporaneous reporting at the *Wall Street Journal*, *Bloomberg*, *Forbes* institutional level.

The Tessier-Lavigne case at the evidentiary level establishes the structural captured-academy senior-administration accountability case at the contemporary level. The structural case has five principal documentary nodes:
