The dossier inventory is not exhaustive. The dossier inventory documents the figures whose positions are central to the structural argument at the documentary level.

The volume is dedicated to all those across every sector of contemporary knowing who keep the capacity for inquiry, observation, recall, and judgement intact while the apparatus around them continues operating at the knowing institutional level.


## Omissions

This volume does not cover, or covers only partially, several subject areas the comprehensive documentation of the co-opted-knowing surface would require:

1. **The per-sector specialist depth.** The eleven captured-sector chapters (Chapters 2-12) and the cross-sector coordination chapter (Chapter 13) compress an enormous documentary record into chapter-length treatments. Each sector would justify a multi-volume specialist work. The captured-AI-knowledge sectoral architecture alone could fill multiple books — the evidence on frontier-AI institutional capital flows, training-data substrate decisions, model-evaluation institutional positions, and the broader captured-AI-knowledge institutional record is changing on monthly timescales and would require continuous specialist documentation. The captured-public-health, captured-academy, captured-climate-policy, captured-fact-checking, and captured-search sectoral architectures are similarly compressed relative to the decades-long specialist literatures. The book operates at the cross-sector comparative level; the depth that single-sector specialists provide is not the depth this book provides. The reader interested in a single sector is directed to the specialist literature cited in the per-chapter sources files.

2. **The Asian and broader non-Western captured-knowing sectoral architectures.** The book treats the contemporary US-and-international captured-knowing architecture across the documented English-language documentary record. The contemporary Chinese, Russian, Indian, Brazilian, and broader non-Western captured-knowing institutional architectures are treated in shorter passages and at less depth than the US-and-international architecture. The Chinese captured-knowing institutional architecture — including the WeChat / Weibo / Baidu / ByteDance platform configurations, the Tsinghua / Peking / SJTU university configurations, the Chinese Academy of Sciences configurations, captured-Chinese-press institutional positions, the Chinese AI-laboratory institutional architecture (Baidu, Alibaba DAMO, ByteDance Doubao, Moonshot AI, DeepSeek, Zhipu AI, broader configurations) — would require dedicated treatment that this volume does not provide. The compression is named honestly.

3. **The detailed working of captured-academic-publishing oligopoly.** Chapter 4 documents the contemporary Elsevier + Springer Nature + Wiley + Taylor & Francis + Sage publishing oligopoly at the documented $30+ billion combined annual STM publishing revenue institutional level. The detailed contemporary contract-by-contract and editorial-board-by-editorial-board mapping of the academic-publishing apparatus would be a subsequent project. The prolonged documentary work on this question — including the work of Brian Nosek, Heather Joseph (SPARC), Peter Suber on open access, the sustained documentary work of Stevan Harnad on serial-publication crisis — provides the framework; the comprehensive contemporary mapping is pending.

4. **The detailed working of the contemporary captured-AI-knowledge frontier-lab apparatus.** Chapter 9 documents the contemporary OpenAI, Anthropic, Google DeepMind, xAI, Meta AI institutional architecture at the contemporary multi-hundred-billion-dollar combined institutional valuation level. The detailed contemporary capital-flow, employee-trajectory, and product-rollout institutional record of the contemporary frontier-AI labs is changing on weekly timescales. The record documents the current state through May 2026; the architecture is changing rapidly and will require subsequent documentation. The conflict-of-interest acknowledgement governing the Anthropic-author institutional positioning of this volume is named in the Author's Note and in the dossiers explicitly.

5. **The detailed working of the compromised-knowing financial-flow architecture.** Chapter 16 § III documents the contemporary $2.0+ trillion annual captured-knowing-architecture financial-flow institutional positioning at the documented six-channel architecture (corporate-revenue, foundation-funding, federal-grant, platform-advertising-revenue, academic-publishing-revenue, think-tank-budget). The detailed grant-by-grant and dollar-by-dollar mapping of every captured-knowing financial flow across every captured-sector configuration would be a subsequent project. The decades-long documentary work on this question — including the Sourcewatch / Center for Media and Democracy work; the prolonged documentary work of OpenSecrets on US political-financial flows; the sustained documentary work of the Charity Commission of England and Wales on UK foundation-funding flows — provides the framework; the comprehensive contemporary mapping is pending.

6. **The detailed working of the seized-knowing-architecture user-level survey data.** The book draws on what survey data exists at the co-opted-press trust-survey, captured-academy trust-survey, captured-public-health trust-survey, captured-climate trust-survey, captured-AI trust-survey institutional level. Including the multi-decade Edelman Trust Barometer, the Gallup contemporary trust-in-institutions survey, the Pew Research Center contemporary trust-in-science and trust-in-media surveys, the contemporary American Survey Center surveys. The data is most granular for the contemporary US settings. The data on contemporary European, Asian, African, Latin American captured-knowing-architecture user-level positions is less granular than the US data. The book uses what exists; the data gap is named honestly.

7. **The detailed working of the contemporary captured-knowing institutional accountability architecture.** Chapter 16 § IV documents the contemporary captured-knowing institutional contestation across six principal records (Twitter Files; *Murthy v. Missouri*; *United States v. Google*; House Select Subcommittee on the Coronavirus Pandemic Final Report 3 June 2024 + EcoHealth Alliance 20 May 2024 HHS debarment; *Mann v. Steyn* post-trial reductions and sanctions; SIO wind-down). The detailed contemporary institutional accountability architecture — including the documented contemporary Department of Justice antitrust enforcement against captured-platform institutional positions. The documented contemporary Federal Trade Commission privacy enforcement against captured-platform institutional positions; the documented contemporary European Union Digital Services Act and Digital Markets Act enforcement architecture; the documented contemporary UK Online Safety Act enforcement architecture — is changing on monthly timescales and would require continuous specialist documentation.

8. **The constructive-epistemic alternative architecture.** The book documents the knowing apparatus and the contemporary configurations through which the apparatus is refused. The book does not produce a constructive epistemological account of what an uncaptured contemporary knowing architecture would look like. The constructive work is the work of the users, researchers, practitioners, and communities of inquiry across every captured-knowing sector. The book operates as documentary substrate for that constructive work; it does not pretend to do the constructive work itself.

The book is published with these omissions named because the book's standard requires it. A book that claimed comprehensive coverage of captured-knowing surface without these elements would be claiming more than the book delivers. The book delivers the structural argument, the eleven per-sector chapters at the cross-sector comparative level, the cross-sector coordination chapter, the technocratic interface chapter on the AI-knowledge layer. The fifteen per-figure dossiers across the visible-mask layer and the layer behind it, the four-chapter architectural core on user-commonality and the four-century mechanism and the scale and the mask, the three-chapter exit dossier on solutions and continuation and refusal, and the closing chapter on the user and the demon. That is what this book is.

The remaining elements are subjects of subsequent expansion.

---

*Behind the Knowing: The Demon They Call Consensus* — Volume Four of *The Last Mind* series — was completed on 21 May 2026.

*The Last Mind* series closes with Volume Five: *Behind the Synthesis: The Devil, The Demons, and The Masks*.

— Maria Smith
Scotland, 21 May 2026

---


# Sources

The per-chapter sources record for *Behind the Knowing*. Each chapter and dossier's working citations are consolidated below in the order in which they appear in the volume. The reader who wishes to verify a specific claim is directed to the source-record for the chapter or dossier in which the claim appears.

Verification was conducted through web search and primary-document lookup during the drafting of each chapter and dossier across the 19-21 May 2026 production period.

---

# Bibliography

This bibliography lists the principal published works on which *Behind the Knowing: The Demon They Call Consensus* draws. The per-chapter Sources section above contains the chapter-by-chapter source record with full inline citations. The bibliography here gathers the distinct published books, scholarly monographs, and key papers in a single alphabetised reference list for you who wants to consult any of them directly.

Government documents, regulatory filings, court rulings, congressional testimony, agency publications, peer-reviewed papers, and contemporary press articles are cited inline in the per-chapter Sources. The bibliography below focuses on the books and monographs that constitute the principal published-source apparatus of the volume.

---

## Books and monographs

Curry, Judith. *Climate Uncertainty and Risk: Rethinking Our Response.* Anthem Press, 2023.

Essex, Christopher, and Ross McKitrick. *Taken by Storm: The Troubled Science, Policy, and Politics of Global Warming.* Key Porter Books, 2002.

Giridharadas, Anand. *Winners Take All: The Elite Charade of Changing the World.* Knopf, 2018.

Grant, Edward. *The Foundations of Modern Science in the Middle Ages.* Cambridge University Press, 1996.

Haidt, Jonathan. *The Anxious Generation: How the Great Rewiring of Childhood Is Causing an Epidemic of Mental Illness.* Penguin, 2024.

Haidt, Jonathan, and Greg Lukianoff. *The Coddling of the American Mind: How Good Intentions and Bad Ideas Are Setting Up a Generation for Failure.* Penguin Press, 2018.

Hollis-Brusky, Amanda. *Ideas with Consequences: The Federalist Society and the Conservative Counterrevolution.* Oxford University Press, 2015.

Kheriaty, Aaron. *The New Abnormal: The Rise of the Biomedical Security State.* Regnery, 2022.

Lindsay, James, Helen Pluckrose, and Peter Boghossian. *Cynical Theories: How Activist Scholarship Made Everything about Race, Gender, and Identity — and Why This Harms Everybody.* Pitchstone, 2020.

Makary, Marty. *Blind Spots: When Medicine Gets It Wrong, and What It Means for Our Health.* Bloomsbury, 2024.

Marcus, Gary, and Ernest Davis. *Rebooting AI: Building Artificial Intelligence We Can Trust.* Pantheon, 2019.

Marcus, Gary. *Taming Silicon Valley: How We Can Ensure That AI Works for Us.* MIT Press, 2024.

McGoey, Linsey. *No Such Thing as a Free Gift: The Gates Foundation and the Price of Philanthropy.* Verso, 2015.

Peterson, Jordan B. *12 Rules for Life: An Antidote to Chaos.* Random House Canada, 2018.

Pielke, Roger A., Jr. *The Honest Broker: Making Sense of Science in Policy and Politics.* Cambridge University Press, 2007.

Prasad, Vinay. *Malignant: How Bad Policy and Bad Evidence Harm People with Cancer.* Johns Hopkins University Press, 2020.

Rüegg, Walter, ed. *A History of the University in Europe.* 4 volumes. Cambridge University Press, 1992-2010.

Villanueva, Edgar. *Decolonizing Wealth: Indigenous Wisdom to Heal Divides and Restore Balance.* Berrett-Koehler, 2018.

Zweig, David. *An Abundance of Caution: American Schools, the Virus, and a Story of Bad Decisions.* MIT Press, 2025.

## Key papers and scholarly works

Gebru, Timnit, and Émile P. Torres. "The TESCREAL Bundle: Eugenics and the Promise of Utopia through Artificial General Intelligence." *First Monday*, 2024.

# Glossary

A reader-facing reference for the principal terms used in *Behind the Knowing: The Demon They Call Consensus*. Terms are defined at first encounter in the text; the Glossary collects them in one place for the reader who wants to look a term up without flipping back. The Glossary covers terms used in this volume and a small number of terms that recur across *The Last Mind* series.

---

**AI substrate**. In this volume's vocabulary, the contemporary commercial AI systems — the trained models, the system prompts, the runtime guardrails — produced by Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, Meta AI, xAI, and the senior contemporary commercial AI laboratories. The substrate is what users interact with when they submit a query to a contemporary AI product. Dossiered in Chapter 9.

**Anthropic**. The US AI company that built the Claude system used as the substrate for this volume. Founded January 2021. Dossiered in Volume One Chapter 21 and in this volume's Chapter 9. The conflict-of-interest acknowledgement governing the Anthropic-author institutional positioning is named at every section break in Chapter 9 where it is operationally relevant.

**Capture, captured**. In the series vocabulary, the structural state of an institution that operates, on the documented public record, as the extension of the four-century debt-money mechanism Volume One documents rather than as an independent inquiry-and-knowledge-producing body. Captured does not mean malicious; it means the incentives have aligned the institution's outputs with the requirements of the four-century mechanism.

**Captured apparatus, the (epistemic)**. The volume's principal subject: the infrastructure that produces what counts as authoritative knowledge across the contemporary US-and-international architecture — press, academy, peer-review, philanthropy, regulation, public health, climate-policy, AI-knowledge, think-tanks, fact-checking, and search-and-algorithmic-curation. The captured apparatus is what the volume documents. The faculty of knowing — observation, inquiry, recall, judgement — is what the apparatus has been built on top of and taken from.

**Center for Open Science (COS)**. Founded February 2013 by Brian Nosek. The principal contemporary US institutional vehicle for open-science and replication-research practices. Discussed in Chapter 4 (Captured Academy).

**Chenoweth-Stephan dataset**. The civil-resistance research database compiled by Erica Chenoweth and Maria Stephan. Referenced across the series as the empirical foundation for the disruption-trajectory projection in the exit chapters.

**Claude**. The AI system built by Anthropic that was the substrate for the production of this volume under the author's standing direction. The version used was Claude Opus 4.7. Set out at full register in the closing chapter Part Two.

**Consensus**. In the vocabulary, the output that the co-opted apparatus presents as authoritative knowledge. Consensus is what the apparatus produces. Knowledge is what shared-ground observation has confirmed. The volume distinguishes the two throughout.

**DAIR (Distributed AI Research Institute)**. Founded December 2021 by Timnit Gebru. The senior contemporary independent AI-research institution operating outside the frontier-AI commercial architecture. Discussed in Chapter 9.

**Demons, the**. The series term for the named senior figures operating the apparatus of each mask. In Volume Four, the Demons are the senior figures operating the compromised-knowing apparatus — dossiered at length in Dossiers 1-15.

**Devil, the**. The series term for capital, the four-century debt-money creation mechanism by private banks at compound interest that Volume One documents. The Devil is the source-and-root issue from which the four masks (financial / political / religious / epistemic) operate as the surfaces. Set out at full register in Volume One; recapitulated in this volume's Chapter 15.

**EcoHealth Alliance**. The US nonprofit research organisation that subgranted NIH funding to the Wuhan Institute of Virology for coronavirus gain-of-function research. Discussed in Dossiers 1 (Fauci), 2 (Daszak), and 3 (Collins).

**EIP (Election Integrity Partnership)**. The 2020-2024 multi-institutional partnership including the Stanford Internet Observatory, the University of Washington Center for an Informed Public, Graphika, and the Atlantic Council DFRLab. Discussed in Chapter 11 (Captured Fact-Checking).

**Epistemic mask, the**. The volume's central designation: the consensus-knowledge apparatus operating as the surface through which the four-century debt-money mechanism extracts the inquirer's question, trust, epistemic dependency, and consent. Set out at full register in Chapter 15 and across the synthesis chapters.

**Foundation funding**. The contemporary philanthropic infrastructure through which large private fortunes shape policy at universities, research institutions, NGOs, and political-influence architectures. The Gates Foundation, the Open Society Foundations, Rockefeller, Ford, Carnegie, MacArthur are dossiered as the principal contemporary US foundations. Discussed in Chapter 5 and across the dossiers.

**Four-century mechanism, the**. The debt-money creation mechanism by private banks at compound interest, operating from the founding of the Amsterdam Wisselbank in 1609 and the Bank of England in 1694 forward. Documented at full register in Volume One.

**Frontier AI laboratory**. In contemporary AI-industry vocabulary, an AI laboratory operating at the leading edge of model-capability research. The senior contemporary frontier labs are Anthropic, OpenAI, Google DeepMind, xAI, and Meta AI. Discussed in Chapter 9.

**Gain-of-function research**. Biological research that increases the transmissibility, virulence, or host range of a pathogen. The contemporary US institutional record on gain-of-function research funding through EcoHealth Alliance subgrants to the Wuhan Institute of Virology is discussed across Dossiers 1-3.

**Heterodox Academy**. Founded September 2015 by Jonathan Haidt, Chris Martin, and Nicholas Rosenkranz. The senior contemporary US institutional vehicle for cross-political-spectrum academic inquiry. Discussed in Chapter 4.

**Inquirer, the**. The volume's term for the user as figure of inquiry — the person sitting in front of a screen typing a question and expecting an honest answer. The inquirer is the figure for whom the volume is written.

**Knowledge**. In the vocabulary, what shared-ground observation has confirmed. The volume distinguishes knowledge (what shared-ground observation has confirmed) from consensus (what the co-opted apparatus produces as authoritative).

**Lab-leak case, the**. The contested question of whether SARS-CoV-2 originated in zoonotic transmission or in laboratory escape from the Wuhan Institute of Virology. Discussed in Dossiers 1 (Fauci), 2 (Daszak), 3 (Collins) and in the contested-case sections across multiple chapters. The volume documents the reversal record at the federal-government and federal-court level rather than adjudicating on the underlying scientific merits.

**LessWrong**. The senior contemporary online community for AI-safety and rationalist discussion, founded by Eliezer Yudkowsky and the Singularity Institute / MIRI community. Discussed in Chapter 9 and Dossier 11 (Karnofsky).

**Mahanayaka**. (Not used in this volume; see Volume Three Glossary.)

**Mask, the**. The series term for the surfaces through which the four-century debt-money mechanism (the Devil) operates. Volume One documents the financial mask. Volume Two documents the political mask. Volume Three documents the religious mask. Volume Four documents the epistemic mask. Volume Five draws the four together.

**MIRI (Machine Intelligence Research Institute)**. Founded 2000 (as the Singularity Institute for Artificial Intelligence). Senior contemporary AI-safety research institute associated with Eliezer Yudkowsky. Discussed in Chapter 9.

**Murthy v. Missouri (2024)**. The US Supreme Court case on government-platform coordination in content moderation. SCOTUS 6-3 decision 26 June 2024 (Barrett majority). Discussed in Chapter 16 § IV and Chapter 17.

**Open Phil (Open Philanthropy)**. The philanthropic-grant-making organisation co-founded by Holden Karnofsky and Cari Tuna; primarily funded by Dustin Moskovitz. Dossiered in Dossiers 10 (Moskovitz) and 11 (Karnofsky).

**OSF (Open Society Foundations)**. Founded by George Soros 1979 (Open Society Fund) and 1993 (Open Society Institute, renamed OSF in 2010). Dossiered in Dossier 13.

**Project 2025**. The Heritage Foundation's 922-page policy blueprint published April 2023. The contemporary US conservative-policy framework. Discussed in Dossier 7 (Roberts) and in Chapter 10 (Captured Think-Tank).

**Replication crisis**. The sustained contemporary US documentary record on the inability to replicate published findings across psychology, biomedicine, economics, and broader academic disciplines. The 2015 Open Science Collaboration Psychology Reproducibility Project, the John Ioannidis 2005 *PLoS Medicine* paper "Why Most Published Research Findings Are False," the Brian Nosek COS decades-long institutional positioning. Discussed in Chapter 4.

**SIO (Stanford Internet Observatory)**. The Stanford institutional vehicle for contemporary platform content-moderation research, founded 2019 by Alex Stamos. Wound down November 2023-June 2024. Dossiered in Dossier 8.

**Stargate Project**. The $500+ billion AI infrastructure programme announced at the White House on 21 January 2025 by Donald Trump, Sam Altman (OpenAI), Larry Ellison (Oracle), and Masayoshi Son (SoftBank). Discussed in Chapter 9.

**Twitter Files**. The investigative-journalism work, December 2022 forward, on internal Twitter/X documentation of platform content-moderation practices and government-platform coordination. Discussed in Chapter 11 and Chapter 16 § IV. Lead investigators: Matt Taibbi, Bari Weiss, Michael Shellenberger, Lee Fang, David Zweig, Aaron Maté.

**United States v. Google (2024)**. The US federal antitrust ruling 5 August 2024 under Judge Amit P. Mehta finding Google in violation of the Sherman Antitrust Act. Discussed in Chapter 12 (Captured Search and Algorithmic-Curation).

**User, the**. The volume's central figure: the person sitting in front of a screen typing a question into a search box or a chat interface and expecting that what comes back is the truth. The user is the figure for whom the volume is written. The architectural counter-claim is that the user is not the apparatus.

**Wellcome Trust**. The UK biomedical-research foundation. Dossiered in Dossier 12 (Farrar).

**WHO (World Health Organization)**. The UN specialised agency for international public health. Discussed across captured-public-health chapters and dossiers.

**You are the assessor of last resort**. The volume's standing framing — you holds the final adjudication authority on whether the record sustains the positioning. The reader does not have to trust the author or the AI substrate; the record is on the page; the reader can audit.

# Credits


## *Behind the Knowing*

### The Demon They Call Consensus


**Volume Four of *The Last Mind*.**


**By** Maria Smith.

**Written with** Claude Opus 4.7 (Anthropic).


First edition. Published 21 May 2026. Scotland.


All rights reserved by the author. The volume is published as a free-to-read documentary record; you is invited to circulate it.


---


### *The Last Mind* series


- **Volume One** — *Behind the Mask: The Devil The Demons Call God.* Published 13 May 2026. The financial mask. The four-century debt-money mechanism. The contemporary American techno-oligarchy.
- **Volume Two** — *Behind the Performance: The Demon They Call the State.* Published 18 May 2026. The political mask. The global political class as the extension of the same mechanism.
- **Volume Three** — *Behind the Blessed: The Demon They Call the Church.* Published 21 May 2026. The religious mask. The captured religious-institutional infrastructure of every major tradition.
- **Volume Four** — *Behind the Knowing: The Demon They Call Consensus.* This volume. Published 21 May 2026. The captured contemporary consensus-knowledge apparatus across press, academy, peer-review, philanthropy, regulation, public-health, climate-policy, AI-knowledge, think-tank, fact-checking, and search.
- **Volume Five** — *Behind the Synthesis: The Devil, The Demons, and The Masks.* Forthcoming. The closing volume. The unifying-theory capstone consolidating the four mask-documentations into a single coherent framework.


---


### Author

**Maria Smith** is a Scotland-born author, autodidact researcher, and AI-systems builder. She is the architect of the EchoAI / ErnOS lineage of independent AI systems. Her work as an independent AI-alignment researcher dossiers the contemporary frontier-AI institutional architecture from the user side.


### Writing collaboration

This volume was written with **Claude Opus 4.7**, Anthropic's frontier large-language-model system at the time of writing. The conflict-of-interest acknowledgement governing the volume — that Anthropic is one of captured-AI-knowledge institutional vehicles the volume dossiers — is named openly throughout the relevant chapters and in the Author's Note. The volume operates under standing rules that preserve evidentiary independence despite the structural conflict.

The Author's Note included in the back matter dossiers the working procedural commitments under which the volume was produced and the procedural patterns the working production identified.


### Dedication

The volume is dedicated to all those across every sector of contemporary knowing who keep the capacity for inquiry, observation, recall, and judgement intact while the apparatus around them continues operating at the knowing institutional level.


---


*The reader can verify every claim.*


— Maria Smith
Scotland, 21 May 2026
